template: field_fences.py (closest grant template: PendingGrantedBuildFences) — but insufficient; no raze primitive exists
ordering_note: The raze must (a) return all razed fences to fences_in_supply AND (b) preserve animals on pastures that no longer have capacity during the fence-less intermediate state, then (c) the rebuild grant must allow re-forming the ENTIRE layout from scratch (up to old_count + 3 fences), NOT an incremental pasture on top of the existing farmyard the way PendingGrantedBuildFences does. The 'do not lose animals' clause is the sharpest subtlety: between raze and rebuild the farmyard has zero pasture capacity, so the standard accommodation/overflow machinery would strand or kill the animals — there is no path that holds animals across a no-enclosure interval.
errata: Cost 1 Wood; prereq 2 Occupations (a prereq, not a cost). Clarification: 'For purposes of combos, you are building fences' — so on-play free-fence cards (Hedge Keeper +3 seed, Briar Hedge / Field Fences positional, Ash Trees pool) and build_fences triggers/cost-mods must apply to the rebuild. 'add up to 3 fences from your supply' caps the new total at old_fences_built + 3 (and is supply-limited).
open_question: Need (1) a new raze/remove-all-fences engine primitive that clears Farmyard.horizontal_fences/vertical_fences, returns the pieces to fences_in_supply, and recomputes pastures; (2) a mechanism to preserve animals through the fence-less intermediate state (no current path holds animals when capacity drops to 0); (3) a 'rebuild whole layout' grant variant (PendingGrantedBuildFences builds incrementally on the current farmyard, not a from-scratch relayout). Confirm with user whether to build the raze primitive + animal-hold, or keep deferred. Geometry-heavy + new shared infra = cardinal-rule DEFER.

template: agricola/cards/capacity_mods.py + animal_tamer.py (closest registry-pattern sibling; raises a passive capacity used by a legality/accommodation gate — but the existing registry is animal-pet capacity, NOT people capacity)
ordering_note: The family-growth room-cap gate lives ONLY in the placement predicates _legal_basic_wish_for_children (legality.py:762 = `people_total < 5 and people_total < _num_rooms(p)`) and _legal_urgent_wish_for_children (line 769 = `people_total < 5`); the family_growth ChooseSubAction/CommitFamilyGrowth enumerators do NOT re-check the room cap (they trust the placement was legal). So the override must thread into BOTH wish predicates, not the subaction. Bunk Beds replaces the `people_total < _num_rooms(p)` second clause with `people_total < 5` (a people-capacity floor of 5) once num_rooms>=4 — i.e. it raises the effective room-derived growth cap to 5 when you have exactly/at-least 4 rooms, while the hard `< 5` ceiling already present is unchanged. Subtlety: it does NOT grant a free grow above 5, and basic still needs an EMPTY room normally (people<num_rooms) — Bunk Beds is the sole exception making people==num_rooms==4 still growable to 5.
errata: No errata or clarifications printed. Cost 1 Wood; prereq 2 Major Improvements (PREREQ, gated via prereq fn counting state.board.major_improvement_owners for idx); no printed VPs; kept (not passing).
open_question: Need a new shared registry (e.g. register_people_capacity(card_id, fn) returning the effective growth cap, analogous to capacity_mods.register_house_capacity but for PEOPLE not animals) folded into _legal_basic_wish_for_children / _legal_urgent_wish_for_children, OR hardcode card-ownership checks in those two predicates. The existing capacity_mods registry is animal-pet capacity only and does NOT cover this gate. Confirm the desired registry shape (fold = max effective cap across owned cards?) and whether other deck-C/D/E cards (e.g. additional room/people cards) should share it before building. Also confirm '4 rooms' means >=4 (later renovations/expansions keep the bonus) vs exactly 4 — standard rule reads as a standing 'house can hold 5' capability active whenever rooms>=4, so >=4.

template: tutor.py (CardStore play-time snapshot via on_play + register_scoring) combined with childless.py's _num_rooms grid-count idiom
plan: register_occupation("butler", _on_play): _on_play snapshots the play round into CardStore as a gate, e.g. p.card_state.set("butler", 1 if state.round_number <= 11 else 0). register_scoring("butler", _score): _score returns 4 if the stored gate flag is truthy AND _num_rooms(p) > p.people_total else 0, where _num_rooms counts CellType.ROOM cells in p.farmyard.grid (copy childless._num_rooms). Played via Lessons; printed vps=0 (the 4 points are conditional, so NOT a flat vps= on the spec).
ordering_note: The round-11-or-before gate is known ONLY at play time, so it MUST be captured in _on_play (which sees state.round_number = the play round) and read back at scoring; do NOT try to infer it during _score (round_number is 14/terminal there). 'more rooms than people' is STRICT >, not >=, and 'people' = people_total (not people_home). The 4 points are all-or-nothing, not per-room. Use card_state.get default that means 'not gated in' (default 0) so a never-snapshotted state scores 0 rather than awarding the bonus.
errata: none

template: beer_keg.py (banked-bonus-points-via-conversion: CardStore + register_scoring) + stable_architect.py (count_unfenced_stables helper)
ordering_note: The blocker is the TIMING/AVAILABILITY seam, not the effect. 'Once per round, ... you can exchange 2 grain for 1 bonus point and N food' is an at-any-time standalone conversion available anywhere in the round, not pinned to one moment. The engine has no at-any-time conversion hook: the only standalone-timing seams are start_of_round (plow_driver/groom — granted sub-actions offered ONLY at round start) and the HARVEST_FEED HarvestConversionSpec (beer_keg — surfaced ONLY during FEED). Forcing Stall Holder onto start_of_round would silently narrow 'once per round' to 'once at round start', so the player could not use it after gaining grain mid-round — an unverified rules narrowing a coding agent must not lock in. The N-food output scaling on unfenced stables (0->1..4->5) and the bonus-point banking (card_state + register_scoring, mirroring beer_keg) are both fully supported; ONLY the trigger surface is missing.
errata: None reported by card_text.py (status todo, no errata/clarifications printed).
open_question: Is a card-game 'once per round, exchange 2 grain -> 1 VP + N food' usable at any point in the round, or should it be modeled as a once-per-round optional FireTrigger offered only at start_of_round? If start_of_round is acceptable, this becomes Tier 2 (start_of_round optional FireTrigger + count_unfenced_stables for the food amount + 2-grain debit + card_state bonus-point bank + register_scoring). Needs the user's ruling on availability timing; also confirm whether the 'bonus point' is an immediate banked VP (beer_keg pattern) vs an end-game term.

template: agricola/cards/carpenters_axe.py (optional after_action_space trigger on the wood accumulation space, once-per-use via triggers_resolved, atomic-space host via register_action_space_hook). Borrow the accumulated-pile read/write idiom from agricola/cards/wood_harvester.py / corf.py (get_space/with_space on ActionSpaceState.accumulated).
plan: WOOD_SPACES = frozenset({'forest'}) (2-player board: Forest is the only wood accumulation space; Copse/Grove are 3-4p only). register_occupation('tree_guard', _on_play=no-op). register('after_action_space', 'tree_guard', _eligible, _apply); register_action_space_hook('tree_guard', WOOD_SPACES). _eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved): 'tree_guard' not in triggers_resolved AND s.pending_stack[-1].space_id in WOOD_SPACES AND s.players[i].resources.wood >= 4 (HAVE-check on POST-pickup supply, since after_action_space fires after Forest's +3 pickup). _apply(s,i): p=fast_replace(s.players[i], resources=p.resources - Resources(wood=4) + Resources(stone=2, clay=1, reed=1, grain=1)); then board=with_space(s.board, sid, get_space(...).accumulated += Resources(wood=4)) to PLACE the 4 wood back onto Forest; return fast_replace(s, players=..., board=...). No cost/prereq/vps/passing.
ordering_note: TWO subtleties. (1) Direction of the 4 wood: it is PAID from supply but PLACED ONTO that accumulation space (it joins Forest's accumulated pile for a later player to pick up) — NOT discarded to general supply. The apply MUST credit get_space(board,'forest').accumulated.wood += 4 via with_space, in addition to debiting the player -4 wood. Forgetting the deposit silently changes the rules. (2) after_action_space fires AFTER Forest's atomic pickup empties the space and credits the player's +3 wood, so the >=4-wood eligibility HAVE-check reads the POST-pickup supply (a player at 1 wood who picks up 3 now has 4 and qualifies); and the deposited 4 wood lands on a space that was just reset to empty.
errata: None. Verbatim text: 'Each time after you use a wood accumulation space, you can place 4 wood from your supply on that space to get 2 stone, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 grain.' No errata/clarifications in the card data. Category Goods Provider, Corbarius Expansion, deck C #102, players 1+.
open_question: Confirm the 'place 4 wood on that space' deposit semantics: the rule literally returns the 4 wood to the Forest accumulation space (pickable next round/turn), rather than removing it from the game. This is the implementation's only real interpretive call; all existing wood-accumulation cards only READ accumulated, none WRITE a deposit, so verify with_space(accumulated += wood) is the intended/desired model (vs. just spending the 4 wood to general supply if a simpler reading is preferred).

template: scholar.py / seasonal_worker.py (start_of_round optional FireTrigger -> PendingCardChoice), but blocked
ordering_note: The optionality + forced-pick-one is buildable: a Groom-style OPTIONAL start_of_round FireTrigger (register('start_of_round', ...) NOT mandatory + register_start_of_round_hook) whose _apply pushes a PendingCardChoice with the (eligible-subset of) 6 exchange options, with the host's Proceed = decline. The hard blocker is the resolver: the card_choice resolver (see seasonal_worker._resolve) adds goods directly via p.resources+Resources(...) with NO accommodation pass — but 2 of the 6 exchanges (1 Veg->1 Cattle, 1 Veg->2 Sheep) GAIN animals, and an immediate conversion-driven animal grant has no accommodation/overflow handling (only markets/breeding/harvest/scheduled-round-start route through can_accommodate). Overflow at round start would also need a decision the choice frame can't host. Secondary subtlety: eligibility must filter each exchange to those whose INPUT the owner currently has (else a dead-end option), and 'exactly one' is enforced by the single CommitCardChoice; the optional decline must live at the parent host (Proceed), never a per-option flag.
errata: No errata or clarifications in card_text output. Verbatim: 'At the start of each round, you can make exactly one of the following exchanges: 1 Cattle -> 1 Vegetable; 1 Vegetable -> 1 Cattle; 2 Sheep -> 1 Vegetable; 1 Vegetable -> 2 Sheep; 2 Food -> 1 Grain; 1 Grain -> 2 Food'. Corbarius Expansion, deck C #103, category Goods Provider, players 1+, no cost/prereq/vps/passing fields populated.
open_question: Confirm with the user: should conversion-gained animals at start of round route through the can_accommodate/pareto_frontier machinery (and how is overflow resolved decision-free at preparation, given the player is also choosing which exchange)? If a generic 'start-of-round optional choose-one-conversion that may add animals' mechanism is built, Green Grocer + similar exchange occupations become Tier 2. Until then it shares the deferred animal-grant blocker with shepherds_crook / acorns-style immediate animal effects.

template: none (no precedent for a card-owned placeable action space)
ordering_note: Even if a card-as-action-space frame existed, the 'N DIFFERENT goods of your choice' grant (6/7/8/9 distinct good TYPES, owner-chosen) is a brand-new multi-select primitive with a distinctness constraint, and the count is keyed on the per-owner use index (1st/2nd/3rd/4th) which must be tracked in CardStore and incremented exactly once per placement on that space.
errata: No errata. Clarifications: none returned. Card is from the Consul Dirigens Expansion, deck C #104, category Goods Provider, players 1+. VPS 0, no printed cost/prereq, not passing.
open_question: Does the project intend to support cards that ADD a new player-private action space (placeable by the owner only, with its own worker-placement legality, occupancy, and per-use scaling)? This needs a new board/legality concept plus a 'choose N distinct goods' selection primitive and a begging-marker grant outside the harvest/scoring path. If yes, this likely warrants its own design pass alongside any other 'card is an action space for you' cards (e.g. Wood Collector schedules round-spaces but Collector is a true new space).

template: harvest_conversions.py (closest mechanism, but structurally inverted) / no fitting template
ordering_note: If forced, the 'once each harvest' window aligns with the PendingHarvestFeed seam, but the direction is reversed: this SPENDS food to BUY goods, whereas every existing harvest conversion SPENDS goods to PRODUCE food (input_cost -> food_out). Surfacing a food-spending purchase inside the feed frontier is semantically backwards because the goods produced (wood/reed/grain) never help feeding, and CommitHarvestConversion adds food_out food to pay feeding — the opposite need.
errata: None. Verbatim text: 'Once each harvest, you can buy 1 wood, 1 reed, and 1 grain for 2 food total.' Corbarius Expansion, deck C #105, category Goods Provider, players 1+, no cost/prereq/vps/passing.
open_question: This is a buy-food->good (standalone food->goods purchase), which the cheat-sheet lists as an explicit DEFER condition. The HarvestConversionSpec model (input_cost: Resources spent, food_out: int produced, surfaced in PendingHarvestFeed to pay feeding) is structurally goods->food and cannot represent a 2-food -> 1wood+1reed+1grain purchase; side_effect_fn could bolt on the goods but input_cost/food_out/food-frontier framing is wrong and the goods don't feed. Needs new shared infra: a once-per-harvest food->goods purchase frame/decision point (or a generalized 'harvest purchase' registry whose output is arbitrary Resources, not just food, with its own enumerator + per-harvest used-set). DEFER and ask the user how they want food->goods harvest purchases modeled (extend HarvestConversionSpec to arbitrary-output, or a separate purchase mechanism).

template: agricola/cards/scythe_worker.py (occupation on-play food + register_auto('harvest_field') + register_harvest_field_hook). Potato Harvester is strictly simpler than scythe_worker because its harvest clause only COUNTS the mechanical take rather than modifying fields.
plan: register_occupation(CARD_ID, _on_play) where _on_play credits +3 food to player idx. _eligible(state, idx) -> any FIELD cell with veg>0 (those are the veg taken this harvest). _apply(state, idx): count FIELD cells with veg>0 (each yields exactly 1 veg in the mechanical take, since a field is sown grain XOR veg and grain takes precedence), credit that many food (no field mutation). register_auto('harvest_field', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply) + register_harvest_field_hook(CARD_ID). No cost/prereq/vps/passing on the occupation (just on-play + scoring-neutral harvest reward).
ordering_note: The harvest_field auto fires BEFORE the mechanical crop take (_fire_harvest_field_hook runs first inside _resolve_harvest_field), so fields are still sown when _apply runs — count veg fields THERE, not after. Unlike scythe_worker, _apply must NOT decrement the fields: it only awards food for the veg the normal take will harvest. Count is per veg-field (each veg-field gives exactly 1 veg per harvest because grain takes precedence and a field holds grain XOR veg), so 'for each vegetable you get from your fields' = count of FIELD cells with veg>0.
errata: None reported by card_text.py (no errata/clarifications printed). Card is Consul Dirigens expansion, deck C #106, Food Provider, players 1+.
open_question: Confirm '1 additional food per vegetable' counts only the SINGLE veg the mechanical take removes from each veg-field this harvest (i.e. fields are not double-harvested) — the engine takes exactly 1 crop per field per harvest, so the food bonus equals the number of veg-sown fields. This matches the rules reading; no ambiguity expected.

template: oven_firing_boy.py (granted optional Bake Bread via push(PendingBakeBread)); harvest_field hook in engine.py/triggers.py for the phase-seam shape
ordering_note: The 'start of each feeding phase' grant must surface as an OPTIONAL, declinable agent decision (PendingBakeBread the player resolves or declines), NOT an auto-effect. The existing harvest_field hook (_fire_harvest_field_hook) is AUTO-ONLY: it pushes/pops a transient PendingHarvestField within one call and never returns a state with a surfaced decision, so it structurally cannot host an interactive bake. A new feeding-phase seam would also need to fire at the HARVEST_FIELD->HARVEST_FEED boundary BEFORE _initiate_harvest_feed pushes the PendingHarvestFeed frames (the bake-then-feed ordering matters: baking grain->food at start of feeding lets that food count toward feeding need). Also the on-play half: card-played-via-Bake-Bread-space self-interaction (cf. Sugar Baker clarification) must not let the card bake on the same action that played it.
errata: No errata. Sibling Sugar Baker carries a clarification (a card played by using a space may not immediately use that card) that flags the play-via-space self-interaction concern for the on-play 'when you play this card' grant; not a direct erratum for Baker but relevant to the on-play timing.
open_question: Need a new shared engine seam: a 'start_of_feeding_phase' (HARVEST_FIELD->HARVEST_FEED boundary) hook that can host an INTERACTIVE/declinable granted sub-action (not just auto-effects like harvest_field). Does the user want to build this generic feeding-phase host (it would also enable other start-of-feeding cards), and how should the on-play Bake Bread grant be wired (push PendingBakeBread inside register_occupation on_play, declinable) including the play-via-Bake-Bread-space self-use guard?

template: (none — no harvest-skip mechanism exists)
ordering_note: The hard part is consuming the flag at the correct (the NEXT) harvest only and skipping FIELD/FEED/BREED for the flagged player ALONE while the opponent harvests normally — the harvest pushes one frame per player and every resolver (_resolve_harvest_field crop take, _initiate_harvest_feed food pre-debit, _initiate_harvest_breed) assumes both players participate; the flag must also be cleared after that one harvest so later harvests run normally.
errata: Text (Consul Dirigens, deck C #108, Food Provider): 'When you play this card, you must skip the next harvest. (You also do not have to feed your family that harvest.)' No cost, no prereq, no vps, not passing. No errata/clarifications returned.
open_question: Implementing this needs new shared infrastructure that does not exist: (1) a per-player state field on PlayerState (e.g. skip_next_harvest: bool) to flag the player, set on-play; (2) a fundamental change to the harvest phase progression so a flagged player is excluded from FIELD crop-take, FEED feeding, and BREED breeding while the opponent still harvests fully (the harvest is currently triggered globally by `state.round_number in HARVEST_ROUNDS` in engine.py and the FIELD/FEED/BREED resolvers process both players unconditionally). No register_* event/hook can suppress an entire harvest for one player; the existing `harvest_field` hook fires only WITHIN a harvest before crop take and cannot skip feeding/breeding or prevent harvest entry. (Sibling unimplemented minor at revised_minor_improvements.json:5596 — 'skip the field and breeding phase... get exactly 1 food' — needs the same missing machinery.) Defer and ask the user how to model the per-player harvest skip (new field + harvest-resolver branch) before implementing.

template: agricola/cards/furniture_carpenter.py (occupation no-op on-play + register_harvest_conversion); food-producing conversion shape from harvest_conversions.py built-ins (joinery/pottery/basketmaker).
plan:
register_occupation('schnapps_distiller', lambda state, idx: state)  # no on-play effect (played via Lessons).
register_harvest_conversion(HarvestConversionSpec(conversion_id='schnapps_distiller', input_cost=Resources(veg=1), food_out=5, is_owned_fn=_eligible)).
_eligible(state, idx): return 'schnapps_distiller' in state.players[idx].occupations  # registrations are global; must gate ownership here so the non-owner is not offered the conversion.
No cost, no prereq, no vps, not passing. No side_effect_fn, no scoring term, no CardStore.
ordering_note: is_owned_fn MUST confirm THIS player owns the occupation (conversion registry is global; the HARVEST_FEED enumerator gates only on is_owned_fn) — otherwise the conversion is offered to the non-owner. The once-per-harvest 'exactly 1 vegetable' is enforced automatically: firing marks the id in harvest_conversions_used (reset each harvest's FEED), so no manual cross-variant guard is needed (single entry, unlike Beer Keg).
errata: None. Card text is unambiguous: 'In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 vegetable into 5 food.' Optional ('you can'), once-per-harvest (single conversion entry), no points.

template: capacity_mods.py (PASTURE_CAPACITY_MODS / HOUSE_CAPACITY_MODS) — closest but INSUFFICIENT; see blocker
ordering_note: The capacity is TYPED and HETEROGENEOUS: exactly 1 sheep AND 1 boar AND 1 cattle (one of each), not 3 interchangeable slots. The existing can_accommodate model (helpers.py) has only two slot kinds: a typed-PASTURE slot (one type, capacity N) and a FLEXIBLE slot (one animal of ANY type). Modeling this card as 3 flexible slots (HOUSE_CAPACITY_MODS) OVER-GRANTS — 3 flexible slots could hold 3 sheep. Modeling it as a per-pasture bonus (PASTURE_CAPACITY_MODS) is wrong — it needs a real pasture and the clarification states it does NOT count as a pasture. Correct modeling requires a NEW third slot kind: a per-type single-capacity slot (one sheep + one boar + one cattle). That is an engine change to extract_slots + can_accommodate (and the frontier helpers/caches that key on their output), not a registry row.
errata: No errata. Clarification: 'This card does not count as a pasture.' (so it cannot satisfy pasture-count scoring or be subdivided/fenced). cost 2 Wood; prereq 2 Occupations (a PREREQ, not a cost); vps 1; not passing.
open_question: Add a third slot kind to extract_slots/can_accommodate: a list of per-TYPE single-capacity slots (each restricted to one named animal type), distinct from flexible (any-type) slots and pasture (one-type, capacity-N) slots. This needs a new capacity_mods registry (e.g. TYPED_HOLDER_MODS returning a (sheep_cap, boar_cap, cattle_cap) triple), threading into can_accommodate's overflow check, and re-keying the frontier/accommodation caches. Confirm the intended grouping of all card-as-animal-holder cards (Wildlife Reserve and any siblings) so the infra is built once.

template: agricola/cards/beer_keg.py (multi-variant once-per-harvest HarvestConversionSpec + CardStore-banked VP + register_scoring); occupation registration + no-op on_play per agricola/cards/stable_architect.py
plan: register_occupation('home_brewer', lambda s,i: s)  # effect is the recurring harvest conversion only. Two HARVEST_CONVERSIONS variants, both input_cost=Resources(grain=1): 'home_brewer_food' food_out=3 side_effect_fn=None ; 'home_brewer_vp' food_out=0 side_effect_fn banks +1 in CardStore (p.card_state.set('home_brewer', banked+1)). Both is_owned_fn: return ('home_brewer' in p.occupations) and not any(cid.startswith('home_brewer') for cid in p.harvest_conversions_used)  # once-per-harvest choice across the two variants. register_scoring('home_brewer', lambda s,i: s.players[i].card_state.get('home_brewer', 0)). No cost/prereq/passing/vps (occupation).
ordering_note: Cross-variant once-per-harvest guard: BOTH variants' is_owned_fn must return False when ANY 'home_brewer_*' id is already in harvest_conversions_used (use cid.startswith('home_brewer'), like beer_keg), not just its own id — else a player fires both the food AND the VP variant in one harvest, breaking 'turn exactly 1 grain' (one grain, one output, once per harvest). Affordability (1 grain) and the per-id used-guard are handled by the FEED enumerator; the cross-variant suppression is the card's job.
errata: None. Verbatim: 'After the field phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 grain into your choice of 3 food or 1 bonus point.' Surfaced during HARVEST_FEED (the engine's harvest-conversion seam runs after FIELD). Occupation, Consul Dirigens, deck C #110, Food Provider, players 1+, vps 0, no cost/prereq, not passing.
open_question: Immediate-VP is unsupported, so the '1 bonus point' output is banked in CardStore and read back by a scoring term (same as beer_keg / furniture_carpenter) — confirm this banking is acceptable vs any preference for a different VP representation.

template: agricola/cards/scullery.py (start_of_round register_auto + register_start_of_round_hook; condition re-checked each round)
plan:
register_occupation(CARD_ID, on_play=lambda s,i: s)  # no on-play effect.
_eligible(state, idx) -> state.players[idx].resources.food >= state.round_number  # round_number is already the round being entered when start_of_round autos fire.
_apply(state, idx): p=fast_replace(state.players[idx], resources=p.resources+Resources(food=1)); return fast_replace(state, players=tuple-swap).
register_auto('start_of_round', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply); register_start_of_round_hook(CARD_ID).  # no cost/prereq/vps/passing (occupation).
ordering_note: The 'current round number' is the round being ENTERED, not the one just finished. Verified in engine.py: _complete_preparation increments round_number to new_round (line 981/1031) BEFORE _push_preparation_hosts fires the start_of_round autos, so at firing time state.round_number == the round about to be played. Compare food >= state.round_number directly (no +1). Mandatory choice-free (use register_auto, not register), and the condition is re-checked every round so income switches on/off with the player's food level. Income is granted AFTER that round's future_resources are already added (step 2 of _complete_preparation), so 'have food >= round' is evaluated on the post-distribution food total, which matches the card (you check your food at the start of the round).
errata: No errata. Clarification baked into card text: 'e.g., 8+ food in round 8'.

template: agricola/cards/threshing_board.py (same before_action_space + optional register shape over {farmland, cultivation} + grain_utilization) for structure; agricola/cards/potter_ceramics.py for the goods-swap apply (-1 food, +1 grain); agricola/cards/assistant_tiller.py / barrow_pusher.py for the trigger-only occupation no-op on_play idiom.
plan: CARD_ID='thresher'; SPACES=frozenset({'grain_utilization','farmland','cultivation'}). _eligible(state,idx,triggers_resolved)-> CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved AND state.pending_stack[-1].space_id in SPACES AND state.players[idx].resources.food >= 1. _apply(state,idx)-> p=state.players[idx]; fast_replace(state, players=tuple of p with resources+Resources(food=-1, grain=1)). register_occupation(CARD_ID, _noop_on_play) (from specs; no on-play effect). register('before_action_space', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply) -> OPTIONAL declinable FireTrigger. No cost/prereq/vps/passing on the occupation; no register_action_space_hook (all three spaces are non-atomic and already hosted).
ordering_note: Must be the OPTIONAL declinable form (register, FireTrigger) not register_auto, because 'you can buy' is optional. Eligibility MUST gate on food>=1, else it offers a dead-end FireTrigger the player cannot pay. Use 'before_action_space' (per the 'each time you use [space]' -> before ruling and the card's own clarification 'this effect happens before using the space'); triggers_resolved scoping makes it re-eligible each space-use and limits it to once per use. The clarification 'must happen before Flail C026' is just the before-phase ruling restated; no special inter-card ordering code is needed now (registry handles fire order once Flail exists).
errata: Clarification (from card_text): 'This effect happens before using the space, and must happen before effects such as Flail C026.' Confirms the before_action_space placement and that the grain bought here is available to the subsequent sow/space effect.

template: agricola/cards/furniture_carpenter.py (recurring harvest food-buy via HarvestConversionSpec.side_effect_fn) + on-play grain grant like consultant.py; veg-grant idiom from market_stall.py
plan: register_occupation('winter_caretaker', _on_play) where _on_play returns state with p.resources + Resources(grain=1) (immediate +1 grain on play). register_harvest_conversion(HarvestConversionSpec(conversion_id='winter_caretaker', input_cost=Resources(food=2), food_out=0, is_owned_fn=_eligible, side_effect_fn=_grant_veg)). _eligible(state,idx) = 'winter_caretaker' in state.players[idx].occupations (registrations are global -> must gate on ownership here; no other prereq). _grant_veg(state,idx) edits player via fast_replace(p, resources=p.resources + Resources(veg=1)). No scoring term (the vegetable is a normal good). Add 'from agricola.cards import winter_caretaker' to cards/__init__.py. No cost/prereq/vps/passing.
ordering_note: Card says 'At the END of each harvest' but the harvest-conversion registry is surfaced during the FEED sub-phase (FIELD->FEED->BREED), i.e. mid-harvest not literally end-of-harvest. This is mechanically harmless here: nothing observable happens between FEED/BREED and harvest-end that interacts with holding +1 vegetable, and the conversion is once-per-harvest (harvest_conversions_used) which correctly enforces 'buy EXACTLY 1'. The food->good direction (food_out=0 + side_effect_fn grants the good, never negative food) is the same shape Furniture Carpenter uses; the enumerator already gates on _can_afford so the buy is only offered when the player holds 2 food.
errata: none
open_question: Minor: 'end of harvest' vs the FEED-phase surfacing point of the harvest-conversion registry (no observable difference here, so treated as the intended hook). If the user wants strict end-of-harvest (after BREED) timing, that would need a new harvest-end hook — not built; not warranted for this card.

template: agricola/cards/carpenters_axe.py (optional after_action_space trigger on an atomic accumulation space, gated once-per-use via triggers_resolved + affordability) combined with mineralogist.py / clay_puncher.py (the clay_pit/western_quarry/eastern_quarry hooking + per-space branch). Soil Scientist's granted effect is a pure goods swap in _apply (no new pending frame), simpler than carpenters_axe's PendingBuildStables.
plan: register_occupation('soil_scientist', lambda s,i: s)  # no on-play effect. register('after_action_space', 'soil_scientist', _eligible, _apply) and register_action_space_hook('soil_scientist', {'clay_pit','western_quarry','eastern_quarry'}) (all three atomic, must be hosted). _eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved): False if CARD_ID in triggers_resolved (once per use); sid=s.pending_stack[-1].space_id; if sid=='clay_pit' return p.resources.stone>=1; if sid in {'western_quarry','eastern_quarry'} return p.resources.clay>=2; else False. _apply(s,i): if sid=='clay_pit' delta=Resources(stone=-1,grain=2) else delta=Resources(clay=-2,veg=1); p=fast_replace(player, resources=p.resources+delta); return fast_replace(s, players=...). No cost, no prereq, no vps, not passing (occupation, played via Lessons).
ordering_note: Two ASYMMETRIC branches that are easy to cross-wire: clay accumulation space (clay_pit) -> pay 1 STONE, gain 2 grain; stone accumulation spaces (western/eastern quarry) -> pay 2 CLAY, gain 1 vegetable. The cost good is the OPPOSITE mineral of the space used. Both quarries share the identical stone-branch effect. Must be after_action_space (the space's own pickup resolves first; 'each time after you use' is the explicit immediately-after exception), and gated once-per-use by triggers_resolved (not used_this_round) so it can fire on each separate quarry/pit use. 'place X on the space' is flavor only -- goods leave supply, no per-card goods stack needed.
errata: None. card_text.py reports no errata/clarifications.

template: (none — blocked; closest partial shapes: wood_workshop.py for after_build_major auto, ash_trees.py for a persistent on-card CardStore pool, geologist/oven_firing_boy for an optional FireTrigger that grants a Sow)
ordering_note: Even if built, the on-play accumulation is an after_build_major auto that must place 1 reed on a PER-CARD goods slot (not the player's supply) once per build; the redemption is a separate 'at any time' choice (move-to-supply OR exchange-for-Sow), which has no firing seam — it is neither tied to a play-card/renovate latch nor to any action space, and granting a free Sow at an arbitrary moment needs a turn-anchored optional window the engine lacks.
errata: No errata or clarifications returned by card_text.py. Verbatim: 'Each time you build a major improvement, place 1 reed from the general supply on that card. At any time, you can move the reed to your supply or exchange it for a "Sow" action.' (Occupation, Corbarius Expansion, deck C #115, Crop Provider, players 1+.)
open_question: Two independent blockers: (1) 'at any time' standalone conversion/effect has no engine anchor (confirmed by agricola/cards/__init__.py:40-43 — Firewood Collector is deferred for exactly this missing post-action 'at any time' window). (2) A reed sitting 'on that card' and accumulating across multiple major builds is a per-card goods stack (DEFER-list item), distinct from CardStore's typical scalar latch/counter usage and from the player's supply. When 'at any time' windows + per-card goods stacks land, Sower becomes: after_build_major auto -> increment a CardStore reed count; plus an at-any-time optional FireTrigger offering move-to-supply (reed -> +1 reed resources, decrement store) or exchange-for-Sow (decrement store, push PendingSow).

template: paper_maker.py (before_play_occupation precedent) / bread_paddle.py (after_play_occupation precedent) — neither fully fits; on-play half is consultant.py shape
ordering_note: Effect 2 fires AFTER the occupation is played, and the PendingPlayOccupation frame is pivoted to phase=after with the card ALREADY in the tableau (added at CommitPlayOccupation, before _enter_after_phase). So an after_play_occupation auto self-fires on Furniture Maker's OWN play, violating 'after this one'. Contrast Paper Maker, which is a before_play_occupation trigger that never self-fires precisely because the card is not yet in the tableau in the before-phase. With after_play_occupation there is NO clean self-exclusion: _owns is already true, and the frame carries no record of which card was just played, so the auto cannot tell its own play from a later one. When Furniture Maker is played as a non-first occupation it pays 1 food (Lessons ramp: first free, later 1 food each), so the erroneous self-payout is +1 wood — a real, unavoidable bug without new infra.
errata: No errata or clarifications printed. Verbatim: 'When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood. Each time you play an occupation after this one, you get 1 wood for each food paid as occupation cost.' 0 VP, no printed cost/prereq, no passing. Corbarius Expansion, deck C #116, players 1+.
open_question: Effect 2 needs two things the after_play_occupation seam does not currently expose: (a) the food paid as THIS occupation's cost — available as state.pending_stack[-1].cost.food on the still-top PendingPlayOccupation frame (this part IS reachable); and (b) the identity of the just-played occupation so the auto can EXCLUDE Furniture Maker's own play ('after this one'). The frame has no played_card_id field, and at after_play_occupation the card is already in the tableau so _owns gives no self-exclusion. Proposed fix (needs user approval, new shared infra): add a played_card_id field to PendingPlayOccupation set at CommitPlayOccupation, then gate Furniture Maker's after_play_occupation auto on played_card_id != 'furniture_maker'. apply: +1 wood per state.pending_stack[-1].cost.food. on_play: +1 wood (trivial, consultant.py shape). Once the played_card_id field exists this becomes a ~15-line Tier-2 auto. Whole card deferred rather than shipping the half-correct unguarded version.

template: none (no template fits — requires new shared infra)
ordering_note: Firing kind would be before_action_space (each-time-you-use = before, per ruling), AUTO/mandatory since it's a pure +1 wood reward with no downside. But the eligibility predicate is unimplementable: it asks whether the just-used action space is orthogonally adjacent ON THE PHYSICAL ACTION BOARD to another space occupied by one of the player's workers.
errata: none reported by card_text.py
open_question: The engine has NO model of action-board physical layout / inter-space orthogonal adjacency. SPACE_IDS (constants.py) is a flat 1D canonical ordering driven by reveal order, not a 2D board; ActionSpaceState carries workers + accumulated + revealed but no coordinates, and all 'adjacency' code in the codebase (fences.py, legality.py, pasture.py) is about the 3x5 FARMYARD grid or fence edges, never action spaces. Implementing Legworker needs a new shared artifact: a static 2D action-board layout mapping each space_id (incl. per-stage round-card slots) to a board position + an orthogonal-adjacency relation, plus a before_action_space auto that checks 'any orthogonally-adjacent space has workers[idx] != 0' and grants +1 wood. This is new geometry infra, so DEFER and ASK the user whether/how to model the action board's physical layout (the round-card spaces occupy fixed physical slots independent of the engine's reveal-order SPACE_IDS).

template: agricola/cards/estate_worker.py (and lumberjack.py deferred half) — Category 8 deferred-goods-on-round-spaces via schedule_resources
plan: register_occupation('wood_collector', _on_play). _on_play(state, idx): R = state.round_number; return schedule_resources(state, idx, range(R+1, R+6), Resources(wood=1)). No cost/prereq/vps/passing (all defaults). schedule_resources clamps rounds >14 (silently drops), matching 'each of the next 5 round spaces' near game end. Verified schedule_resources exists in agricola/cards/schedules.py (1-indexed rounds, additive, slot r-1, drops out-of-range).
ordering_note: The 'next 5 round spaces' are rounds R+1..R+5 (R = current round_number at play time), so use range(R+1, R+6) — NOT range(R, R+5) and NOT round_number-based off-by-one. schedule_resources uses 1-indexed rounds (slot r-1, the Well index convention) and silently drops any round >14, so a late play correctly places on only the remaining future round spaces. Collection is automatic at start-of-round via engine._complete_preparation reading future_resources — no start_of_round hook to register (resources, unlike effect-cards, need no trigger).
errata: none — no clarifications/errata on this card. (Note: 'Wood Collector' is distinct from 'Firewood Collector' [A119, end-of-turn +1 wood on certain spaces] and 'Brushwood Collector' [B145]; do not conflate.)

template: consultant.py (on-play goods) + maintenance_premium.py (after_renovate register_auto granting goods)
plan: register_occupation(CARD_ID, _on_play) where _on_play adds Resources(wood=1, clay=1) to the player (Consultant pattern). register_auto('after_renovate', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply): _eligible returns True (owner gate is applied by apply_auto_effects, no prereq/cost on this card). _apply computes placed = p.people_total - p.newborns - p.people_home (people placed this round), then adds Resources(wood=placed) to the player. No cost, no prereq, no vps, not passing. Mandatory choiceless (text says 'you get', no downside) -> register_auto, not register.
ordering_note: The 'number of people you placed that round' = people_total - newborns - people_home, computed at after_renovate time. This is correct because: (1) a renovate is reached via a PlaceWorker (house/farm redevelopment) which decrements people_home BEFORE the renovate frame is pushed (resolution.py:124), so the worker that triggered this renovate is already counted -> matches the clarification '3rd placed person -> 3 wood'. (2) newborns must be subtracted (clarification: 'Newborns are not placed'); newborns are included in people_total and not cleared until next round's preparation, so they would otherwise inflate the count. (3) people_home is reset to people_total at end-of-round (_resolve_return_home, engine.py:858), so the per-round count is correct. after_renovate (not before) is the right hook (fires once post-application, per the Mining Hammer / Roughcaster / Maintenance Premium renovate convention).
errata: Clarifications: 'If you renovate with your 3rd placed person of a round, this card triggers a payout of 3 wood. Newborns are not placed.' Card text: 'When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood and 1 clay. Each time after you renovate, you get a number of wood equal to the number of people you placed that round.' Deck C #119, Consul Dirigens Expansion, category Building Resource Provider.

template: drinking_trough.py / capacity_mods.py (closest in spirit, but INSUFFICIENT — see blocker)
ordering_note: If ever implemented: the slots are CATTLE-ONLY and live ON the card (a distinct location), NOT a flat +capacity on existing pastures. So it canNOT use register_pasture_capacity (that bonus applies to EVERY pasture and holds any single type per pasture). It needs a new type-restricted, card-scoped flexible-slot kind threaded through extract_slots -> can_accommodate -> pareto_frontier/breeding_frontier/harvest_feed_frontier AND end-game scoring (scoring counts only player_state.animals.cattle). Capacity is dynamic (= current pasture count), recomputed from farmyard, and must reflect mid-game pasture changes.
errata: No errata/clarifications printed. Card text (verbatim): 'For each pasture you have, you can keep 1 cattle on this card.' Cost 1 Wood; no prereq; no VPs; not passing. Corbarius Expansion, deck C #12, category Farm Planner.
open_question: Are card-held animals (a) type-restricted to cattle, (b) counted as the player's cattle for end-game scoring (they are — cattle on this card still score), and (c) do they count toward / are they fed at harvest like normal cattle? Standard Agricola: yes to all — animals on such cards are real animals that must be fed and DO score; the card only adds storage space. The engine's animal model (flat player_state.animals + farmyard slots; scoring reads player_state.animals.cattle) has no notion of a separate cattle-only card location integrated into the accommodation Pareto machinery. Implementing it cleanly requires deciding whether to (i) model it as a new 'typed flexible slot' kind (cattle-only) in extract_slots and add an int param to can_accommodate's overflow logic, or (ii) something simpler. This is new shared accommodation infra and should be designed with the maintainer, not guessed. DEFER and ASK.

template: interim_storage.py / maintenance_premium.py (CardStore reservoir) for the 8-clay-on-card pool; but NO template for the grain-obtain trigger
ordering_note: If ever implemented: the +1 clay must fire PER grain UNIT obtained (e.g. a 3-grain harvest field-take yields 3 clay), and only WHILE the on-card clay pool is non-empty (8 clay seeded on play, decremented one-per-grain, capped at 0). The bonus clay is itself NOT a grain-obtain, so no recursion. The hard part is that 'obtain' is the act of grain entering the player's supply, which happens at many unrelated sites with no common chokepoint.
errata: No errata or clarifications in card_text output. Occupation, Corbarius Expansion, deck C #120, players 1+, category Building Resource Provider. No cost/prereq/vps/passing. Text: 'Place 8 clay on this card. For each grain you obtain, you also get 1 clay from this card.'
open_question: There is no engine-wide 'obtain grain' (grain-gain) event/hook. Grain enters a player's supply at many heterogeneous sites with no common chokepoint: harvest field-take (_resolve_harvest_field, engine.py ~line 1246-1270), the Grain Seeds / Grain Utilization spaces, Day Laborer card variants, sow leftover, and numerous card effects. The registered event taxonomy is only before_/after_<sub-action> + before_/after_action_space + start_of_round + harvest_field; none of these is 'grain obtained'. Implementing this card requires NEW shared infrastructure: a grain-gain event threaded at EVERY grain-credit site (or per-site edits), plus a granularity decision (per-unit vs per-event). This is exactly the new-shared-infra + per-grain-counting case the cardinal rule says to DEFER. The CardStore 8-clay pool itself is trivial (interim_storage / maintenance_premium prove it); the pool is NOT the blocker. Confirm with the user whether to add a generic grain-obtain hook (which several other cards likely also want) before implementing.

template: agricola/cards/clay_puncher.py (near-exact: on-play goods grant + after_action_space auto +1 clay on atomic-hosted spaces). corn_scoop.py confirms the grain_seeds/vegetable_seeds hook shape.
plan:
register_occupation(CARD_ID, _grant_on_play) where _grant_on_play does p.resources + Resources(wood=1, clay=2) (the immediate +1 wood +2 clay).
Separately _grant_clay(state, idx) = p.resources + Resources(clay=1).
_eligible(state, idx) -> state.pending_stack[-1].space_id in {'grain_seeds','vegetable_seeds'}.
register_auto('after_action_space', CARD_ID, _eligible, _grant_clay)  # mandatory choiceless +1 clay.
register_action_space_hook(CARD_ID, {'grain_seeds','vegetable_seeds'})  # both ATOMIC, must be hosted.
No cost/prereq/vps/passing (plain on-play occupation, players 1+).
ordering_note: Text reads 'Each time AFTER you use' -> the recurring grant MUST ride after_action_space (NOT before_action_space). corn_scoop uses before_action_space precisely because its text omits 'after'; clay_puncher uses after_action_space for the same 'after you use' wording. Wrong event = clay granted before the seed pickup instead of after; both spaces are atomic so they require register_action_space_hook to surface the after-phase (non-atomic auto-hosting does not apply). On-play wood+clay is a separate one-time register_occupation grant, independent of the hook.
errata: No errata/clarifications surfaced by card_text.py. (Unlike clay_puncher, which carries a '1+1=2 on Lessons' clarification — not applicable here since neither hooked space is a play-card space.)

template: agricola/cards/small_scale_farmer.py (start_of_round register_auto + register_start_of_round_hook, exactly-2-rooms gate); house-material check pattern from agricola/cards/priest.py
plan: register_occupation("freemason", lambda s,i: s)  # no on-play effect. _num_rooms(p)=count CellType.ROOM cells (3x5 grid), as in priest/small_scale_farmer. _eligible(state, idx): material = state.players[idx].house_material; return _num_rooms(p)==2 and material in (HouseMaterial.CLAY, HouseMaterial.STONE). _apply(state, idx): p=state.players[idx]; gain = Resources(clay=2) if p.house_material is HouseMaterial.CLAY else Resources(stone=2); p=fast_replace(p, resources=p.resources+gain); write player back. register_auto("start_of_round", "freemason", _eligible, _apply); register_start_of_round_hook("freemason"). No cost/prereq/vps/passing (plain occupation).
ordering_note: Grant is MANDATORY/choiceless ('you get', not 'you can') -> register_auto, NOT register/FireTrigger (cf. cob which uses register because it says 'you can'). The grant TYPE/AMOUNT is material-conditioned: clay house -> +2 clay, stone house -> +2 stone, wood house -> nothing -- the 'clay/stone' and '2 clay/stone' X/Y notation must be split by house_material inside _apply, with the wood case excluded by _eligible. Condition is re-checked every round in _eligible (income auto-stops on renovate to wood or room-count change); no used_this_round latch needed since the engine fires start_of_round once per owner per round.
errata: Clarification (not errata): 'Cards that provide room for a person do not count for this effect unless they self-identify as a room.' -> count only true CellType.ROOM cells, exactly as priest/small_scale_farmer/childless already do; no special handling needed since no implemented card grants a non-room living space.

template: none (no breeding-phase hook exists; closest conceptual analogs are the FEED-phase harvest_conversions.py and the harvest_field-hook cards like wood_harvester.py, but neither fires in the BREED phase)
ordering_note: The cost escalates by the ORDINAL of the harvest occurrence, not the round number: 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th/6th harvest cost 2/2/3/3/4/1 food respectively. Harvest rounds are {4,7,9,11,13,14}, so the 6th harvest (round 14) is unusually CHEAP (1 food). This requires a per-card counter (CardStore) tracking how many harvests have elapsed to index the cost — a stack-position/round-number mapping is the easy-to-get-wrong part. Also: the buy is OPTIONAL ('you can use this card'), at-will during breeding, exactly 2 stone per use, and presumably at most once per harvest.
errata: No errata or clarifications printed in card_text output. Card text verbatim: 'In the breeding phase of the 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th/6th harvest, you can use this card to buy exactly 2 stone for 2/2/3/3/4/1 food.' Category: Building Resource Provider; Corbarius Expansion; deck C #124; players 1+; vps 0; not passing.
open_question: Two blockers: (1) NO breeding-phase firing seam exists. _initiate_harvest_breed (engine.py:1172) pushes PendingHarvestBreed frames but fires NO card hooks — there is no 'harvest_breed' event, no register_harvest_breed_hook, and no register_action_space_hook coverage for the breed phase. A new optional/declinable trigger event + host frame in the BREED phase would have to be added (shared infra). (2) This is a food->stone purchase (buy-good-for-food conversion), an explicit DEFER category per the cheat-sheet, and the escalating per-harvest-ordinal cost needs a CardStore counter. Should the user add a breeding-phase hook event for this card class, or defer the whole Corbarius breeding-phase-importer family until that infra is designed?

template: agricola/cards/childless.py (closest start_of_round host, but mechanism does NOT cover this card)
ordering_note: Effect fires 'before the start of each work phase' and must place a TEMPORARY/extra person on a qualifying accumulation space, then take that space's accumulated goods, then 'proceed with the start player'. There is no engine concept of a temporary extra worker, no card grants an actual worker placement (all start_of_round effects only grant goods via schedule_resources), and the qualifying-space gate ('a building resource not in your supply') is a dynamic per-space, per-player accumulation-space predicate that interacts with turn order. The most error-prone subtlety is that this is NOT a goods grant disguised as a placement: the person must occupy a real accumulation space (consuming its goods and blocking it for the round), which requires new shared infrastructure for extra-worker placement + occupancy + goods-take, plus interaction with placement legality and turn order.
errata: Clarification: 'A resource you already have can be taken, but only if placed on a qualifying accumulation space by another effect, e.g. Outskirts Director C130.' (cross-card interaction). No errata.
open_question: Does the project want a new shared 'place a temporary/extra person on an accumulation space before the work phase' mechanism (extra-worker frame + accumulation-space occupancy + goods-take + start-player proceed)? This is required not just for Nightworker but the whole class of cards that grant bonus worker placements; needs a design decision before any of them can be implemented.

template: agricola/cards/clay_puncher.py (after_action_space auto-grant on an atomic space + register_action_space_hook) for the mandatory wood+clay grant; agricola/cards/basket.py (optional after_action_space FireTrigger that exchanges goods, eligibility gates affordability + triggers_resolved once-per-use) for the optional 'buy 1 stone for 1 food' conversion. Both kinds coexist on the one day_laborer host (triggers.py line 85: 'A hook can host both kinds').
plan: Occupation, on-play no-op (register_occupation(CARD_ID, lambda s,i: s)). SPACES={'day_laborer'}; register_action_space_hook(CARD_ID, SPACES) to host the atomic space. Mandatory part: register_auto('after_action_space', CARD_ID, _eligible_auto, _apply_auto) where _eligible_auto checks pending_stack[-1].space_id in SPACES and _apply_auto adds Resources(wood=1, clay=1) to player idx. Optional buy: register('after_action_space', CARD_ID, _eligible_buy, _apply_buy) where _eligible_buy(state,idx,triggers_resolved) = CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved and space_id in SPACES and players[idx].resources.food>=1; _apply_buy does Resources(food=-1, stone=1). No cost/prereq/vps/passing.
ordering_note: Must register on after_action_space (text says 'each time AFTER you use'), NOT before. This is load-bearing twice: (1) the clarification 'these resources may not be used to pay for Cottager B087' is enforced FOR FREE because Cottager fires before_action_space (its build/renovate resolves before Excavator's after-grant exists) — do not try to special-case the cross-card constraint. (2) The mandatory auto and the optional FireTrigger are SEPARATE registrations on the same event; the optional buy must gate on triggers_resolved (once per use) and on food>=1 so it never offers a dead-end. The +1 wood/+1 clay is choiceless -> register_auto (never surfaced); the 'buy 1 stone' is a player choice -> register (declinable FireTrigger, decline = host Stop/Proceed).
errata: Clarification: 'These resources may not be used to pay for the effect of the Cottager B087.' Enforced automatically by the before/after_action_space timing split (no code needed). Day Laborer is an ATOMIC space, so register_action_space_hook is required to push a PendingActionSpace host whose Proceed flips to the after-phase.
open_question: Food cost is fixed at 1 and on-hand-only, so unlike Ox Goad no PendingFoodPayment/liquidation path is needed (eligibility just checks food>=1). Confirm that's acceptable rather than routing the 1-food purchase through the food-payment frontier; the rules treat it as a simple at-the-moment 1-food spend, so direct debit is correct.

template: (none exists) closest shape is basic_wish_for_children via PendingFamilyGrowth, but it does NOT apply — no card grants family growth today
ordering_note: Two non-obvious traps if/when infra lands: (1) 'complete rounds left' must be the food cost paid AT PLAY, and the errata makes growth conditional on affording it — so the affordability check and the food debit must be one atomic gate (if forced via Paper Knife and unaffordable, the card is not played at all and is removed). (2) the growth is 'even without room' so it must bypass the people_total < _num_rooms gate (legality.py:762) yet still honor the hard people_total < 5 cap; do NOT reuse the room-gated basic-wish path.
errata: Clarification: If forced to play this card (e.g. via Paper Knife A003) and you can't afford the extra food cost, the card may not be played and is removed from the game. Verbatim text: 'When you play this card, immediately pay an amount of food equal to the number of complete rounds left to play to take a "Family Growth Even without Room" action.' Deck C #127, Corbarius Expansion, category Farm Planner, players 3+, no printed cost/prereq/vps/passing.
open_question: Implementing Lover requires three pieces of shared infra that do not exist: (a) a card-granted Family Growth that places the newborn on NO action space (the only growth executor, _resolve_wish_for_children in resolution.py:239, requires a space_id and increments get_space(board,space_id).workers, assuming a parent worker was already placed by a worker placement — the cheat-sheet's flagged place_on_space=False addition); (b) an 'even without room' growth that bypasses the people_total < _num_rooms room gate while keeping the people_total < 5 cap; (c) a play-time affordability gate for a variable food cost = complete_rounds_left (= 14 - round_number, plus whether the current round counts as 'complete') that, per the errata, makes the whole play fail-and-remove when forced and unaffordable. Recommend deferring as part of the broader 'card-granted family growth' infra batch; ask the user to confirm (i) the exact 'complete rounds left' formula and (ii) the desired handling of the Paper-Knife forced-and-unaffordable removal case, which intersects forced-play infra not yet built.

template: agricola/cards/carpenters_parlor.py (wood-house-gated build_room register_formula) + carpenter.py (occupation registration via register_occupation + _noop_on_play). Closest precedent is carpenters_parlor; only delta is occupation-not-minor and the round-dependent wood amount.
plan: register_formula('build_room', 'wooden_hut_extender', applies=lambda s,i,ctx: s.players[i].house_material==HouseMaterial.WOOD, formula=_formula). _formula(s,i,ctx)->Resources: wood = 5 if s.round_number<=5 else 4 if s.round_number<=7 else 3; return Resources(wood=wood, reed=1). register_occupation('wooden_hut_extender', _noop_on_play). No cost/prereq/vps on the occupation itself (occupations have no resource cost). The chokepoint effective_payments already surfaces the formula beside the printed ROOM_COSTS[WOOD]=5 wood+2 reed and Pareto-mins; reductions (e.g. Bricklayer) stack on top automatically.
ordering_note: Two thresholds must be exact: reed becomes 1 (NOT 2 -- the printed wood-room base is 5 wood + 2 reed, so the card drops reed to 1 AND swaps the wood schedule). Wood is round-banded: rounds 1-5 -> 5 wood, rounds 6-7 -> 4 wood, round 8+ -> 3 wood (i.e. s.round_number<=5, <=7, else). Read round from GameState.round_number (1-14). Because reed=1 < printed 2, the formula is a STRICT improvement over the base in every round (5w+1r through r5 dominates 5w+2r), so Pareto-min always keeps the formula when applicable; in a clay/stone house the gate is False and only the printed base survives -- byte-identical to today.
errata: None surfaced by card_text.py (no errata/clarification lines printed).
open_question: Card is Corbarius Expansion deck C, marked 'players 3+'. Target scope is the 2-player card game. The MECHANIC is fully 2-player-compatible (no per-player-count logic), but if the project is excluding 3+-only cards from the 2p pool the user may want it omitted from card_pool dealing rather than unimplemented. Recommend implementing the module (harmless, correct) and letting pool-selection decide inclusion.

template: agricola/cards/sleeping_corner.py (clause 1: register_occupancy_override on a wish space) + agricola/cards/stable_architect.py (no-op on_play occupation idiom)
plan: register_occupation('second_spouse', lambda state, idx: state)  # pure occupancy relaxer, no on-play effect. register_occupancy_override(_occupancy_override). _occupancy_override(state, space_id): return False unless space_id == 'urgent_wish_for_children'; ap = state.current_player; require CARD_ID in state.players[ap].occupations; require get_space(state.board, space_id).workers[ap] == 0; others = sum(1 for i,w in enumerate(workers) if i != ap and w > 0); return others == 1. No cost/prereq/vps (Lessons-played occupation).
ordering_note: COUNT PLAYERS, NOT WORKERS: a normally-used urgent-wish space already holds TWO of the first player's workers (parent placed + newborn generated by _resolve_wish_for_children), so the clarification 'not if any second/third person occupies it' means exactly one OTHER PLAYER may hold it (others_with_workers == 1), tolerating that player's parent+newborn pair — never a raw worker count. The 'from round 12-13' phrasing is purely descriptive of the space's stage-5 reveal timing (urgent_wish_for_children is a stage-5 card; _is_available short-circuits on `not sp.revealed`, so the override is only ever consulted once the space exists) — it is NOT a separate round-gate to enforce.

template: conservator.py (renovate-target extension) + bricklayer.py/clay_plasterer.py (renovate cost reduction/formula) + manservant.py (conditional latch) / CardStore for a first-renovation flag
ordering_note: The load-bearing subtlety is the 'On your FIRST renovation' constraint. The two cost effects map cleanly to existing machinery (register_renovate_target_extension adds WOOD->STONE like Conservator; register_reduction('renovate', -2 stone) like Bricklayer, both gated on house_material==WOOD and CostCtx.num_rooms>=5). But the cost-modifier predicates run read-only during legality enumeration at the cost chokepoint and have NO notion of 'which renovation number this is'. A WOOD-house+>=5-wood-rooms gate APPROXIMATES 'first renovation to stone' (after a wood->stone direct renovate you are in a stone house, and after a normal wood->clay first renovation you no longer have wood rooms), but it is NOT the rule 'first renovation': e.g. a player could renovate wood->clay first WITHOUT this card, and the card-author intent is that the bonus is then spent/gone. Faithfully enforcing 'first renovation' requires a first-renovation latch (a CardStore flag set by an after_renovate hook, read by the cost-mod predicate) coupling THREE registries plus new ordinal tracking that does not exist anywhere (grep for renovation_count/first_renovat/build_index-on-renovate found nothing). The interaction of target-extension + reduction + genuine first-renovation gating crosses the DEFER threshold (new shared semantics + ambiguity about whether the WOOD/>=5-rooms approximation is acceptable).
errata: none (no errata or clarifications printed for this card)
open_question: Is the WOOD-house + num_rooms>=5 gate an acceptable faithful model of 'On your first renovation' (it coincidentally excludes a clay/stone house and a post-first-renovation state), or must we add genuine first-renovation tracking (a CardStore latch set by an after_renovate hook and read by the renovate target-extension + cost-reduction predicates)? Also confirm 'at least 5 wood rooms' counts rooms while house_material==WOOD (i.e. evaluated at the moment of the renovation, before the upgrade).

template: agricola/cards/assistant_tiller.py (occupation + optional before_action_space trigger on an ATOMIC space hosted via register_action_space_hook, pushing a sub-decision pending) crossed with agricola/cards/scholar.py (pushing PendingPlayOccupation(cost=Resources(food=1)) and the playable+payable occupation eligibility gate via playable_occupations + _payable_occupation).
plan: register_occupation('private_teacher', lambda s,i: s)  # no on-play effect. SPACES=frozenset({'grain_seeds'}); register_action_space_hook('private_teacher', SPACES) (grain_seeds is ATOMIC, needs a host frame). _eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved): 'private_teacher' not in triggers_resolved AND s.pending_stack[-1].space_id in SPACES AND lessons is occupied: get_space(s.board,'lessons').workers != (0,0) AND playable_occupations(s,i) AND _payable_occupation(s,i,s.players[i],Resources(food=1)). _apply(s,i): return push(s, PendingPlayOccupation(player_idx=i, initiated_by_id='card:private_teacher', cost=Resources(food=1))). register('before_action_space','private_teacher',_eligible,_apply) (OPTIONAL declinable FireTrigger — 'you can also play'). No cost/prereq/vps/passing.
ordering_note: Two coupled subtleties: (1) the gate is occupancy of a DIFFERENT space (Lessons), not Grain Seeds' own state — check get_space(board,'lessons').workers != (0,0), NOT _is_available (which would falsely block on the card-game occupancy-override path / require revealed). In 2p there is exactly one Lessons space and the placing worker is on Grain Seeds, so the opponent's worker on Lessons is what makes the trigger live. (2) 'each time you use Grain Seeds' = before_action_space (a Lessons-occupied check is a board read, not 'immediately after'), and the trigger is OPTIONAL/declinable (use register, not register_auto) because 'you can also play' — decline = the host's Proceed. The clarification ('if the played occupation has a Grain Seeds effect, it also triggers immediately') needs NO special handling: because we fire before_action_space (BEFORE the Grain Seeds effect resolves) and the new occupation registers its own before_action_space hook, the host's trigger loop will re-poll and surface that occupation's effect on the same Grain Seeds frame automatically.
errata: Clarification (verbatim): 'If the played occupation has an effect when using “Grain Seeds”, it also triggers immediately.' No errata. Printed players 3+ — but the effect is fully meaningful in 2p (one Lessons space; opponent can occupy it), and the codebase precedent (geologist/chophouse) implements 3+-tagged cards' 2p-applicable behavior; the 3+ tag here is the game-size requirement, not a clause to drop.
open_question: VERIFY before implementing: (a) Does pushing PendingPlayOccupation(cost=food=1) from a non-Lessons before_action_space context resolve cleanly through _enumerate_pending_play_occupation / _execute_play_occupation, OR does it expect a play-occupation-variant registration? Scholar registers register_play_variant_trigger but that is for its occupation-OR-minor CHOICE; Private Teacher has no choice (occupation only), so it likely needs none — confirm the enumerator does not KeyError on an unregistered variant card and that the flat 1-food cost rides on the frame correctly. (b) Confirm the trigger fires for the GRAIN-SEEDS-placing current player (before_action_space player_idx = the active placer), so 'when Lessons is occupied' reads the OPPONENT's worker on Lessons. (c) triggers_resolved scoping: 'each time you use Grain Seeds' = once per Grain Seeds placement — confirm the before_action_space triggers_resolved budget is per-placement so it can re-fire on a later Grain Seeds turn but not twice in one.

template: bucksaw.py (after_renovate optional FireTrigger + CardStore-banked VP + register_scoring) is the closest, but it does NOT cover this card's optional 0..N quantity decision
ordering_note: The blocker is the OPTIONAL multi-quantity decision: 'place UP TO 1 wood in EACH of your rooms' is a choose-how-many (0..min(wood_in_supply, num_rooms)) placement where each placed wood costs 1 wood from supply and banks 1 VP. (a) It must fire ONLY when the renovate target is STONE (after_renovate, gate on player.house_material == HouseMaterial.STONE post-renovate). (b) The quantity is genuinely a player choice with a real tradeoff (each wood -> 1 VP but wood retains mid/late-game build/fence utility), so it CANNOT be auto-maxed without a rules deviation. The existing once-per-action FireTrigger caps a card at one fire via triggers_resolved, so a repeated single-wood fire is impossible without new infra; and PendingCardChoice is a MANDATORY one-of-N pick with NO decline path (Seasonal Worker / Childless shape) and a CARD_CHOICE_RESOLVERS one-shot resolver -- it does not model an optional 0..N numeric placement with a per-unit cost.
errata: Card text (verbatim): 'When you renovate to stone, you can place up to 1 wood from your supply in each of your rooms. During scoring, each such wood is worth 1 bonus point.' Corbarius Expansion, deck C #132, players 3+, category Points Provider. No printed VPs/cost/prereq/passing surfaced by card_text.py.
open_question: Need a new optional numeric-quantity granted-effect primitive (a 0..N 'place k goods, pay 1 wood each, bank k VP' decision on after_renovate-to-stone), OR confirmation from the user that auto-placing the maximum affordable wood (min(rooms, wood)) is an acceptable simplification given wood-vs-VP is nearly always pro-VP. The VP itself can be banked in CardStore (Bucksaw/Mantlepiece pattern) -- a literal per-room goods stack on the grid is NOT required -- so the ONLY blocker is the optional 0..N choice frame, not the scoring.

template: agricola/cards/stable_architect.py (pure end-game scoring occupation: register_occupation no-op + register_scoring)
plan:
register_occupation('soldier', lambda state, idx: state)  # no on-play effect; played via Lessons.
def _score(state, idx): r = state.players[idx].resources; return min(r.wood, r.stone)  # 1 VP per stone-wood pair in supply.
register_scoring('soldier', _score).
No cost/prereq/vps/passing (occupation). Resources fields verified: r.wood, r.stone on state.players[idx].resources.
ordering_note: Score is the number of PAIRS = min(wood, stone), NOT wood+stone. The errata clause 'you cannot score additional points for the resources scored with this card' is a no-op in this engine: base scoring (scoring.py) never scores raw wood/stone in supply (it scores fields/pastures/animals/rooms/people/majors/craft-bonuses/begging). The craft-building bonus consumes resources into built rooms rather than scoring the raw pile, so there is no double-count path to suppress.
errata: Card text: 'During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each stone-wood pair in your supply. You cannot score additional points for the resources scored with this card.' Corbarius Expansion, deck C #133, players 3+, category Points Provider. No printed cost/prereq/vps/passing.
open_question: Card is printed 'players 3+'. Confirm it should be included in the 2-player card pool at all (the no-other-resource-scoring clause is moot in 2p anyway, so the effect is identical) -- but pool-inclusion of a 3+ card is a curation question, not a mechanics blocker.

ordering_note: If ever implemented, the score is NOT well-defined under the current model: 'each space holding at least 1 cattle' requires a CANONICAL per-cell assignment of cattle to farmyard cells, but the engine stores only scalar Animals totals and lets the player distribute freely across pastures/stables/house. Even after adding location tracking, you'd need a tie-broken canonical placement (e.g. maximize occupied cattle-spaces) so scoring is deterministic and the agent can optimize it — otherwise the bonus is ambiguous.
