Currently only totals are stored in Animals; location is derived from capacity checks'). Cow Prince scores per farmyard space holding >=1 cattle, which needs that location tracking PLUS a canonical placement rule. This is exactly the deferred 'card-as-animal-holder / location-aware scoring' class — defer until a per-cell animal-placement subsystem exists. Note also this card is a 3+ player card.

template: big_country.py (on-play graduated-by-round wood half) + stable_architect.py (scoring shape); but the scoring half has NO viable template
ordering_note: The scoring clause 'each player with no negative points gets 3 bonus points' affects BOTH players, but the existing register_scoring registry sums fn(state, idx) ONLY for the player who _owns the card (scoring.py line 238-241: `if _owns(ps, card_id)`). A normal register_scoring term therefore awards the bonus only to the Constable owner, never the opponent — wrong. AND 'no negative points' requires inspecting every negative-capable source (field_tiles/pastures/grain/veg/animals -1 floors, unused_spaces<=0, begging<=0, plus negative card/printed-VP terms — the clarification enumerates 'printed card values, beggars, negative categories, negative bonuses'), which is a whole-breakdown predicate the registry does not expose to a per-player scoring fn. Both gaps are new shared scoring infrastructure.
errata: Clarification: 'Negative point examples: printed card values, beggars, negative categories, negative bonuses.' Card printed as players-3+ but graduated-by-player-count slashes (1/3/6/9 rounds -> 1/2/3/4 wood) are independent of player count, so the on-play half is well-defined for 2p. Deck C #135, Consul Dirigens, category Points Provider, no cost/prereq/printed VPs.
open_question: Need a new opponent-affecting / 'each player'-style scoring mechanism (a global scoring term not gated by _owns, evaluated once per player) AND a 'has no negative points' predicate over the full per-player ScoreBreakdown (sum of all negative-capable categories + negative card terms == 0). Should this be a register_global_scoring(card_id, fn(state, scored_idx)->int) that the owner triggers but is evaluated for both seats, with a helper has_no_negative_points(state, idx)? The on-play wood half alone is trivially implementable (copy big_country's 14-round_number, thresholds 9->4/6->3/3->2/1->1 wood) but implementing only the on-play half would ship a silently mis-scored Points-Provider occupation, so defer the whole card until the scoring mechanism exists.

template: stable_architect.py (owner-only register_scoring) — but blocked because the scoring award must reach the NON-owning opponent too
ordering_note: BLOCKER: the scoring clause 'each player with a pasture of highest capacity gets 3 bonus points' awards 3 points to EVERY player tying for the max pasture capacity across all players — including the opponent who does NOT own the card. register_scoring(card_id, fn(state, idx)) is structurally owner-only: score(state, idx) sums only terms where _owns(ps, card_id) is True (agricola/scoring.py L238-240), so a P0-owned occupation can never add points to P1's score. This requires NEW shared infrastructure (an all-player / opponent scoring-award mechanism) that does not exist in the registries. Secondary subtlety even for the wood half: 'still 3/6/9 complete rounds left -> 2/3/4 wood' is a stepwise threshold on rounds_left = NUM_ROUNDS(14) - state.round_number, evaluated at play time (>=9 left ->4, >=6 ->3, >=3 ->2, else 0); exact threshold/round-boundary semantics ('complete rounds left' vs current round) are easy to get wrong and worth user confirmation. Implementing only the wood grant while dropping the opponent's scoring award would be a silently-incomplete (wrong) card.
errata: None listed. Card is from Corbarius Expansion, deck C #136, category Points Provider, players 3+ (3+ clause itself is not a blocker — other 3+ cards like geologist/chophouse/feeding_dish are implemented by scaling to 2 players; the blocker is the opponent-side scoring award, not the player count).
open_question: Is there appetite to add an all-player / opponent scoring-award mechanism (e.g. an any_player variant of register_scoring, or a comparative-scoring helper that the score() loop applies to BOTH players)? Several 'each player who ... gets N points' cards will need it. Also confirm the exact reading of 'still 3/6/9 complete rounds left to play' (rounds_left = 14 - round_number, stepwise >=9/>=6/>=3 -> 4/3/2 wood) before any future implementation of the wood half.

template: wood_workshop.py (closest: before/after improvement-event auto) + cookery_outfitter.py (cooking-index set) — but neither suffices
ordering_note: Fires AFTER a cooking improvement is built (any_player=True), granting +1 wood +1 food to the owner. The fatal gap: the auto-effect eligibility/apply signature is (state, owner) only — it is NOT passed the acting player or the CommitBuildMajor action, and PendingBuildMajor carries NO major_idx field (the index lives only on the commit action). So at after_build_major the handler can confirm SOME major was just built (top frame = PendingBuildMajor, phase=='after') but cannot tell whether it was a COOKING improvement (Fireplace 0/1, Cooking Hearth 2/3, ovens 5/6) vs a non-cooking major (Well 4, etc.). Distinguishing them requires the built index, which the current event plumbing does not surface.
errata: No errata/clarifications in the card JSON. Note: card is players '3+' (out of 2-player scope). 'Plays OR builds a cooking improvement' is generic wording — in practice all cooking improvements are MAJORS, so it is always a build_major, never a play_minor. Open ruling: whether OVENS (Clay 5 / Stone 6) count as 'cooking improvements' is ambiguous — Cookery Outfitter's errata explicitly EXCLUDES ovens, but Charcoal Burner has no such note, so the intended set is genuinely unsettled.
open_question: Two blockers: (1) the card is a 3+-player card, outside the 2-player target scope. (2) No mechanism exists to tell which major improvement was just built at the after_build_major auto event — needs new shared infra (either a more specific event like after_build_cooking_improvement, or threading the built major_idx onto the PendingBuildMajor frame / into the auto-effect apply signature). Also requires a ruling on whether ovens count as cooking improvements. Defer and ask the user on all three.

template: seasonal_worker.py (Day Laborer occupation hook + PendingCardChoice mandatory-with-choice); young_animal_market.py (immediate animal grant)
ordering_note: If ever implemented: this is a before_action_space MANDATORY-WITH-CHOICE on the day_laborer host (exactly Seasonal Worker's shape) whose options are dynamic — base choice {sheep, grain}, plus food-gated alternates {buy_boar @1 food, buy_cattle @2 food}. The food-cost options must be filtered out of the options tuple at push time when unaffordable (PendingCardChoice surfaces every option with NO decline, so an unaffordable buy can't be offered); the food debit + animal gain happen inside the card's _resolve. 'On the Day Laborer action space you ALSO get' = before_action_space per the each-time-you-use ruling, sharing the before-phase with Seasonal Worker / Assistant Tiller / Cottager.
errata: None. Card text verbatim: 'On the "Day Laborer" action space, you also get your choice of 1 sheep or 1 grain. Instead of that good, you can buy 1 wild boar for 1 food or 1 cattle for 2 food.' Corbarius Expansion, deck C #138, Goods Provider, players 3+ (note: printed for 3+ players; usable in 2p per project scope).
open_question: Two deferred mechanisms compound here. (1) IMMEDIATE ANIMAL GRANT with no accommodation: every branch grants a live animal (sheep, or boar/cattle bought) mid-Day-Laborer with no accommodation/overflow handling — the cheat-sheet's explicit DEFER class (immediate animal grants are markets/breeding/harvest/scheduled-round-start only). young_animal_market gets away with an on-PLAY animal gain only because net count is unchanged and the engine doesn't force accommodation on gain; a strict +1/+2 animal gain on a space-hook is the genuinely-deferred case. (2) BUY-FOOD->ANIMAL: 'buy 1 boar for 1 food / 1 cattle for 2 food' is a standalone buy-with-food purchase — flagged DEFER (buy-food->good). Confirm with the user whether to (a) build the immediate-animal-grant + food-purchase infra now, or (b) keep deferring until a shared animal-acquisition-with-accommodation primitive exists.

template: consultant.py (for the on-play grant half only); NO template exists for the at-any-time reed->food conversion half
ordering_note: The card is ONE unit but its text has two parts: (a) on-play +1 reed +1 food (trivially a Consultant copy via register_occupation), and (b) a PERSISTENT 'At any time, you can turn 1 reed into 2 food'. Part (b) is the defining feature and CANNOT be faithfully represented today. The engine deliberately never surfaces standalone at-any-time conversions (Foundations: optionality is preserved by bundling conversions into the food-payment / harvest-feeding decision points where the food proceeds are actually needed). The harvest-conversion registry (HARVEST_CONVERSIONS, register_harvest_conversion) is the nearest mechanism but it surfaces conversions ONLY during the FEED sub-phase with a once-per-harvest cap (PlayerState.harvest_conversions_used) -- that is strictly narrower than 'at any time' and would mis-model the card (it must also be available to pay food costs / card costs anytime, per FOOD_PAYMENT_DESIGN.md). The general at-any-time food-liquidation/conversion-closure machinery is DESIGNED but NOT IMPLEMENTED (FOOD_PAYMENT_DESIGN.md). Implementing only part (a) would ship a silently-incomplete, strictly-inferior card -- not acceptable. Defer the whole card until the food-payment conversion machinery lands; then it is a Tier-1 on-play grant + one register_conversion/at-any-time entry. Most error-prone subtlety AT IMPLEMENTATION TIME: this is reed->food (build resource -> food), the inverse direction of a typical food-payment liquidation, so it must be wired as a conversion node usable by BOTH the harvest-feed frontier AND any future card-cost food-payment frontier, with the Pareto-dominance computed over upstream goods only (never over the food proceeds), exactly as the begging/feeding frontier does -- otherwise it gets pruned away.
errata: None. Card is a 3+ player printed card (Corbarius Expansion, deck C #139). The on-play grant '1 reed and 1 food' has no player-count branch, so no 2-player adaptation is needed for part (a). No errata/clarifications returned by card_text.py.
open_question: When the food-payment/at-any-time-conversion machinery (FOOD_PAYMENT_DESIGN.md) lands, confirm whether a persistent unbounded reed->2food conversion is exposed via a register_conversion entry surfaced in EVERY food-payment frontier (harvest feed + card-cost food payment), rather than the FEED-only once-per-harvest HARVEST_CONVERSIONS path -- the latter would incorrectly cap and mis-time it.

template: agricola/cards/bricklayer.py (register_reduction on 'renovate' + 'build_room'); prereq helper modeled on agricola/cards/asparagus_gift.py field-cell iteration
plan: def _no_reed(state, idx, ctx, cost): return cost - Resources(reed=cost.reed)  # zero out the reed component (floor-at-0 in apply_reductions makes plain subtraction safe, but subtracting cost.reed is exact). register_reduction('renovate', CARD_ID, _no_reed); register_reduction('build_room', CARD_ID, _no_reed). prereq=_three_grain_fields(state,idx): count FIELD cells in farmyard.grid (3x5) with cell.grain>0, return count>=3. register_minor(CARD_ID, prereq=_three_grain_fields, vps=1)  # cost is FREE per card_text (no cost printed); no min/max_occupations.
ordering_note: Effect is 'no longer NEED reed' = remove the reed component ENTIRELY, not a fixed -1. Use cost - Resources(reed=cost.reed) so a build needing 2 reed (e.g. renovate to clay) drops the full 2, not just 1. A literal '-1 reed' (Bricklayer-style) would be WRONG for any cost with reed>=2. Register on 'renovate' and 'build_room' ONLY (singular event names per cost_mods registry) -- NOT build_major/play_minor (card says renovate or build a room only).
errata: None reported by card_text. Note prereq '3 Grain Fields' is a PREREQ (must hold at play time), not a cost; printed cost is FREE (none shown).
open_question: Interpretation of '3 Grain Fields' prereq: taken as >=3 FIELD cells currently carrying grain (cell.grain>0). Could alternatively mean fields growing grain specifically vs any sown field -- 'grain field' wording strongly implies grain present. Confirm if user reads it as grain-bearing fields (assumed) vs something looser.

template: consultant.py (on-play +1 grain part only); NO existing template for the Bake-Bread-instead-of-Minor redirect
ordering_note: The redirect is an ALTERNATIVE to (a replacement of) the play_minor sub-action, NOT an additive bake. 'Each time you get a Minor Improvement action' = before/at each minor-granting host's choose-phase (Major/Minor Improvement space, Meeting Place, House Redevelopment minor option, Basic Wish for Children minor option) you may pick a Bake Bread action in place of playing the minor. It must offer BOTH branches (play minor OR bake instead) wherever a minor action is granted, and the bake substitution must respect that some of those hosts the minor is optional/mandatory-one. Treating it as additive (bake AND minor) would be wrong.
errata: none surfaced by card_text.py. 3+ player card (Corbarius); 2-player engine has no player-count gate issue here — effect is uniform.
open_question: There is no machinery to inject an ALTERNATIVE sub-action ('Bake Bread instead') into the play_minor choice branch of the minor-granting host spaces. Existing extension points (BAKE_BREAD_ELIGIBILITY_EXTENSIONS, granted-sub-action FireTriggers, register_play_occupation_variant/PLAY_VARIANT_TRIGGERS) are all additive or occupation-only and do not replace a host's play_minor branch with a bake branch. Implementing this needs net-new shared infra: a registry + legality enumerator hook that, for an owning player, offers a ChooseSubAction('bake_bread') alternative at every space that grants a 'Minor Improvement' action (Major/Minor Improvement, Meeting Place, House Redev minor opt, Basic Wish minor opt), plus the commit wiring to push PendingBakeBread on that branch. Also needs a settled ruling on the exact set of spaces that count as 'getting a Minor Improvement action' in this 2p card engine. Defer and ask the user.

template: agricola/cards/catcher.py (occupation no-op on-play + before_action_space auto) crossed with agricola/cards/corf.py (any_player=True owner-fires-on-opponent-turn, +1 goods) and agricola/cards/claw_knife.py (same sheep_market space, confirms NO register_action_space_hook needed)
plan: register_occupation('sheep_provider', lambda state, idx: state)  # no-op on play; the hook IS the effect. _eligible(state, idx): return state.pending_stack[-1].space_id == 'sheep_market'. _apply(state, idx): p = fast_replace(state.players[idx], resources=state.players[idx].resources + Resources(grain=1)); return fast_replace(state, players=tuple(p if i==idx else state.players[i] for i in range(2))). register_auto('before_action_space', 'sheep_provider', _eligible, _apply, any_player=True). Occupation has no cost/prereq/vps. Add module import to cards/__init__.py.
ordering_note: Two coupled subtleties: (1) any_player=True is REQUIRED — 'each time ANY player (including you) uses Sheep Market' means the owner gains grain on the OPPONENT's Sheep Market turn too; apply_auto_effects iterates owners=range(2) only when any_player is set (triggers.py:138). (2) Despite any_player, NO register_action_space_hook is needed: sheep_market is NON-ATOMIC and self-hosts — _initiate_sheep_market (resolution.py:364-376) calls apply_auto_effects(state,'before_action_space',ap) on every use including the opponent's, so the host frame already exists (the hook index only conditionally hosts ATOMIC spaces; verified vs Claw Knife / Milk Jug). Adding the hook would be harmless-but-wrong noise. Grain has no capacity limit so the +1 grant is always safe; before- vs after-phase is immaterial here (pure goods grant, not threshold-on-accumulated like Corf).
errata: Printed text reads 'players 3+' (Corbarius Expansion crop-provider). This restriction is irrelevant in the 2-player engine and has no mechanical effect — implement normally. No errata/clarification on the effect itself; card_text.py shows status todo.

template: agricola/cards/brewing_water.py (optional before_action_space trigger on an atomic space, goods grant) + agricola/cards/milk_jug.py (the 'other = 1 - idx' opponent-grant pattern); corn_scoop.py is the same grain_seeds atomic-host structure but mandatory.
plan:
register_occupation('market_crier', lambda s, i: s)  # no on-play effect.
register_action_space_hook('market_crier', frozenset({'grain_seeds'}))  # host the atomic Grain Seeds space when owned.
register('before_action_space', 'market_crier', _eligible, _apply)  # OPTIONAL declinable FireTrigger ('you can get'); decline = host Proceed.
_eligible(state, idx, triggers_resolved): CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved (once per use) AND pending_stack[-1].space_id == 'grain_seeds'. No resource gate (pure gain).
_apply(state, idx): self gets Resources(grain=1, veg=1); other = 1 - idx gets Resources(grain=1) (milk_jug pattern, both in one atomic apply). No cost/prereq/vps/passing.
ordering_note: Must be OPTIONAL (register, not register_auto) because 'if you do, each other player gets 1 grain' is a real downside the owner may want to decline late game — corn_scoop's mandatory register_auto would be wrong. The self-grant (grain+veg) and the opponent's grain MUST resolve together in the single _apply: 'if you do' couples them, so you cannot take your grain/veg and skip giving the opponent grain. Use the optional-trigger arity: _eligible(state, idx, triggers_resolved) 3-arg, _apply(state, idx) 2-arg. before_action_space (per 'each time you use [space]' ruling, no 'immediately after'); the once-per-use semantics come from the host's triggers_resolved guard.
errata: None reported by card_text.py (no errata/clarifications section emitted). Printed 'players 3+' (Consul Dirigens), but the slug is registered for the 2-player card game; in 2p 'each other player' is exactly the single opponent (other = 1 - idx) — well-defined, no scaling needed.
open_question: Card is printed for 3+ players; confirm it is in-scope for the 2-player pool (mechanics are fully well-defined for 2p — one opponent). No engine blocker either way.

template: agricola/cards/milk_jug.py (Milk Jug, A50 — Cattle Market before_action_space auto) + feeding_dish.py (confirms the gained-staging mechanism); on-play no-op like catcher.py
plan: register_occupation('cowherd', _on_play) where _on_play is identity (no-op). register_auto('before_action_space', 'cowherd', _eligible, _apply) with any_player=False (owner-only, 'each time YOU use'). _eligible(s,i): return s.pending_stack[-1].space_id == 'cattle_market'. _apply(s,i): top = s.pending_stack[-1]; return replace_top(s, fast_replace(top, gained=top.gained + 1)). No cost/prereq/vps/passing — occupations played via Lessons. Imports: register_occupation (specs), register_auto (triggers), replace_top (pending), fast_replace (replace).
ordering_note: DO NOT add cattle directly to player.resources/animals. _initiate_cattle_market stages the picked-up cattle on PendingCattleMarket.gained (an int, NOT on the player) and fires before_action_space with that frame ON TOP of the stack, BEFORE CommitAccommodate moves them onto the player. Cowherd must bump that frame's `gained` by 1 via replace_top, so the +1 cattle flows through the SAME accommodation/overflow Pareto frontier as the market's own cattle (capacity, conversion-on-overflow). Adding to the player directly would bypass accommodation and is wrong. Fires unconditionally on use even if gained==0 (empty space): 0->1 is still correct.
errata: None. Verbatim text: 'Each time you use the "Cattle Market" accumulation space (introduced in round 10 or 11), you get 1 additional cattle.' Card is printed 'players 3+' / Consul Dirigens, but cattle_market exists as a stage-4 accumulation space (rounds 10-11) in this 2p engine — STAGE_CARDS[4]=['cattle_market', ...] in constants.py, NONATOMIC_HANDLERS — so the hook maps cleanly. cattle_market is NON-ATOMIC (self-hosts via _initiate_cattle_market's apply_auto_effects before_action_space call), so NO register_action_space_hook is needed.

The boar lives ON THE CARD (a separate per-card animal pile), never in the player's animal supply, so it is exempt from the normal animal-accommodation/overflow path on grant but DOES participate in breeding with newborns spilling to the farmyard (and only then needing accommodation).
errata: clarifications: 'This card only holds boar received via this effect.' and 'The boars may participate in breeding (but the newborn boar must find space elsewhere.)' No errata. Note: printed players-count is 4+ (Corbarius), but triage scope is 2-player.
open_question: Multiple compounding gaps, each individually a defer: (1) NO precedent for animals held ON a card separate from the player's animal supply ('1 wild boar, held by this card') -- the engine's Animals live on PlayerState, and breeding/accommodation/scoring all read player supply, not a per-card pile; supporting a card-held boar pile that ALSO feeds breeding-with-spill-to-farmyard is new shared infra. (2) Immediate animal grant with no accommodation (cheat-sheet DEFER: immediate animal grants -- markets/breeding/harvest/scheduled-round-start only). (3) 'Each time you use an accumulation space' is a SET-WIDE hook over all 10 ACCUMULATION_SPACES (constants.ACCUMULATION_SPACES), several of which are atomic (forest/clay_pit/reed_bank/quarries/...) and would each need register_action_space_hook; there is no single existing event keyed on 'any accumulation space' -- before/after_action_space filter by one space_id. (4) The mandatory 4-clay->boar conversion is a custom per-card good->card-held-animal exchange, not expressible via cost-mod conversions (those are resource->resource for paying build costs). Recommend DEFER and ask the user; the card-held-animal + breeding-spill mechanic in particular needs a deliberate design decision before any card of this shape is built.

template: (closest shapes: roughcaster.py / maintenance_premium.py for after_renovate any_player autos; bucksaw.py for optional after_renovate FireTrigger) — but NO single template combines the required mechanisms
ordering_note: The trigger fires on ANOTHER player's renovate but the EFFECT (pay 2 food, build a clay room) belongs to the OWNER, not the acting/renovating player. So the firing index (acting=opponent) and the effect target (owner) diverge — the apply/build-room must run with owner_idx, never acting_player. 'Renovates to stone' per the clarification includes a direct wood->stone renovate, so eligibility must key on the post-renovate house being STONE (after_renovate fires post-application, like roughcaster), NOT specifically a clay->stone transition.
errata: Clarification: 'If another player renovates from wood to stone, this card's effect is still triggered.' (So eligibility = opponent's house is now stone post-renovate, regardless of prior material.) Printed: players 4+, deck C #149, Corbarius Expansion, category Farm Planner, no cost/prereq/vps/passing on the occupation itself.
open_question: Two compounding blockers: (1) The effect is OPTIONAL/declinable ('you CAN pay 2 food') yet must fire for the OWNER on the OPPONENT's renovate. The optional-trigger machinery (register/FireTrigger, TriggerEntry) has NO any_player concept — optional triggers are enumerated by legal_actions only for the decider at the current pending frame (the acting player), so an out-of-turn owner can never be offered the FireTrigger. any_player exists ONLY on register_auto (mandatory, choiceless), which is the wrong firing kind here. (2) 'Build a clay room at no additional cost' is a granted BUILD-ROOM sub-action requiring a cell/geometry choice — it must push a PendingBuildRooms frame for the NON-ACTING owner mid-resolution of the opponent's renovate turn (out-of-turn sub-decision with player_idx=owner). This is the same nonacting-player-sub-action gap flagged in the cheat-sheet. Plus the '2 food to build at no additional cost' is a fixed-food payment (food-cost machinery is deferred per FOOD_PAYMENT_DESIGN.md, not yet implemented). DEFER and ask the user how to model an optional out-of-turn owner-targeted granted build.

register_minor('trellis', cost=Cost(), min_occupations=2)  # cost null, vps null, kept (passing_left null).

_eligible(state, idx, triggers_resolved): return ('trellis' not in triggers_resolved) and state.pending_stack[-1].space_id == 'pig_market' and _any_legal_pasture_commit(state, state.players[idx], space_id='card:trellis', initiated_by_id='card:trellis')  # once-per-use + dead-end guard.
_apply(state, idx): from agricola.pending import PendingGrantedBuildFences, push; return push(state, PendingGrantedBuildFences(player_idx=idx, initiated_by_id='card:trellis'))  # multi-shot host; 'pay wood as usual' => register NO free-fence/discount, so PendingBuildFences pushed by the wrapper uses normal wood cost, build_fences_action=True.

The 'you can' optionality must be the FireTrigger itself (register with mandatory default False), NOT a register_auto. eligibility must gate on a buildable pasture existing now (via _any_legal_pasture_commit) so it never offers a dead-end FireTrigger, AND gate 'trellis' not in triggers_resolved so it fires at most once per Pig Market use. 'pay wood for the fences as usual' => register NO free-fence seed/edge/pool and NO cost reduction; the granted PendingBuildFences just pays normal wood.

Recommend the wrapper: the FireTrigger already supplied the opt-in, but the wrapper gives the natural multi-shot 'build a pasture / Stop' loop and matches the only existing card that grants a literal Build Fences action. Confirm this is consistent with how a FireTrigger-initiated grant nests above the PendingPigMarket host frame (the wrapper lands on top of the market frame; after it pops, the market action proceeds since this is the BEFORE phase).

Even with that infra, a PendingSow(player_idx=owner) would have to be pushed/resolved during the opponent's space resolution (divergent-player_idx out-of-turn decision) in the AFTER phase ('each time AFTER another player uses') — a genuine out-of-turn interactive decision for the non-active player that the current optional path does not host.

template: ox_goad.py (optional FireTrigger w/ cost) crossed with maintenance_premium.py (after_renovate event) — but NEITHER supports the opponent-optional-trigger combination this card needs.

The optional-trigger path is the blocker — see blocker_group. As a fallback to even classify it: it would fire on after_renovate, eligibility = owner owns >=2 wood AND the renovator is the OTHER player, apply = pay 2 wood / gain 1 grain + 1 food + 1 accumulating bonus VP.
errata: No errata or clarifications printed. Verbatim text: 'Each time another player renovates, you can exchange exactly 2 wood for 1 grain, 1 food, and 1 bonus point.' Card is players 4+ (Consul Dirigens) — playable in the 2p card pool but the 'another player' clause is the 1 opponent.
open_question: PRIMARY BLOCKER: the optional/declinable FireTrigger path (register/TRIGGERS/_eligible_fire_triggers in legality.py) has NO any_player mechanism — TriggerEntry has no any_player field, and _eligible_fire_triggers only enumerates triggers for pending.player_idx, which at an after_renovate host frame is the RENOVATING (acting) player, not the owner. Only the MANDATORY/choiceless register_auto path supports any_player=True (Milk Jug). So an opponent-fired DECLINABLE trigger needs new shared infra: add any_player to TriggerEntry + register(), and have _eligible_fire_triggers scan all owners' optional triggers at a host frame (routing each FireTrigger to the owner-idx, not pending.player_idx, for both eligibility and apply). That is a cross-cutting legality/dispatch change, not a single-card copy. SECONDARY: '1 bonus point' is an accumulating runtime VP — no storage path exists; would need a CardStore counter incremented per exchange + a register_scoring(pattern_maker, fn) that reads it (the 'A OR B'-style infra gaps the cheat-sheet says to defer). DEFER and ASK the user how opponent-optional triggers should be modeled (and whether to batch all such cards together).

template: consultant.py (immediate-goods on-play half) + schedules.py (deferred half) — but neither covers the return-home-phase timing this card needs
ordering_note: The food reward is paid at the START of the SAME round's returning-home phase (not a future round), and its amount = number of occupied action space cards at that instant — so it must be evaluated/applied during _resolve_return_home, AFTER all workers are placed but BEFORE they return home. Existing schedule_resources/schedule_effect only target FUTURE rounds via future_resources/future_rewards collected at start_of_round; there is no current-round / return-home slot, so this is a snapshot-at-return-home count, not a round-start schedule.

(2) Even setting scope aside, no return-home-phase card hook exists in the trigger machinery (only start_of_round / harvest_field / before-after_action_space); paying food at the start of the same round's returning-home phase based on a live occupied-space count would require a new shared event/hook into engine._resolve_return_home plus a same-turn deferred-reward path. Per the cardinal rule (needs new shared infra + out-of-scope player count), DEFER and ask the user.

template: consultant.py (on-play occupation) is the closest IF it were in scope, but the card is out of scope
ordering_note: If ever ported to a 4p engine: 'Immediately add 1 cattle ... Afterward, you get 1 grain + 1 food for each cattle on Cattle Market' — the +1 must be applied to the accumulation space FIRST, then the per-cattle grain+food is counted over the NEW total (the clarification 'X cattle => X grain + X food' counts post-add). Reading the count before adding would undercount by 1.
errata: Clarification present in data: 'X cattle on Cattle Market => X grain + X food'. No errata text otherwise.
open_question: Card is players:'4+' (deck C #156, Corbarius Expansion) — usable ONLY in 4+ player games; this engine is strictly 2-player and ALL 61 implemented occupations are players:'1+' (zero 3+/4+ implemented). The card is therefore out of scope for the 2-player card pool. Secondary blocker even if a 4p engine existed: the on-play effect ADDS 1 cattle from the general supply directly onto the shared 'Cattle Market' accumulation space (accumulated_amount += 1) and then reads that space's count — modifying a shared board accumulation space at card-play time is a mechanism no implemented card uses (it is not the same as taking goods OFF a space). Cattle Market itself exists in the engine (constants.py rate ('cattle',1); legality/resolution _*_cattle_market), so the read/write is mechanically expressible via get_space/with_space, but the whole card should be deferred on the player-count gate alone. Recommend: exclude 4+ cards from the 2-player pool entirely.

template: agricola/cards/small_scale_farmer.py (register_auto('start_of_round') choiceless-mandatory +resource income with an eligibility re-checked each round). childless.py is the start_of_round occupation analog; this card is simpler (no crop choice, so register_auto not register(mandatory=True)).
plan: register_occupation('resource_analyzer', lambda s,i: s)  # no on-play effect. register_auto('start_of_round', 'resource_analyzer', _eligible, _apply). register_start_of_round_hook('resource_analyzer'). _eligible(state, idx) -> bool: me=state.players[idx].resources; opp=state.players[1-idx].resources; count = sum(getattr(me,t) > getattr(opp,t) for t in ('wood','clay','reed','stone')); return count >= 2. _apply(state, idx): p=state.players[idx]; p=fast_replace(p, resources=p.resources+Resources(food=1)); return fast_replace(state, players=tuple(...)). No cost/prereq/vps/passing (plain occupation).
ordering_note: STRICT '>' not '>=' ('more building resources than' = strictly greater), and the threshold is 'at least two TYPES' (>=2 of the 4 building-resource types {wood,clay,reed,stone}, NOT total quantity). Count the types where my count strictly exceeds the opponent's, require count>=2. In 2-player 'all other players' = the single opponent (1-idx). register_auto eligibility is the 2-arg (state, idx) signature (no triggers_resolved) per small_scale_farmer; effect is choiceless so it is register_auto, not register(mandatory=True).
errata: None. No errata or clarifications surfaced by card_text.py. Card is printed for 4+ players but reduces cleanly to a single-opponent comparison in 2p.

Confirm the project includes Corbarius-expansion cards in scope (card is Corbarius #157, status todo) -- but that is a scope question, not an implementation blocker.

template: agricola/cards/plow_hero.py (closest before-placement shape) — but it does NOT actually fit; see blocker_group
ordering_note: Two interacting subtleties make even a workaround unsafe: (1) 'before you place a person' is a true per-placement hook with NO space scope, but the only placement-adjacent event the engine fires is `before_action_space`, which is SPACE-scoped and fires AFTER the worker is placed (plow_hero relies on this exact post-placement timing). To fire on EVERY placement you'd have to register_action_space_hook for ALL ~10 atomic spaces (forest/fishing/clay_pit/reed_bank/both quarries/grain_seeds/vegetable_seeds/day_laborer/meeting_place) so every space hosts and emits before_action_space — a force-everything-to-host hack no existing card does. (2) The '8 wood on accumulation spaces' threshold is order-sensitive: 'before you place a person' means count wood BEFORE the chosen space's wood is taken, but a before_action_space firing on the just-used space (e.g. forest) happens after placement and before goods are taken at Proceed — so whether the space you're placing on is included in the 8-wood count is ambiguous and timing-dependent.
errata: No errata/clarifications returned by card_text.py. Card is players 4+ (Corbarius Expansion, deck C #158, Food Provider). Verbatim: 'Each time before you place a person, if there are at least 8 wood total on accumulation spaces, you get 1 food.'
open_question: Implementing this cleanly requires a NEW engine event `before_place_person` (a per-placement hook fired in _apply_place_worker BEFORE the space resolves, independent of space and of whether the space is hosted) — new shared infra not in the registries. Additionally the card is 4-player-only ('players 4+'), outside the 2-player card-game scope. Confirm with user: (a) is a `before_place_person` event worth adding for this/other placement-trigger cards, and (b) is this card even in-scope given players 4+? Defer until both are answered.

The most error-prone subtlety is moot here because the card is blocked.

The effect = food_on(Traveling Players) - food_on(Fishing) granted from supply at start of each round; one operand is structurally unrepresentable. The start_of_round hook machinery (register_auto('start_of_round',...) + register_start_of_round_hook) DOES exist and would be the correct firing kind once/if Traveling Players is added, but the card is not part of the 2-player game scope.

template: calcium_fertilizers.py / brewery_pond.py (before_action_space + register_action_space_hook) — closest in SHAPE only; the target space cannot be expressed
ordering_note: The hook would be before_action_space (a bare 'each time before you use' = before, per the timing ruling; granting +1 grain is choiceless so register_auto). The fatal subtlety is WHICH space it fires on: 'the most recently revealed action space card (after it has been placed on the round space)' = the single action-space card turned up for the CURRENT round. GameState carries no such identity — only per-space `revealed` bools (which accumulate across all rounds of a stage) and `round_number`. There is no field recording which card was placed on this round's slot, and the reveal ORDER is deliberately hidden in the Environment as non-common-knowledge (Foundations: state holds only the public `revealed` flag, never which round an unrevealed/revealed card maps to). So the eligibility predicate `pending_stack[-1].space_id == most_recently_revealed_card` is uncomputable from public state.
errata: No errata or clarifications printed. Occupation, Corbarius Expansion, deck C #160, category Crop Provider. Players 4+. Cost/prereq/vps/passing: none (plain occupation, no cost, 0 VP, no passing).
open_question: To implement, the engine would need to expose the most-recently-revealed round-space card's space_id as common knowledge — a new GameState field (e.g. `last_revealed_space`) set in the RevealCard / _complete_preparation walk, plus a before_action_space eligibility predicate keyed on it. This is new shared infrastructure AND surfaces info the design treats as hidden; defer until the user decides whether to add round-space-identity tracking. Also a 4+ player card, outside the 2-player target scope.

template: young_animal_market.py (closest on-play animal-gain shape) / acorns_basket.py (deferred-animal precedent) — but neither fits: this is a NET animal increase with a non-animal cost, and an occupation, not a minor
ordering_note: If ever built: the food cost is 1 food x (number of HARVEST_ROUNDS still ahead). Use the harvest_house.py pattern but INVERTED — harvest_house counts COMPLETED harvests (h < round_number); Game Catcher needs REMAINING harvests (h >= round_number), and the errata says a harvest in progress when the card is played does NOT count toward the cost (so the predicate is round-number-vs-harvest, and the mid-harvest case must exclude the current harvest round). Getting the >= vs > boundary and the in-progress-harvest exclusion right is the chief subtlety. Secondary: the animal grant is +1 cattle AND +1 boar with NO animal cost, so it is a genuine net increase that can overflow accommodation.
errata: Clarification 1: if FORCED to play (e.g. Paper Knife A003) and cannot afford the extra food cost, the card may not be played and is removed from the game. Clarification 2: if played DURING a harvest (e.g. Begging Student D097) the current harvest does not add to the cost.
open_question: Two independent blockers, either alone forces defer: (1) IMMEDIATE NET ANIMAL GRANT — +1 cattle +1 wild boar with no animal cost is a net increase that can exceed farm accommodation; the cheat-sheet rule is explicit that immediate animal grants have no accommodation path (only markets/breeding/harvest/scheduled-round-start handle overflow), so a new immediate-grant-with-accommodation mechanism would be required. young_animal_market grants 1 cattle on-play ONLY because it costs 1 sheep (net animal count unchanged, cannot overflow) — that exemption does not apply here. (2) MANDATORY DYNAMIC FOOD COST GATING PLAYABILITY — OccupationSpec has NO cost/cost_fn field (occupations are free to play; only OPTIONAL food-source triggers like Paper Maker exist via OCCUPATION_FOOD_SOURCES). A mandatory, state-dependent food cost that determines whether the occupation can be played at all (and the forced-play / removed-from-game errata) is unmodeled shared infra. Both belong to DEFER buckets: immediate-animal-grant and pay-food-at-play-as-mandatory-cost. Recommend confirming with the user before any implementation.

template: agricola/cards/estate_worker.py (occupation + schedule_*) crossed with agricola/cards/acorns_basket.py (schedule_animals for animals)
plan: register_occupation('cattle_whisperer', _on_play). In _on_play: R = state.round_number; return schedule_animals(state, idx, (R+5, R+8), Animals(cattle=1)). No cost / prereq / vps / passing (occupation, played via Lessons; the schedule IS the effect). schedule_animals (cards/schedules.py) places 1 cattle on each of the two future round slots; they are collected + auto-accommodated at the start of those rounds by engine._collect_future_rewards (same pareto/can_accommodate machinery as the animal markets). Rounds > 14 are silently dropped by schedule_animals, correctly modeling 'place on each corresponding round space' near game-end.
ordering_note: 'Add 5 and 8 to the CURRENT round' is printed-board phrasing for offsets R+5 and R+8 from the round when the card is played (state.round_number), NOT fixed rounds 5 and 8. schedule_animals is 1-indexed and writes slot r-1; pass the absolute round numbers (R+5, R+8), not the offsets. Because the slots are R-relative, playing late (e.g. R>=10) means one or both rounds exceed 14 and are silently dropped by the helper's 1..14 clamp — the desired behavior. The animals are scheduled (future_rewards), NOT granted immediately, so the 'immediate animal grant has no accommodation -> DEFER' caveat does NOT apply: scheduled round-start grants are the explicitly-supported accommodation path (Acorns Basket is the named precedent in schedule_animals' docstring).
errata: None in card_text output. Status 'todo'. Tagged 'players 4+' / Consul Dirigens Expansion deck C #166, category Livestock Provider.
open_question: Card is from a 4+ player deck (printed 'players 4+'). Mechanically it is single-player and works fine in the 2-player engine, but whether it should be INCLUDED in the 2-player card pool is a pool-membership/scope decision for the user, separate from implementability. Implementation itself has no blocker.

The owner picks which of sheep(1f)/boar(2f)/cattle(2f) to buy (or decline), so it is a register(...) FireTrigger with eligibility = owner has the food AND can accommodate the animal — NOT a register_auto choiceless effect like Milk Jug. template: seasonal_worker.py (closest: optional/mandatory-with-choice before_action_space hook on the day_laborer host, register_action_space_hook) — but blocked, not implementable from it
ordering_note: If ever built: 'Each time you use Day Laborer, INSTEAD OF 2 food you CAN get 3 animals' is an OPTIONAL ALTERNATIVE that REPLACES the space's own 2-food reward (not an add-on like Seasonal Worker / Cottager). It must fire as a before_action_space declinable trigger that, when taken, must SUPPRESS the day_laborer base 2-food resolve — there is no mechanism today to cancel an atomic space's own effect from a hook, so the swap itself has no clean seam. Optionality lives at the parent host (choose-or-decline), never a per-frame flag.
errata: No errata/clarifications returned by card_text.py. Card is players-4+ printed (Corbarius), but project targets 2-player; the '3 different animals' = exactly one each of sheep/boar/cattle from general supply.
open_question: Two independent blockers, each needing new shared infrastructure: (1) IMMEDIATE animal grant (3 different animals) at an action-space trigger with NO accommodation path — schedule_animals/_collect_future_rewards only accommodates at round-START, and immediate animal grants are the cheat-sheet's explicit DEFER case. (2) 'You must pay 1 food each harvest left to play' is a RECURRING MANDATORY NEGATIVE obligation enforced at every remaining harvest feeding phase; FutureReward/future_resources are positive-goods-only and collected at round-start, with no negative-scheduling or per-harvest forced-payment hook into FEED. Also the effect REPLACES (not augments) the Day Laborer base reward, which no current hook can cancel. Defer until: (a) an immediate-animal-grant-with-accommodation helper exists, (b) a negative/recurring harvest-cost obligation mechanism (feeding-phase hook + obligation field) exists, and (c) a reward-suppression seam for atomic-space hooks exists. Ask the user how the harvest obligation should integrate with feeding (separate forced payment vs. added to the feed requirement) before implementing.

template: shifting_cultivation.py (on-play push of PendingPlow primitive) — but the geometry relaxation is unsupported
ordering_note: The load-bearing clause is 'which needs not be adjacent to another field' — a GEOMETRY RELAXATION of the standard plow. The existing PendingPlow primitive always enumerates targets via _legal_plow_cells (legality.py:352), which for any player who already has >=1 field requires the new field to be orthogonally adjacent to an existing field (lines 374-380). PendingPlow carries no field to relax this, and _enumerate_pending_plow (legality.py:1531) hardcodes _legal_plow_cells(p). Implementing this card correctly requires NEW SHARED INFRA: a new PendingPlow field (e.g. allow_non_adjacent: bool, default False) threaded through _enumerate_pending_plow to enumerate ALL empty non-enclosed cells (the field_cells-empty branch behavior) when set, plus a parallel C++ engine port to keep differential gates green. Implementing it as a plain shifting_cultivation copy would SILENTLY ENFORCE the wrong (adjacency) constraint — the card would be strictly weaker than printed and could even be unplayable in a layout where a non-adjacent plow is the only option. Note also the prereq 'Exactly 3 Field Tiles' (count grid cells of CellType.FIELD == 3) is a normal prereq predicate and is fine; the blocker is purely the plow geometry.
errata: none reported by card_text.py (no errata/clarifications section)
open_question: Add an allow_non_adjacent (or relaxed-geometry) flag to PendingPlow + _enumerate_pending_plow so granted plows can ignore the adjacency requirement? This same mechanism is likely reused by other 'plow a field anywhere / not adjacent' cards, so it is worth designing once as shared infra. Requires a C++ port for the differential gates. Confirm desired enumerator behavior when the flag is set: all empty non-enclosed cells (matching the first-field branch), regardless of existing fields.

template: moldboard_plow.py (closest analog: CardStore-counter pool + before_action_space Farmland plow grant); also mole_plow.py / assistant_tiller.py
ordering_note: BLOCKER: 'you can also plow UP TO 2 fields from this card' means the grant must fire TWICE within a SINGLE Farmland use. But the engine (engine.py:444-445, _apply_fire_trigger) UNCONDITIONALLY adds card_id to the host frame's triggers_resolved on every fire, and the granted PendingPlow pops back to that same Farmland host after CommitPlow — so any triggers_resolved-gated trigger (moldboard_plow / mole_plow / cooperative_plower pattern) can fire AT MOST ONCE per action-space use. A vanilla moldboard_plow copy would silently under-implement to 1 field/use instead of 2. PendingPlow is single-shot (no multi-count). There is no within-use multi-grant-per-use mechanism in the registries. The card ALSO needs a 4-tile lifetime pool (CardStore counter, like moldboard's 2-use counter) drawn down 1 per plowed field across the game — that half is fine, but it is orthogonal to and does not solve the twice-per-use problem.
errata: No errata/clarifications printed in card_text output. cost 3 Wood; prereq 3 Occupations (a HAVE-check predicate, like mole_plow's round prereq); vps 0; not passing; Consul Dirigens Expansion, deck C #19, category Farm Planner.
open_question: Need new shared infra to allow a single before_action_space grant to fire up to N (=2) times within one action-space use — e.g. a per-use grant counter the engine consults alongside/instead of triggers_resolved, OR a multi-shot 'plow up to N' PendingPlow variant. Defer until that mechanism is designed (it would also unblock any future 'plow up to k additional' card). Confirm with user how to model 'up to 2 per use' (re-offer the grant after each CommitPlow vs a counted multi-plow pending) before implementing.

template: agricola/cards/mini_pasture.py (on-play restricted/free granted build + playability prereq); the build-stable push itself mirrors agricola/cards/groom.py (PendingBuildStables(cost, max_builds=1)).
plan:
register_minor('stable', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1)), prereq=_can_build_free_stable, on_play=_on_play).
_on_play: return push(state, PendingBuildStables(player_idx=idx, initiated_by_id='card:stable', cost=Resources(), max_builds=1, build_stables_action=False)).  # stable is FREE; card cost (1 wood) is paid by the play-minor path, not the build.
_can_build_free_stable(state, idx): from agricola.legality import _can_build_stable; return _can_build_stable(state, state.players[idx], Resources())  # >=1 stable in supply AND a legal empty cell; free cost is trivially payable. Mirrors Mini Pasture's mandatory-playability gate so the grant never deadlocks.
No vps, no prereq-card-count, not passing. The PendingBuildStables enumerator handles cell selection + only allows Proceed once num_built>=1, so with max_builds=1 the build is effectively forced (matches 'Immediately build 1 stable').
ordering_note: The grant is effectively MANDATORY: PendingBuildStables's enumerator offers Proceed only once num_built>=1, so at num_built=0/max_builds=1 it returns ONLY CommitBuildStable cells (no decline path). That is fine ('Immediately build 1 stable'), BUT it means if no legal stable can be built when the card is played the action set is EMPTY -> deadlock. Therefore the playability prereq (a legal empty cell AND >=1 stable in supply, checked via _can_build_stable with FREE cost) is load-bearing and must exactly anticipate the grant, exactly as Mini Pasture's prereq does. Secondary: set build_stables_action=False (card effect, not the literal Build Stables action) so future action-scoped stable-build triggers (e.g. Stable Tree) don't fire on it -- mirrors Mini Pasture's build_fences_action=False; today this field has no trigger consumer (canonical skip-field only) but =False is the correct forward-compat choice.
errata: No errata/clarifications on this card. Text reminder: '(The stable costs you nothing, but you must pay the cost shown on this card.)' => the build is free; the 1-wood card cost rides the normal play-minor cost path, NOT the PendingBuildStables.cost (which is Resources()).
open_question: Confirm the build is intended as mandatory-on-play (the default PendingBuildStables behavior gives no decline path). The text 'Immediately build 1 stable' reads as mandatory and matches the user's Mini Pasture ruling ('Immediately fence' = mandatory), so implementing it mandatory is the proposed default -- but worth a one-line confirm since 'granted sub-actions are optional unless you must' is the general rule and 'Immediately' is the borderline word the Mini Pasture precedent resolved toward mandatory.

template: (none — no reveal-event template exists; closest in spirit is nest_site.py/pavior.py for the Family-Growth-without-worker idea, but they fire on start_of_round, not on a card reveal, and grant goods/effects, not a person-less Family Growth)
ordering_note: Even if both blockers were solved, the subtle point is the firing seam: the trigger fires on the REVEAL of western_quarry/eastern_quarry specifically (when their stage card is turned up), NOT at generic start_of_round and NOT 'each time you USE the quarry'. The two quarries reveal at fixed but distinct rounds; the card fires once per quarry-space reveal (so up to 2 firings/game), gated on 'room in your house' (people < rooms) at that instant. Wiring it to start_of_round would mis-fire on every round and on the wrong condition.
errata: None found in card_text output (no errata/clarifications block printed). cost: 4 Food; no prereq; 0 VP; not passing.
open_question: Two independent pieces of new shared infrastructure are required before this card is buildable, and both should be confirmed with the user: (1) NEW EVENT 'card/quarry reveal' — there is no reveal-triggered firing event. _apply_reveal_card (engine.py:954) only sets revealed=True and pops; it fires no card events (the comment in _complete_preparation literally says 'Future: card triggers fire here'). A new event ('quarry_revealed' or a general 'on_reveal' filtered to QUARRY_SPACES) plus its firing seam at the reveal step would need to be added. (2) PERSON-LESS FAMILY GROWTH — PendingFamilyGrowth always names a target space via initiated_by_id, and _execute_family_growth (resolution.py:965) calls _resolve_wish_for_children(state, space_id) to place the newborn ON that space. A 'Family Growth without placing a person' needs the place_on_space=False addition the cheat-sheet already flags as DEFER. Both are shared-infra changes, so per the cardinal rule this card defers and the design (new reveal event + person-less growth) should be settled with the user.

template: carpenters_axe.py (after_action_space over accumulation spaces, optional FireTrigger, register_action_space_hook for atomic hosting) + bread_paddle.py (pushes PendingBakeBread gated on _can_bake_bread)
plan: register_minor('steam_machine', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=2)), vps=1)  # 2 Wood; 1 VP; no prereq; not passing.

Define ACC_ATOMIC = frozenset({'fishing','forest','clay_pit','reed_bank','western_quarry','eastern_quarry'}) (the 6 ATOMIC accumulation spaces that need hosting); the 3 markets (sheep/pig/cattle) are non-atomic and self-host, so they need NO hook but DO surface after_action_space.

_eligible(state, idx, triggers_resolved): return (CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved) and (state.pending_stack[-1].space_id in ACCUMULATION_SPACES) and (state.players[idx].people_home == 0)  # people_home==0 => this was the player's LAST placement of the work phase (decremented at placement, before after-phase) and _can_bake_bread(state, state.players[idx]) so it never grants a dead-end.
_apply(state, idx): return push(state, PendingBakeBread(player_idx=idx, initiated_by_id='card:steam_machine'))  # the granted, optional Bake Bread sub-action.
register('after_action_space', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply)  # OPTIONAL declinable FireTrigger ('you can ... take a Bake Bread'); decline = don't fire.
register_action_space_hook(CARD_ID, ACC_ATOMIC)  # host ONLY the 6 atomic accumulation spaces; markets self-host.
ordering_note: Two interlocking subtleties. (1) 'last action space you use' = the placing player's people_home == 0 at the after_action_space moment (people_home is decremented in _place_worker_on_space BEFORE the after-phase fires, and engine.py line 783 uses people_home==0 as the canonical 'done placing' signal), so the check is exact. (2) Hosting scope: register_action_space_hook is required ONLY for the 6 ATOMIC accumulation spaces (in ATOMIC_HANDLERS: fishing/forest/clay_pit/reed_bank/western_quarry/eastern_quarry) to push a PendingActionSpace frame; the 3 animal markets are NON-atomic, self-host their before/after lifecycle (verified vs Claw Knife/Milk Jug), and must NOT be added to the hook (but ARE matched by the ACCUMULATION_SPACES membership test, so they still grant the Bake Bread). Gate on _can_bake_bread so the fire is never a dead-end.
errata: None. Card text verbatim: 'Each work phase, if the last action space you use is an accumulation space, you can immediately afterward take a Bake Bread action.' Cost 2 Wood, 1 VP, no prereq, not passing. Deck C (Consul Dirigens) #25, category Actions Booster.
open_question: Confirm the 'last action space you use' = people_home==0 mapping is acceptable: in the 2p game with fixed family size and no in-scope extra-worker grants, the player's final placement is exactly when people_home hits 0 after decrement, so the mapping is exact. (If a future extra-worker card lands, the eligibility would need to consult that; flagged but not blocking.) Also confirm ACCUMULATION_SPACES from constants.py is the intended 'accumulation space' set (the 5 building + fishing + 3 markets); meeting_place is excluded in the card game since it gives no food/accumulation there.

register_minor('teachers_desk', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1)), min_occupations=1)  # prereq '1 Occupation' = min_occupations, NOT a cost.
register('before_action_space', 'teachers_desk', _eligible, _apply)  # OPTIONAL trigger ('you can also'); decline = host's Proceed. Both Major Improvement (PendingSubActionSpace, space_id 'major_improvement') and House Redevelopment (PendingHouseRedevelopment, space_id 'house_redevelopment') fire before_action_space in their before-phase enumerators — single registration covers both. _eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved): return 'teachers_desk' not in triggers_resolved AND s.pending_stack[-1].space_id in {'major_improvement','house_redevelopment'} AND s.players[i].resources.food>=1 AND bool(playable_occupations(s,i))  # liquidation-aware affordability for the 1-food is fine since gate also matched by enumerator's commit; never a dead-end fire.

ordering_note: TIMING = before, not after. 'Each time you use the action space' with NO 'after'/'immediately after' wording => before_action_space per the cheat-sheet ruling (Forestry Studies rides after_action_space ONLY because its text says 'after you use'). template: beer_keg.py (closest sibling: optional once-per-harvest grain->VP via HarvestConversionSpec + CardStore bank + register_scoring); also baking_sheet.py for banked-VP pattern
ordering_note: Two compounding blockers, EITHER of which is a rules/infra call above a coding agent. (1) WRONG PHASE: card fires 'at the end of the FIELD phase of each harvest'. The only optional, agent-decided grain->VP surface in the engine is the FEED-phase HarvestConversionSpec hook (what Beer Keg uses). The field-phase hook (_fire_harvest_field_hook -> apply_auto_effects) is MANDATORY-AUTO ONLY: PendingHarvestField is a transient host built and torn down inside _fire_harvest_field_hook, has NO legality enumerator, and never reaches a returned state, so no FireTrigger/optional decision can be hosted there. Mapping the effect onto FEED is a behavioral approximation, not a faithful timing port -> rules judgment for the user. (2) CROSS-PLAYER FOOD GRANT: 'all other players get 1 food each' would have to be injected via side_effect_fn into the opponent's resources WHILE the opponent's own PendingHarvestFeed frame is live (both players' FEED frames coexist; resolution is SP-first). No existing card grants the opponent goods from inside a conversion fired during the active player's FEED frame; whether the opponent's frame re-derives food_owed correctly after the injection is unverified and order-dependent.
errata: No errata or clarifications returned by card_text.py. Cost 2 Wood; prereq 'No Grain in Your Supply' (a HAVE-NOT check at play time, i.e. resources.grain == 0); vps 0 printed (the 2 points are repeatable per-harvest banks, not a static term); not passing.
open_question: Is mapping 'end of field phase' onto the FEED-phase HarvestConversionSpec hook an acceptable behavioral equivalence (so Beer Table can be a food_out=0 Beer-Keg-style conversion that banks 2 VP and grants the opponent +1 food via side_effect_fn)? OR should the field phase gain a genuine optional-decision surface? AND is +1 food to the opponent mid-FEED (while their own feed frame is pending) sound given SP-first FEED resolution order? Defer-and-ask per the cardinal rule.

No prereq, not passing.
ordering_note: Score 0 unless house_material == HouseMaterial.STONE (a stone room is a ROOM cell in a STONE house). Count CellType.ROOM cells, NOT pastures/stables, and gate on STONE first so a clay/wood house yields 0. This mirrors scoring.py lines 210-216 exactly (num_rooms gated by house_material). Use register_scoring (additive to base scoring), not the printed vps field.
errata: No errata or clarifications returned by card_text.py. The trailing sentence 'You can only use one card to get bonus points for your stone house' is a mutual-exclusion clause shared only with Luxurious Hostel (revised minor, line 4304, 'more stone rooms than people -> 4 bonus points'). Luxurious Hostel is NOT implemented, so the clause is inert today; scoring this card alone is correct.
open_question: The 'only use one card to get bonus points for your stone house' dedup clause is unenforceable with the current register_scoring model (each scoring fn is independent and additive). It is a no-op now because the only co-card (Luxurious Hostel) is unimplemented. If/when Luxurious Hostel lands, the two will need a shared mutual-exclusion mechanism (e.g. a 'stone house bonus' group key resolved in scoring) so a player holding both only scores one. Defer that infra until the second card exists.

template: stable_architect.py (register_scoring scaffold) — but the required negative-point-sum helper does not exist
ordering_note: Writing Chamber's own bonus is non-negative (0..7), so it cannot feed itself — BUT a register_scoring fn receives only (state, idx) and cannot call score() to read the breakdown: score() folds SCORING_TERMS (scoring.py:238-239) and re-invokes this same fn -> infinite recursion. The negative total it must read is scattered across all 7 category tables (each -1 at zero: fields/grain/veg/pastures/sheep/boar/cattle), unused_spaces (<=0), begging (x-3), negative printed minor VPs (Brewery Pond -1, Mantlepiece -3), AND other cards' negative register_scoring terms. Summing those correctly is shared cross-card logic, not card-local — it requires a new scoring.py accessor (e.g. negative_points(state, idx)) that recomputes every negative score component WITHOUT folding non-Writing-Chamber scoring terms or this card, plus an explicit reentrancy/ordering contract so any future negatives-reading card never loops. Then bonus = min(-negative_total, 7), clamped to >=0.
errata: Clarification (from card_text): negative-point sources include printed card values, beggars, negative categories, negative bonuses. cost 2 Wood; no prereq; no printed VP; not passing. Corbarius Expansion, deck C #31, Points Provider.
open_question: Add a shared scoring.py helper negative_points(state, idx) that returns the sum of all negative score contributions (category-table -1s, unused, begging, negative printed minor VPs, and negative register_scoring terms) EXCLUDING Writing Chamber's own term and without re-folding scoring terms recursively. Confirm scope: do 'negative bonuses' include negative craft/major bonuses (none are negative today)? Confirm a sole human's begging at -3 each is the intended 'beggars' source. Confirm clamp: bonus = clamp(total_negative_points (as a positive magnitude), 0, 7).

template: agricola/cards/grange.py (minor with prereq= + vps=3, no on_play needed)
plan:
register_minor('abort_oriel', cost=Cost(Resources(clay=2)), prereq=_prereq, vps=3)  # no on_play, no scoring term (vps auto-scored).
_prereq(state, idx): for both players p in state.players, count len(p.occupations)+len(p.minor_improvements); return True iff EVERY player's count < 5 (i.e. no player already has >=5 cards in front). Built majors are NOT 'cards in front' so do not count major_improvement_owners.
No on_play effect, no triggers, no hooks. Cost 2 clay paid at play via the standard Cost path.
ordering_note: prereq is evaluated at legality/play-time over the CURRENT state, BEFORE this card is added to the tableau (legality.py:1308 -> prereq_met). So when the playing player has exactly 4 cards in front and no one is >=5, the prereq passes and this becomes their 5th card -- exactly satisfying the clarification 'may be played as one's fifth card'. Use strict '< 5' (block at >=5), never '<= 5'. Count is per-player over ALL players (the restriction triggers on ANY player, including the opponent, reaching 5+).
errata: Clarification only: 'This card may be played as one's fifth card.' (handled automatically because prereq reads pre-play state). No errata changing the effect.
open_question: What counts as a 'card in front of you'? Implemented as occupations + minor improvements only (standard Agricola: built major improvements are tiles, not cards). If the user wants majors counted too, add len of owned majors -- but per rules they should NOT be counted.

template: agricola/cards/stable_architect.py (register_scoring) + agricola/cards/grange.py (minor field-count idiom)
plan: register_minor('greening_plan', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(food=3))). No prereq, no on_play (no-op), no vps (bonus is variable, scored via register_scoring not the flat vps).  register_scoring('greening_plan', _score) where _score(state, idx) counts unplanted fields n = sum over grid cells with cell_type==CellType.FIELD and grain==0 and veg==0, then maps via a threshold ladder: return 5 if n>=6 else 3 if n>=5 else 2 if n>=4 else 1 if n>=2 else 0.
ordering_note: The threshold ladder is non-uniform (>=2/4/5/6 -> 1/2/3/5 pts; note the GAP at 3 fields gives the same 1pt as 2, and the JUMP from 3 to 5 pts between 5 and 6 fields) -- evaluate from the highest threshold down so each band returns the correct value; a naive linear formula is wrong. 'Unplanted' means a FIELD cell that is sown to nothing (grain==0 AND veg==0); a plowed-but-never-sown field and a field whose crop was fully harvested both count. Scoring runs after the final harvest (phase BEFORE_SCORING), so by the time _score is called all rounds-14 crops are already consumed -- no special timing handling needed; just read the terminal farmyard.
errata: Clarifications (from card_text): Garden Designer C099 plants FOOD on fields, so a food-planted field is NOT unplanted and must not count -- but Garden Designer is not implemented and food-on-field is not representable (Cell only has grain/veg), so this is a non-issue here. Boar held on unplanted field tiles via Mud Patch A011 do not affect this card's scoring -- also not implemented. Fields are checked after the final harvest is completed. None of these edge cards exist in the engine, so the plain grain==0 and veg==0 test is exact for the implementable scope.

template: agricola/cards/furniture_carpenter.py (near-exact: food_out=0 harvest-conversion + CardStore bonus-point counter + register_scoring; also beer_keg.py)
plan: register_minor('elephantgrass_plant', cost=Cost(Resources(clay=2, stone=1)), min_occupations=2)  # no on_play, no vps (the point is earned, not printed). register_harvest_conversion(HarvestConversionSpec(conversion_id='elephantgrass_plant', input_cost=Resources(reed=1), food_out=0, is_owned_fn=_eligible, side_effect_fn=_award)) where _eligible(s,i)= CARD_ID in s.players[i].minor_improvements (no extra ownership gate — unlike Furniture Carpenter there is no Joinery condition). _award banks +1 via p.card_state.set(CARD_ID, p.card_state.get(CARD_ID,0)+1). register_scoring(CARD_ID, lambda s,i: s.players[i].card_state.get(CARD_ID,0)).

This is the SAME accepted pattern Furniture Carpenter ('each harvest...') and Beer Keg use — there is no separate post-BREED seam. Safe because reed is never a feeding/cooking input, so surfacing the reed→VP swap mid-FEED rather than strictly after BREED has zero feeding interaction. Use food_out=0 (VP-only, NOT food). The point must be banked in CardStore and read back at scoring (no immediate-VP mechanism); do NOT set vps= (that scores the printed-keep VP, which is 0 here).
errata: none reported by card_text.py (no errata/clarifications shown).
open_question: Confirm the FEED-phase HARVEST_CONVERSIONS seam is the intended home for 'immediately after each harvest' (consistent with already-implemented Furniture Carpenter / Beer Keg); the engine has no separate after-harvest hook, and reed-vs-feeding non-interaction makes the sub-moment difference behaviorally inert.

template: agricola/cards/debt_security.py (minor + cost + register_scoring, no on_play); same shape as stable_architect's negative-equivalent scoring term
plan: register_minor(CARD_ID, cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1)), max_occupations=0, vps=7)  # printed 7 VP via MinorSpec.vps; prereq 'No Occupations' maps to max_occupations=0 (enforced by prereq_met, specs.py L180). register_scoring(CARD_ID, _score) where _score(state, idx) = -(len(ps.hand_occupations) + len(ps.hand_minors)) for ps = state.players[idx] — one negative point per card still in hand at scoring.
ordering_note: Score is NEGATIVE: return -(hand size), not +. Count the DECIDER's own hand only (hand_occupations + hand_minors on PlayerState), not the opponent's. The printed 7 VP must come through MinorSpec.vps (added by scoring.py's minor-vps loop), NOT inside _score — putting +7 in _score would double-count against the vps loop. The two text clauses ('cannot discard cards unplayed', 'cannot play if you already have') are rules/draft constraints with no engine surface (this engine never discards hand cards unplayed; cards are unique within a pool so no duplicate), so they are inert — implement nothing for them.
errata: none
open_question: None blocking. prereq 'No Occupations' = max_occupations=0 (verified enforced by prereq_met, specs.py L178-180). The two text clauses ('cannot discard cards unplayed' / 'cannot play if you already have [this card]') have no engine surface and need no implementation — confirm with user only if they want a defensive duplicate-play guard, but cards are unique within a pool so it cannot arise.

plan: register_minor('farm_store', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=2, clay=2)), vps=0).  Define 7 output variants: the 6 distinct building-resource pairs C(4,2) over {wood,clay,reed,stone} plus the single-veg option, e.g. _OUTPUTS = [Resources(wood=1,clay=1), Resources(wood=1,reed=1), Resources(wood=1,stone=1), Resources(clay=1,reed=1), Resources(clay=1,stone=1), Resources(reed=1,stone=1), Resources(veg=1)].  For each, register_harvest_conversion(HarvestConversionSpec(conversion_id=f'farm_store_{tag}', input_cost=Resources(food=1), food_out=0, is_owned_fn=_make_is_owned(), side_effect_fn=_make_grant(out))).  _make_is_owned: returns True iff 'farm_store' in p.minor_improvements AND no cid in p.harvest_conversions_used startswith 'farm_store' (cross-variant once-per-harvest guard, exactly beer_keg._make_is_owned).  _make_grant(out): p = fast_replace(p, resources=p.resources + out); _update_player. Omitting this lets the player exchange 1 food per variant (up to 7 food -> 14 goods) per harvest. side_effect_fn only adds goods (food_out=0, input_cost=Resources(food=1)), so no food double-count. Affordability/timing is correct-by-machinery: conversions are offered during HARVEST_FEED AFTER feeding cost is pre-debited and only when the 1 food is affordable, so 'after the feeding phase' maps exactly to spending surplus food (precedent: Furniture Carpenter's 'each harvest you can buy ... for food').

template: agricola/cards/clay_hut_builder.py (schedule_resources deferred-goods body) + agricola/cards/junk_room.py (register_auto on an improvement-build event). New module agricola/cards/farm_building.py.
plan:

register_auto("after_build_major", CARD_ID, lambda s, i: True, _apply).
def _apply(state, idx): R = state.round_number; return schedule_resources(state, idx, range(R + 1, R + 4), Resources(food=1)).

Collection at start of each scheduled round is the existing future_resources plumbing (engine._complete_preparation), proven by the Well.
ordering_note: Round indexing is the trap. schedule_resources takes 1-INDEXED rounds and does slot = rnd-1 internally, so 'next 3 round spaces' = range(R+1, R+4) (mirror Clay Hut Builder, NOT the Well's 0-indexed range(R, R+3) which writes future_resources[r] directly). schedule_resources already clamps rounds outside 1..14, so a late-game major (e.g. round 13 -> only round 14 gets food; round 14 -> nothing) is handled correctly = 'on each REMAINING round space'. Event must be after_build_major (majors only), NOT after_build_improvement (which also fires for minors).

Implementing this needs new shared infra: either a list of alternative base Costs on MinorSpec surfaced as a PendingChooseCost-style choice at play, or a register_base_route-style entry that adds (2 Clay + 1 Reed) as an alternative base alongside the printed 2 Wood. template: agricola/cards/chophouse.py (near-exact: before_action_space schedule onto next-N round spaces, collected at start_of_round). Same shape but single space + fixed N=4.
plan:
register_minor('stew', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(clay=1)))  # no vps, not passing, no prereq
SPACES = frozenset({'day_laborer'})
_eligible(state, idx): return state.pending_stack[-1].space_id in SPACES
_apply(state, idx): R = state.round_number; return schedule_resources(state, idx, range(R+1, R+1+4), Resources(food=1))
register_auto('before_action_space', 'stew', _eligible, _apply)  # 'each time you use' = before; pure-benefit -> auto (choiceless), matching Chophouse
register_action_space_hook('stew', SPACES)  # day_laborer is ATOMIC (in ATOMIC_HANDLERS) so must be explicitly hosted
ordering_note: Two subtleties. (1) 'Each time you use the Day Laborer action space' = before_action_space per the Trigger-Timing ruling (a bare 'each time you use [space]' fires BEFORE the space's own effect, like Chophouse/Corn Scoop). Because food is placed onto FUTURE round spaces (not collected this turn), before/after is end-state-identical, but use before_action_space to honor the ruling. (2) day_laborer is ATOMIC, so it does NOT self-host -- register_action_space_hook is REQUIRED or the before_action_space frame is never pushed and the effect silently never fires (Cottager already hosts day_laborer this exact way). schedule_resources writes 1-indexed rounds R+1..R+4 and clamps slots outside 1..14, so late-game uses silently drop out-of-range round spaces ('each REMAINING round space'). Collection at round start is automatic via future_resources in engine._complete_preparation -- no start_of_round trigger needed.

The genuinely hard part is the 'once per round, you can pay 1 vegetable to get 1 bonus point' standalone optional conversion: it has no action-space seam and is an at-any-time during the round choice.

The food->round-space half (schedule_resources over rounds N+1,N+2) is fully supported; the VP-conversion half is not. Should this be surfaced as an optional start_of_round choice (one offer per round) with the VP tracked in CardStore and added via register_scoring? template: agricola/cards/trellises.py (deferred-goods Category 8, schedule_resources); planted-field counter copied from agricola/cards/ash_trees.py _prereq_two_planted_fields
plan: register_minor("garden_claw", cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1)), on_play=_on_play). _on_play(state, idx): R = state.round_number; grid = state.players[idx].farmyard.grid; planted = sum(1 for r in range(3) for c in range(5) if grid[r][c].cell_type == CellType.FIELD and (grid[r][c].grain > 0 or grid[r][c].veg > 0)); n = 3 * planted; return schedule_resources(state, idx, range(R + 1, R + 1 + n), Resources(food=1)). No prereq, no vps, not passing. schedule_resources clamps slots to 1..14 so 'each REMAINING round space' is free; n==0 (no planted fields) schedules nothing.
ordering_note: The cap is min(remaining round spaces, 3*planted_fields). DON'T add a separate min against rounds-left: schedule_resources silently drops slots outside 1..14, so range(R+1, R+1+3*planted) yields the 'each remaining round space, up to 3x' clamp for free (Trellises relies on exactly this). Also: count is 3x PLANTED fields (FIELD cell with grain>0 OR veg>0) measured AT PLAY-TIME on_play, not total/unplanted fields and not fence pieces; placement starts at R+1 (next round, never the current round), matching the Well/Trellises slot convention (slot N-1 holds round N).

template: (closest, but insufficient) asparagus_gift.py (before/after CardStore snapshot) + big_country.py (used-farmyard-space definition: cell_type != EMPTY OR enclosed)

The check is a count-INCREASED comparison using big_country's used-cell definition (cell_type != EMPTY OR (r,c) in enclosed_cells), never a same-state equality.
errata: None. prereq: 1 Occupation (min_occupations=1, a have-check not a cost). No cost, no printed VPs, not passing. Category: Food Provider. Corbarius Expansion, deck C #48.
open_question: Farmstead fires 'after EACH turn' (any worker placement, regardless of which space), conditional on whether that turn newly-used a farmyard space. The engine has NO turn-boundary / after_turn / end-of-turn event (events are sub-action-scoped + before/after_action_space, the latter firing only on HOSTED spaces, once per action). To implement, we'd need either (a) a new shared 'after_turn'/end-of-turn firing event, or (b) hosting EVERY action space (incl. the atomic ones) plus a turn-spanning before/after used-space-count snapshot via CardStore. Both are new shared infrastructure, and the cheat-sheet flags end-of-turn timing + new-shared-event as DEFER. Recommend deferring and asking the user how they want a generic 'after your turn' hook modeled before implementing this class of card.

template: harvest_conversions.py (closest; insufficient — once-per-harvest only)
ordering_note: If ever built, the conversion must be offerable up to N times in one feeding phase where N = empty unfenced stables, with each firing consuming 1 grain for 5 food. The cap is re-evaluated against current grain after each firing (min(grain_left, stables_left)). 'Empty' = no animal on that unfenced stable; 'unfenced' = stable cell not inside any pasture. Both the per-firing repeat and the per-cell animal occupancy are the subtleties — neither is supported today.

open_question: Two missing pieces: (1) HarvestConversionSpec + harvest_conversions_used (frozenset[str]) model only a SINGLE once-per-harvest firing — there is no count-cap / multiplicity field, so 'for each empty unfenced stable, you can exchange...' (up to N firings) needs new repeatable-harvest-conversion infrastructure. (2) 'empty unfenced stable' is a derived GEOMETRIC quantity requiring per-stable animal occupancy, but the engine pools animals at the player level (p.animals) and does NOT place animals on specific unfenced stable cells in the grid — so 'empty stable' is not derivable from current state. Both are new shared infra; per cardinal rule, DEFER and ask the user how empty-unfenced-stable occupancy should be modeled and whether to add a count-capped harvest conversion.
