template: trellises.py / acorns_basket.py (on-play round-count grant) for clause 1 only; NO template exists for clause 2.
ordering_note: Clause 1 ('1 food for each completed round left to play') would denormalize as an immediate direct grant of (14 - round_number) food at on_play (e.g. play in round 5 -> 9 food: rounds 6..14 are the 'completed rounds left to play'). Confirm the exact round-count convention with the maintainer before implementing (is the current round counted? 'completed round left to play' = future full rounds = 14 - round_number). This is NOT a schedule_resources case (food is gotten immediately, not banked on round spaces). But this clause alone does not gate the card.
errata: None reported by card_text.py. Note 'wild boar' = boar in Animals; 'cattle' = cattle.
open_question: The blocking second clause 'At any time, you can exchange 1 sheep plus 1 wild boar for 1 cattle' is a standalone, free-standing at-any-time animal->animal conversion. Per the cheat-sheet DEFER rule and the engine's 'preserving optionality' foundation (standalone conversions are never surfaced as their own action; only cost-payment substitution exists via register_conversion in cost_mods.py, which is subtract-only / cost-context-only and cannot host a free 2-animals-for-1-animal swap). No machinery surfaces an at-any-time animal exchange action, and animal exchanges interact with accommodation/capacity (gaining a cattle while losing a sheep+boar changes pasture occupancy). This needs new shared infra (an at-any-time conversion frame surfaced into legal_actions, with capacity-aware accommodation). DEFER and ASK the user how at-any-time conversions should be modeled before implementing — even though clause 1 is trivially a Tier-1 grant, the card cannot be played correctly without clause 2.

template: milk_jug.py (closest opponent-action any_player hook; also brewing_water.py / herring_pot.py for the Fishing-space host frame)
ordering_note: The opponent-pays-owner direction is a routing trap: register_auto(any_player=True) calls apply with idx=OWNER, but the food is DEBITED from the ACTING opponent (other = 1-idx) and credited to the owner (idx) -- opposite of milk_jug, which only credits. But this debit is moot because two harder obstacles force a defer (see blocker_group/open_question).
errata: cost 1 Reed; 1 VP; not passing. Clarifications: (a) others must have >=1 food BEFORE using Fishing -- a precondition, i.e. an owner-card-dependent LEGALITY restriction on the OPPONENT's placement; (b) food taken from the Fishing action space may not be used to pay the owner (the pay is a separate before-the-action debit, so naturally satisfied since pay happens before the food is taken); (c) the 2 food is placed on Fishing at return-home ONLY for rounds in which another player actually used Fishing.
open_question: Two pieces of new shared infrastructure are missing. (1) No mechanism lets one player's owned card change the OPPONENT's legal placements -- making 'fishing' illegal for the opponent when they have <1 food requires a new placement-prereq registry keyed on an opponent-owned card, threaded into legal_placements. (2) No return-home event seam exists, and no primitive deposits goods directly onto an ACCUMULATION space (Fishing) -- schedules.py only schedules onto round spaces / start-of-round effect hooks, not return-home, and not onto an accum space. The conditional 'only rounds another player used Fishing' also needs per-round state (CardStore latch set on the before_action_space fire, consumed/cleared at return-home). Defer per cardinal rule: needs >=2 new shared mechanisms (opponent-placement legality + return-home accum-deposit) plus per-round conditional state.

template: catcher.py (before_action_space register_auto reading the host frame) + milk_jug.py (market host, no register_action_space_hook needed) + cookery_outfitter.py (Cooking-Improvement detection via major_improvement_owners[FIREPLACE_INDICES+COOKING_HEARTH_INDICES])
ordering_note: GROSS-vs-NET boar is the load-bearing ambiguity. When taking pig_market, the player chooses via CommitAccommodate how many boar to KEEP (commit.boar); over-capacity boar are immediately released to food at cooking rates and never land on the farm. 'new wild boar you GET from the effect of an action space' could mean the gross count taken from the space (PendingPigMarket.gained, readable at the before_action_space push) OR only the net boar that actually land (commit.boar, requires firing in/after _execute_accommodate). These differ exactly when the player is capacity-limited. Gross => a ~15-line register_auto('before_action_space', eligible= top.space_id=='pig_market', apply=+1 food * pending.gained). Net => fire after the accommodate commit (after_action_space) and read commit.boar — no existing card reads a market commit count, so net needs a small new read path. Do NOT lock the interpretation; it is a ruling.
errata: No errata or clarification text in revised_minor_improvements.json (deck C #52). Card data text only.
open_question: Official ruling: does '+1 food per new wild boar you get from the effect of an action space' grant food for (A) the gross boar taken from the Pig Market space (PendingPigMarket.gained), or (B) only the net boar that remain on the farm after accommodation (commit.boar, since over-capacity boar are auto-converted to food)? They differ when the Pig Market boar exceed capacity. Also: is the only intended source the Pig Market accumulation space (the sole implemented boar-granting space), and should it ever fire for boar gained via card effects? Once the user fixes the interpretation this is a small Tier-1 (gross/before_action_space) or Tier-2 (net/after accommodate) implementation; the prereq (owns Fireplace 0/1 or Cooking Hearth 2/3), cost 1 reed, and 1 VP are all straightforward.

template: none (closest shape would be harvest_conversions.py / a cook-to-food hook, but no such hook exists)
ordering_note: The bonus is per SIMULTANEOUS batch of 2 goods cooked, not per good and not per cooking action: 'turn 2 goods into food at the same time' yields exactly +1 (cooking 4 goods at once = +1, not +2 under the strict '2 at a time' reading; the official ruling gives +1 for each distinct pair cooked together). It must fire at EVERY cook-to-food moment (harvest CommitConvert, animal-acquisition overflow, breeding overflow, future card-cost food liquidation), and the +1 must be folded into the Pareto frontier ENUMERATION (so the agent sees the improved option), not just the apply side.
errata: No errata/clarifications returned by card_text.py. cost 2 clay, 1 VP, Food Provider category, Corbarius deck C #53.
open_question: Two structural blockers: (1) There is NO event/hook fired when goods are cooked into food anywhere in the engine. Verified: the only apply_auto_effects events are before_action_space / before_major_minor_improvement / after_build_improvement / harvest_field / start_of_round. The cook-to-food sites (_execute_convert at harvest feed, animal-overflow release, _execute_breed, the future PendingFoodPayment liquidation) apply cooking_rates directly with no firing seam, so this card has nothing to attach to. (2) Even with a new event, the '+1 per 2-good simultaneous batch' bonus changes the food-output arithmetic that drives the Pareto frontiers (harvest_feed_frontier / breeding / overflow), so it cannot be a pure after-the-fact auto effect — the enumeration itself must know about the bonus or the agent never sees the better conversion. This needs new shared infrastructure (a cook-to-food event carrying the simultaneous-good count + a cooking-rate/food-output modifier threaded into the frontier helpers) spanning multiple sites; defer and ask the user how they want a 'food-output modifier' modeled.

template: agricola/cards/beer_keg.py (the multi-variant once-per-harvest HarvestConversionSpec with cross-variant mutual-exclusion); simpler than Beer Keg because Studio has no banked points (no CardStore / side_effect_fn / scoring term).
plan: register_minor('studio', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(clay=1, reed=1)), vps=1)  # printed point handled by register_minor's vps; no prereq, no on_play. Then register THREE HarvestConversionSpec entries (studio_wood: input Resources(wood=1) food_out=2; studio_clay: input Resources(clay=1) food_out=2; studio_stone: input Resources(stone=1) food_out=3), each with side_effect_fn=None. Each variant's is_owned_fn(state, idx): return ('studio' in state.players[idx].minor_improvements) AND not any(cid.startswith('studio') for cid in state.players[idx].harvest_conversions_used)  -- the cross-variant guard enforces 'use the card once per harvest'. Wire into cards/__init__.py with 'from agricola.cards import studio  # noqa: F401'.
ordering_note: Cross-variant MUTUAL EXCLUSION is the load-bearing subtlety: the three options are ONE registry-of-three but 'exactly 1 wood/clay/stone' = the card may fire AT MOST ONCE per harvest. Each variant is a separate HARVEST_CONVERSIONS entry, and the enumerator gates only on is_owned_fn + affordability + 'conversion_id not in harvest_conversions_used'. The per-id used-set does NOT block the OTHER two ids, so is_owned_fn MUST read harvest_conversions_used directly and suppress ALL studio_* variants once any one has fired (mirror Beer Keg's _make_is_owned guard with cid.startswith('studio')). Without this guard a player could illegally fire wood+clay+stone in a single harvest. harvest_conversions_used is reset to empty at each harvest's FEED start, so each of the 6 harvests gets one fresh use.
errata: None. Card text verbatim: 'In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 wood/clay/stone into 2/2/3 food.' Cost 1 Clay + 1 Reed; vps 1; no prereq; not passing. (Note: distinct from 'Studio Boat' [studio_boat], deck C #39, a different card.)
open_question: None blocking. The wood/clay/stone are spent at face value (subtract-only) and produce food at 2/2/3 — matches the existing HarvestConversionSpec input_cost/food_out exactly. No conversion-closure or affordability-search machinery needed (these are raw resources the player already holds).

template: millwright.py (per-action-budgeted clay<-wood conversion on build_stable + after_build_stables reset) for the COST clause; wall_builder.py (after_build_stables auto) + a before_build_stables snapshot for the REWARD clause
ordering_note: The REWARD clause '...for your last one, get 3 food' is the blocker. 'Your last one' is genuinely ambiguous between (a) the FINAL/4th stable you ever build (supply -> 0; total over a game = 1+1+1+3 = 6 food) and (b) the last stable built in EACH build-stables action (a +3 every action's final stable). Card text + the single clarification (which only addresses the cost clause) do NOT disambiguate, and the two readings give materially different food. Cannot be guessed. Secondary subtlety even once resolved: the reward must be computed at after_build_stables from a DELTA (stables-built-this-action = post-count - pre-count, where count = 4 - stables_in_supply(farmyard)), so it needs a before_build_stables snapshot into CardStore, not just the after hook (the after hook alone can't tell how many stables this action built). The cost clause is itself clean: a build_stable conversion replacing exactly 1 wood with 1 clay, budget 1 per build-stables action (Millwright CardStore-counter + after_build_stables reset pattern), and the clarification confirms it only re-routes cost, never grants extra stables.
errata: clarifications: 'This card does not grant the ability to build additional stables, only to change any wood cost into 1 clay.' (governs the COST clause only; silent on the 'last one' reward semantics)
open_question: Does 'for your last one, get 3 food' mean (a) the final 4th stable you build all game (supply reaches 0), or (b) the last stable built in each separate build-stables action? Need user ruling before implementing the reward clause. (Cost clause is unambiguous and ready: build_stable conversion, 1 wood->1 clay, once per action.)

template: (none — no implement template; closest in-spirit is asparagus_gift.py for on-play optional buy, but both clauses are defer-class)
ordering_note: Even if the on-play buy were the only clause, it is a buy-food->good conversion (food -> 1 vegetable at 3 food), which the cheat-sheet and codebase have no machinery for; do not approximate it as an unconditional on-play +veg.
errata: None reported by card_text.py (no errata/clarifications surfaced).
open_question: Crudité has TWO defer-class halves: (1) on-play 'you can immediately buy exactly 1 vegetable for 3 food' = an optional buy-food->good conversion with no existing registry (register_conversion is build-cost substitution only, not standalone food spend). (2) 'At any time, you can discard 1 vegetable on top of another vegetable in a field to get 4 food' = an at-any-time standalone conversion (cards/__init__.py explicitly notes NO anchor for 'at any time' effects exists yet) that ALSO requires field-contents/geometry state (a sown vegetable field with >=2 veg) the engine does not model as a spendable resource. Defer until the 'at any time' post-action conversion window AND field-vegetable-as-payment-source machinery are designed. Ask the user.

template: agricola/cards/wood_cutter.py (action-space hook + register_auto) crossed with agricola/cards/basket.py (after_action_space + register_action_space_hook on the Forest wood space)
plan:
CARD_ID='woodcraft'; WOOD_SPACES=frozenset({'forest'}) (2p: Forest only).
_eligible(s,idx): return s.pending_stack[-1].space_id in WOOD_SPACES and s.players[idx].resources.wood <= 5  (threshold checked AFTER the space's wood income, which after_action_space guarantees).
_apply(s,idx): p=fast_replace(s.players[idx], resources=p.resources+Resources(food=1)); return fast_replace(s, players=tuple(p if i==idx else s.players[i] for i in range(2))).
register_minor(CARD_ID, cost=Cost(), min_occupations=1)  # prereq '1 Occupation' -> min_occupations=1; no spendable cost, no VPs, not passing.
register_auto('after_action_space', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply)  # mandatory/choiceless -> auto, not a declinable FireTrigger.
register_action_space_hook(CARD_ID, WOOD_SPACES)  # host the atomic Forest space.
ordering_note: MUST use the after_action_space event (NOT before): the '<=5 wood' threshold is read AFTER the Forest space's own +3 wood pickup lands; firing before would read pre-income wood and give food incorrectly. The clarification ('checked before cards that trigger after, e.g. Tree Guard C102') is about relative ordering AMONG after-triggers and does not change that this is an after_action_space effect. Use register_auto (mandatory, choiceless 'you get 1 food') and therefore NO triggers_resolved guard is needed (after-autos fire once per action-space flip naturally) — unlike Basket which guards because it is a declinable conversion.
errata: Clarification (from card JSON): 'This effect is checked before cards that trigger "after", e.g. Tree Guard C102.' I.e. Woodcraft's after-trigger resolves ahead of other after-triggers; informational only for the single-card implementation.

template: agricola/cards/beer_keg.py (harvest-conversion + register_scoring twin); also harvest_conversions.py for the spec dataclass
plan: register_minor(CARD_ID, cost=Cost(resources=Resources(stone=2, veg=1)), vps=2). One HarvestConversionSpec: register_harvest_conversion(HarvestConversionSpec(conversion_id='schnapps_distillery', input_cost=Resources(veg=1), food_out=5, is_owned_fn=lambda s,i: CARD_ID in s.players[i].minor_improvements, side_effect_fn=None)). No CardStore, no variants. register_scoring(CARD_ID, _score) where _score computes total_veg = p.resources.veg + sum(grid[r][c].veg for FIELD cells) then returns (1 if total_veg>=5 else 0) + (1 if total_veg>=6 else 0).
ordering_note: The 5th/6th-vegetable scoring bonus MUST count vegetables exactly as scoring.py line 181 does: total_veg = resources.veg + sum(field-cell .veg), NOT just resources.veg. Using only supply veg undercounts a player holding vegetables on unharvested fields. The single conversion entry + per-FEED reset of harvest_conversions_used automatically enforces 'exactly 1 vegetable per feeding phase' (once-per-harvest), so no extra guard is needed.
errata: None. Card text is unambiguous; cost 2 Stone + 1 Vegetable, 2 VP, not passing.

template: wood_harvester.py (harvest_field auto) is the closest in spirit, but it does NOT fit — Stone Clearing needs new per-field state the engine has no representation for.
ordering_note: If ever built: the field-phase stone take MUST run in the `harvest_field` hook (fires in `_resolve_harvest_field` BEFORE the hardcoded grain/veg mechanical take). But the deeper blocker is that the mechanical take loop only knows `cell.grain`/`cell.veg`; it cannot take stone, and a field holding stone must NOT be touched by that grain/veg loop. The 'considered planted until the stone is gone' status must persist across rounds and block re-sow / count as planted for prereqs.
errata: ERRATA: harvest the fields with stone normally, and the fields are considered planted until the stone is gone. (Card text: 'Immediately place 1 stone on each of your empty fields. Harvest them during the next field phase. These fields are considered planted until then.' cost 1 Food, 0 VP, not passing.)
open_question: Needs new shared infra: (1) a way to hold STONE on a FIELD cell — Cell has only {cell_type, grain, veg}, no stone field, and no per-card/per-field store maps to specific cells; (2) custom field-phase harvest that takes stone (not grain/veg) from exactly those fields while leaving the hardcoded grain/veg take loop untouched; (3) a persistent 'considered planted' status for stone-bearing fields that blocks re-sow (_can_sow / _execute_sow) and counts as planted for planted-field prereqs/scoring, surviving across multiple rounds until the field phase. This is the per-card-goods-stack / card-as-field DEFER category PLUS a new Cell field and engine harvest-loop change. Recommend implementing a generic 'good-on-field' (Cell.stone or a field-crop generalization) as shared infra first, then revisit — and ASK the user before adding it, since it touches the engine field/harvest model in both Python and the C++ port.

template: field_fences.py (granted-subaction-via-wrapper shape) + potter_ceramics.py (before_bake_bread trigger) — but neither suffices; no build-major-grant analog exists
ordering_note: If ever built: the trigger is 'each time BEFORE you get a Bake Bread action' => before_bake_bread, OPTIONAL/declinable (register, not register_auto), eligible only when Clay Oven (major idx 5) or Stone Oven (major idx 6) is build-affordable now AND not yet owned; it must push a choose-or-decline wrapper (not the build host directly) so declining is a clean Stop, mirroring field_fences' PendingGrantedBuildFences. Re-eligible per Bake Bread action via per-frame triggers_resolved scoping.
errata: cost 2 Clay; vps 5; prereq 'Return the Clay/Stone Oven' (returning an already-built Clay/Stone Oven major to supply is part of the play prerequisite/cost); category Food Provider; deck C #60 Corbarius. Clarification: 'Is an oven, is not an upgrade, and cannot bake.' On-play: immediately +5 food.
open_question: Two pieces of new shared infra are needed, both absent: (1) a granted-Build-Major sub-action frame (a PendingGrantedBuildMajor analog of PendingGrantedBuildFences, restricted to majors {5,6}, routed through effective_payments/can_pay), since NO card currently grants a build-major outside the Major Improvement action space (all major cards only modify cost via cost_mods). (2) the prereq 'Return the Clay/Stone Oven' — returning an already-BUILT major to the supply as a play prerequisite has no mechanism; ReturnImprovement exists only as a payment ROUTE for building a major (Cooking-Hearth-via-Fireplace return), not as a minor's prereq/cost. Both must be designed with the user before implementation.

template: agricola/cards/baking_sheet.py (A30) — IDENTICAL card text + clarification; Beer Stein differs only in cost (1 clay vs none) and prereq (none vs 'No Grain Field'). Same family: loppers.py (after-event optional trigger + CardStore-banked VP + register_scoring).
plan:
register_minor('beer_stein', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(clay=1)))  # no prereq, vps=0
register('after_bake_bread', 'beer_stein', _eligible, _apply)  # OPTIONAL trigger
register_scoring('beer_stein', _score)
_eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved): CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved and players[i].resources.grain >= 1
_apply(s,i): resources += Resources(grain=-1, food=2); card_state.set(CARD_ID, get(CARD_ID,0)+1); rebuild players tuple via fast_replace
_score(s,i): players[i].card_state.get('beer_stein', 0)  # 1 VP per fired exchange
ordering_note: Use after_bake_bread, NOT before_bake_bread. The clarification 'You must bake normally to make this exchange' is satisfied structurally because PendingBakeBread's before-phase offers only FireTrigger+CommitBake, so the after-phase is reachable only once a normal bake is committed. before_bake_bread (Potter Ceramics' event) would let the exchange fire without a committed bake and could deplete grain the bake needs — wrong. The VP must be CardStore-banked + register_scoring (use-count dependent), NOT a flat vps= on the spec (which would award the point without ever exchanging). Once-per-action is automatic: _apply_fire_trigger stamps triggers_resolved before applying; each new bake action gets a fresh PendingBakeBread with empty triggers_resolved.
errata: Clarification: 'You must bake normally to make this exchange.' No errata. cost 1 Clay; printed 0 VP (bonus point earned per exchange); deck C #61; Corbarius Expansion; Food Provider category.

template: harvest_conversions.py (closest existing home: HARVEST_CONVERSIONS registry) — but it does NOT fit; no template applies
ordering_note: The doubled-rate use must interleave with the existing flat-rate cooking liquidation inside the deferred-food-payment feed/breed Pareto frontiers (food_payment_frontier / harvest_feed_frontier). The error trap: double-counting — the same animal/veg could be liquidated both via the normal cooking_rates path AND via this doubled use, so the frontier must reserve and exclude the doubled good from the flat-rate pass. Also 'each of your cooking improvements once' means per-improvement state (a player with BOTH a Fireplace and a Cooking Hearth gets TWO doubled uses, not one), which the once-per-harvest single-id HARVEST_CONVERSIONS model cannot express.
errata: No errata on this card. Note: per Cookery Outfitter's errata, 'cooking improvements' = Fireplaces (major idx 0,1) + Cooking Hearths (idx 2,3) only; Clay/Stone Ovens do NOT count. Cost 2 Clay, 0 VP, not passing, Corbarius Expansion deck C #62.
open_question: Should the doubled use be modeled as a new per-improvement, once-per-harvest tier in the feed/breed Pareto frontiers (food_payment_frontier / harvest_feed_frontier rewritten to offer 'double the first 1 animal-or-veg per owned cooking improvement'), or as a distinct sub-action that reserves the good from the flat-rate liquidation? Either path is new shared harvest-frontier infrastructure — the engine's single most subtle decision point — and the HARVEST_CONVERSIONS registry (fixed input_cost/food_out, single conversion_id) structurally cannot model 'double an existing cooking conversion, once per improvement, capped at 1 good.' Needs user direction before implementing.

template: agricola/cards/harvest_conversions.py (closest shape: register_harvest_conversion / HarvestConversionSpec, surfaced as a CommitHarvestConversion in the FEED phase) -- but it does NOT fit, see blocker.
ordering_note: If ever built: the conversion fires in the FEED sub-phase (once per harvest, like joinery/pottery), so it must coexist with the existing harvest_feed_frontier and the harvest_conversions_used 'decided' set. The most error-prone subtlety is that the cost is HYBRID -- 1 grain from SUPPLY (a Resources cost the spec already models) PLUS 1 grain removed from a planted FIELD tile (a per-Cell Cell.grain, NOT a supply resource). Affordability and execution must check/consume BOTH, and when multiple fields hold grain the field choice is a player decision the choiceless side_effect_fn(state,idx)->state cannot express.
errata: None reported by card_text.py. cost: 2 Wood, 1 Clay; category Food Provider; Corbarius Expansion deck C #63; passing=no, printed vps=0 (the 2 points are earned dynamically each harvest, not a kept-card printed VP).
open_question: Two pieces of shared infra are missing. (1) The HarvestConversionSpec affordability gate is _can_afford(p, spec.input_cost) over SUPPLY Resources only and _execute_harvest_conversion does p.resources - input_cost + food; neither can require/remove a grain from a FIELD tile (Cell.grain), nor choose among multiple grain-bearing fields. (2) The '2 bonus points' are earned per-harvest by a player action (up to 6 times/game) with no end-of-game board signature, so they need a CardStore-backed accumulator surfaced via register_scoring -- the existing harvest-conversion side_effect_fn path has no such counter wired. Should we (a) extend HarvestConversionSpec with a field-grain cost + an earned-points accumulator, or (b) defer Craft Brewery until a batch of field-consuming / earned-VP harvest cards justifies that infra? Recommend defer.

template: agricola/cards/plow_driver.py (optional start_of_round trigger, once-per-round used_this_round latch, pay-a-cost-then-effect) + agricola/cards/schedules.py:schedule_resources (deferred goods onto future round spaces, as in trellises.py / Well)
plan: register_minor(CARD_ID, cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1, clay=2)), vps=1, on_play=lambda s,i: s)  # no on-play effect; the recurring effect is the start_of_round trigger. _eligible(state, idx, triggers_resolved): CARD_ID not in p.used_this_round AND p.resources.grain >= 1. _apply(state, idx): debit 1 grain, latch used_this_round |= {CARD_ID}, then schedule_resources(state, idx, range(R+1, R+5), Resources(food=1)) where R=state.round_number (schedules food on the next 4 round spaces; clamps past round 14 for free). register('start_of_round', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply); register_start_of_round_hook(CARD_ID)  # persistent every-round host, like Plow Driver (NOT Grassland Harrow's schedule-gated single host).
ordering_note: The scheduled food lands on rounds R+1..R+4 (the NEXT 4 round spaces, exclusive of the current round R), collected at each of those rounds' start via future_resources in _complete_preparation/_collect path. Use range(R+1, R+5), NOT range(R, R+4). The once-per-round latch is correct because engine._complete_preparation clears used_this_round (step 3) BEFORE _fire_preparation_hook surfaces this round's start_of_round triggers (step 5) — so the latch resets each round automatically. Cost is plain grain (a real Resources field), so debit p.resources - Resources(grain=1) directly; NO PendingFoodPayment liquidation path is needed (unlike Plow Driver's food cost). Eligibility must require grain>=1 so the trigger is never a dead-end.
errata: None in card_text.py output (no errata/clarifications printed). vps=1, cost 1 Wood + 2 Clay, not passing, no prerequisite, Corbarius Expansion deck C #64, category Food Provider.
open_question: Modeling choice: the printed 'Once per round, you can pay...' is physically usable at ANY point during the round, but the only clean engine seam for a recurring per-round paid grant is the start_of_round trigger (this is how Plow Driver / Handplow model 'at the start of each round, you can' and the analogous 'once per round' grants). Firing only at round start (before worker placements) is a faithful and standard approximation here — worth a one-line confirm with the user but does not block implementation.

template: agricola/cards/strawberry_patch.py (Category-8 deferred-goods minor; uses schedule_resources on play). Closest non-relative-round sibling logic; copy its structure, swap food->grain and the relative range for the fixed rounds [8,10,12], and drop the prereq.
plan:
from agricola.cards.schedules import schedule_resources; from agricola.cards.specs import register_minor; from agricola.resources import Cost, Resources.
def _on_play(state, idx): return schedule_resources(state, idx, (8, 10, 12), Resources(grain=1))
register_minor('granary', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=3, clay=3)), vps=1, on_play=_on_play)
No prereq, not passing. schedule_resources writes future_resources slots 7/9/11; engine._complete_preparation auto-collects grain into resources.grain at the start of rounds 8/10/12.
ordering_note: schedule_resources uses FIXED 1-indexed rounds (8,10,12), NOT a play-round-relative range like Strawberry Patch's range(R+1,R+4) — do not subtract/add round_number. The 'remaining spaces' clause needs NO special handling: if Granary is played at/after round 8 or 10, schedule_resources still writes that slot but it was already distributed-and-cleared by _complete_preparation, so a past-round slot is a harmless dead write that is never re-collected — exactly matching 'place on the remaining spaces.' Collected grain lands in the general grain supply (resources.grain), not on a field.
errata: None. Card text is complete; cost 3 Wood/3 Clay, 1 VP, no prerequisite, not passing.

template: (none) — closest in shape is harvest_field cards (butter_churn.py / loom.py / crack_weeder.py register_auto on 'harvest_field'), but their event seam fires at the WRONG time for this card.
ordering_note: The card's trigger is END-OF-HARVEST ('after each harvest in which you have N grain in your supply'), which must read grain AFTER the FIELD-phase crop take has added field grain to supply AND after FEED may have spent grain on feeding — i.e. after HARVEST_BREED completes. The only existing harvest hook is the 'harvest_field' event, which register_auto fires BEFORE the mechanical crop take (verified in _resolve_harvest_field / _fire_harvest_field_hook in engine.py: _fire_harvest_field_hook runs first, reading the still-sown grid). Using it would count grain at the wrong moment (pre-take, pre-feed) and give wrong results. The HARVEST_BREED -> PREPARATION/BEFORE_SCORING transition in _advance_until_decision (engine.py case 7) has NO hook seam, and grep over all card registrations shows NO after_harvest/end_of_harvest/harvest_breed event exists.
errata: ERRATA confirmed by card_text.py: last 'and' should be 'or'. Corrected text: 'After each harvest in which you have 2 or 3+ grain in your supply, you get 1 food or 1 additional grain, respectively.' i.e. exactly-2 grain -> gain 1 food; 3+ grain -> gain 1 grain (per the 2/3+ <-> food/grain 'respectively' mapping with errata's or).
open_question: Two infra questions for the user before implementing: (1) Add a new 'after_harvest'/'end_of_harvest' event fired once per player after HARVEST_BREED resolves (auto-only, choiceless reward), mirroring _fire_harvest_field_hook but at the post-BREED seam — does this belong as shared infra, or is there a preferred seam? (2) Threshold semantics under the errata: is the grain count read AFTER feeding has spent grain (so a player who fed grain may drop below 2/3), and is the band exactly-2 -> food / 3+ -> grain (i.e. exactly-2 gives ONLY food, not also the 3+ branch)? Confirm before coding.

template: nest_site.py (start_of_round mandatory choiceless goods grant via register_auto + register_start_of_round_hook); structurally identical shell
ordering_note: The blocking subtlety is the eligibility predicate 'at least 1 sheep IN A PASTURE'. The engine models animals only as AGGREGATE counts on PlayerState.animals plus a feasibility check (helpers.can_accommodate tries all type->pasture assignments and only verifies SOME valid placement exists); it never pins a specific sheep to a specific pasture/stable/house-pet slot. So 'sheep in a pasture' is a board fact the engine deliberately does not track. Loom (the only other sheep card) reads animals.sheep TOTAL, which is wrong here: a player with 1 sheep held as the house pet, on a standalone stable, or (future) on an animal-holding card has 0 sheep in a pasture yet animals.sheep>=1. A charitable feasibility reading 'animals.sheep>=1 AND len(farmyard.pastures)>=1' is determinable but can over-pay (sheep could legally be the house pet while a pasture sits empty). The structural shell is otherwise trivial: round_number is already incremented to the round being ENTERED before _fire_preparation_hook runs (engine.py _complete_preparation), and HARVEST_ROUNDS={4,7,9,11,13,14} exists, so 'round that does not end with a harvest' = state.round_number not in HARVEST_ROUNDS; round 1 is naturally excluded (setup skips preparation).
errata: none (card_text.py reports no errata/clarifications)
open_question: Does the engine's aggregate-animal model permit a settled interpretation of 'at least 1 sheep in a pasture'? Either (a) accept the feasibility heuristic eligible = animals.sheep>=1 and len(farmyard.pastures)>=1 (slight over-pay vs the physical-board ruling when a sheep is kept as house pet / standalone-stable animal while a pasture is empty), or (b) this needs real per-animal location tracking (new shared infra) and stays deferred. Needs a user ruling before implementing. The rest is a verbatim nest_site copy: register_minor(cost=Cost(resources=Resources(reed=1)), vps=1); register_auto('start_of_round', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply_grain_plus_1); register_start_of_round_hook(CARD_ID).

Naively swapping pool grain<->veg is wrong on both the location (field vs pool) and the rate (3:1).

open_question: Implementing this needs a card-borne field: a new sow destination outside farmyard.grid (with its own plow/sow legality + per-card crop storage, likely CardStore), and a harvest-field take that pulls vegetables from the card and applies the forced 4-food-per-veg conversion. This is new shared infrastructure across sow legality (legality.py), sow mechanics (resolution.py _execute_sow), and the harvest field take (engine.py _resolve_harvest_field) — not expressible with any existing register_* on the current grid-only model. template: closest existing module: agricola/cards/loom.py (harvest_field hook) for the field-phase half, and the major_minor_improvement host in resolution.py for the grant half; but neither provides a reusable primitive this card can compose, so no template actually fits.
ordering_note: Two sequential effects in one on_play: the field-phase crop-take MUST run BEFORE the granted Major/Minor Improvement action (a pushing on_play lands its frame on the stack, and _execute_play_minor's post-processing one-shots/after_play_minor pivot would interleave with any pushed frame). Also: the granted improvement is OPTIONAL ('you get a ... action', not 'you must') per the granted-sub-actions-are-optional ruling, so it needs a decline/exit path.
errata: Clarification: 'This is not a harvest and is for you only.' So it is a single-player crop-take only — NO feeding, NO breeding, NO once-per-harvest-conversion reset, and it affects only the playing player's fields, not the opponent's.
open_question: Two pieces of new shared infrastructure are required. (1) Field-phase-for-one-player: there is no reusable single-player crop-take primitive. _resolve_harvest_field (engine.py) is a phase resolver tightly coupled to the FULL harvest — it takes crops for BOTH players, resets harvest_conversions_used, pushes FEED pendings, and sets phase=HARVEST_FEED. Honoring 'for you only / not a harvest' means extracting just the crop-take loop (each planted field yields +1 grain or +1 veg into the owner's supply, decrement the field's count) into a new reusable helper callable from on_play. (2) Granted standalone Major/Minor Improvement: PendingMajorMinorImprovement exists but is designed to be HOSTED under a parent (the major_improvement space / house_redevelopment) that owns the Stop/Proceed exit; its before-phase enumerator (_enumerate_pending_major_minor_improvement) emits NO Stop, so pushing it standalone when the player can't afford any major and has no playable minor yields ZERO legal actions (deadlock). A self-contained granted-improvement wrapper frame with an explicit decline/Stop exit (mirroring PendingGrantedBuildFences) is new infra. Recommend deferring and asking the user how to scope both pieces.

template: seasonal_worker.py (mandatory-with-choice + PendingCardChoice; round-gated grain/veg options) is the structural twin; tumbrel.py / seed_pellets.py are the after_sow / before_sow auto-grant shape
ordering_note: BLOCKER: the mandatory-with-choice gate (legality.has_unfired_mandatory_trigger withholding the phase-exit) is wired ONLY for the PendingActionSpace before_action_space host, the start_of_round host (_enumerate_pending_preparation), and the BEFORE-phase of the Delegating sub-action host (_enumerate_pending_subactionspace, line 1775). NO after-phase sub-action enumerator gates on it: _enumerate_pending_sow's after-phase (legality.py L1465-1468) appends Stop() UNCONDITIONALLY, as does the bake/renovate/etc. after-phase. So a mandatory=True trigger registered on after_sow would surface as a FireTrigger but the player could just pick Stop and skip it -> the 'you get 1 grain' is not actually enforced. Enforcing it requires adding has_unfired_mandatory_trigger gating to the PendingSow after-phase Stop (structurally to every sub-action after-phase). Secondary subtlety even once gated: 'after you take an unconditional Sow' = the after_sow event (CommitSow before->after flip), and once-per-sow scoping must use pending.triggers_resolved (the eligibility_fn's 3rd arg) like seasonal_worker, NOT a CardStore counter.
errata: None. Verbatim (deck C #73, Corbarius, Crop Provider, cost 2 Food, vps 0, not passing): 'Each time after you take an unconditional "Sow" action, you get 1 grain from the general supply. From round 11 on, you can get 1 vegetable instead.' Threshold is round 11 (cf. Seasonal Worker's round 6).
open_question: The card is mandatory-with-choice (must take >=1 grain; from round 11 may swap to 1 veg) on the after_sow sub-action after-phase, but the has_unfired_mandatory_trigger gate is not implemented for any sub-action after-phase Stop. Two paths to confirm with the user: (A) add the mandatory gate to the sub-action after-phase enumerators (shared legality change; the clean correct fix, also unblocks any future after-phase mandatory-with-choice card), then implement exactly as seasonal_worker (register('after_sow', mandatory=True) -> _apply pushes PendingCardChoice with options ('grain',) pre-round-11 / ('grain','veg') from round 11; register_card_choice_resolver; once-per-sow via triggers_resolved); or (B) model it as an OPTIONAL declinable trigger whose apply pushes PendingCardChoice (no legality change) — but this misrepresents a mandatory grant as declinable, a correctness compromise to ask about rather than guess. Lean: path A is the right model; defer pending the user's call on changing shared after-phase legality.

This is NOT a cost-modifier (the wood is moved to supply, then spent through normal payment), so effective_payments/register_reduction/conversion do not model it; and 'up to 4' needs a numeric-choice frame to pick 0-4, which no generic card-move-to-supply commit frame provides.

Cost 2 Food; no prereq; vps 0; not passing.
open_question: Two pieces of new shared infra would be needed: (a) a RETURN_HOME firing event/hook (a per-round, end-of-round accumulator seam — also reusable by other 'in the returning home phase' cards); and (b) a 'move N (player-chosen 0..max) goods from a CardStore counter to supply before a build' mechanism (a choice frame + before_build_major host), distinct from the cost-modifier pipeline since it liquidates a card stockpile into supply rather than reducing a printed cost. template: agricola/cards/throwing_axe.py (near-exact: before_action_space auto on forest + register_action_space_hook; simpler — no Pig Market condition). Also milk_jug.py for the before_action_space pattern.

ordering_note: Fire on the BEFORE phase (before_action_space), per the 'each time you use [space]' ruling — same as Throwing Axe/Canoe/Milk Jug — so the +2 wood lands before the Forest's own accumulated wood take (order is immaterial here since it's a pure additive grant, but before is the canonical/correct phase). Use register_auto (mandatory, choiceless, no downside), NOT an optional FireTrigger. Do NOT pass any_player=True: 'each time YOU use' is owner-only, unlike Milk Jug's 'any player'. forest is atomic so register_action_space_hook is REQUIRED — without it no host frame exists and the auto never fires.

template: agricola/cards/market_stall.py (passing on-play minor shell: register_minor passing_left=True, cost=Cost(), no vps/prereq) + agricola/cards/calcium_fertilizers.py::_apply (field-grid edit body), with the threshold changed from grain/veg > 0 to == 1.
plan: register_minor('plant_fertilizer', cost=Cost(), passing_left=True, on_play=_on_play)  # cost/vps/prereq all null in JSON; passing_left='X' => passing card.

_on_play(state, idx): walk p.farmyard.grid; for each Cell with cell_type is CellType.FIELD, check 'exactly 1 good': if cell.grain == 1 and cell.veg == 0 -> fast_replace(cell, grain=2); elif cell.veg == 1 and cell.grain == 0 -> fast_replace(cell, veg=2); else leave.

Rebuild grid -> fast_replace(p.farmyard, grid=...) (pasture cache rides along: fields never lie in pastures) -> fast_replace(p, farmyard=...) -> splice player back into state.players tuple. Return state unchanged if nothing changed.

No action-space hook, no FireTrigger, no CardStore: it is a one-shot pure-goods grant applied automatically at play time (no downside). __init__.py already imports every card module; add the import line.
ordering_note: THE threshold trap: 'exactly 1 good' means cell.grain == 1 (xor) cell.veg == 1, NOT > 0. Calcium Fertilizers (the body template) uses > 0 and must NOT be copied verbatim. A freshly-sown field holds 3 grain or 2 veg, so it does NOT qualify; only fields harvested down to a single token do (each harvest_field decrements grain/veg by 1, engine.py:1254-1259). Result of fertilizing is 2 of that type. Also: a field carrying BOTH grain and veg is two types -> skipped (the grain==1 and veg==0 / veg==1 and grain==0 XOR guards handle this).

Treating it as automatic (apply to all eligible fields) per the cheat-sheet's 'pure-goods you can grant with no downside may stay automatic' ruling, matching Calcium Fertilizers. Adding free crops to your own fields is strictly beneficial, so no declinable FireTrigger frame is needed. Flag if the user wants strict optionality, but automatic is the correct convention here.

template: agricola/cards/plow_hero.py (pay-food optional FireTrigger + food-payment resume) crossed with agricola/cards/barrow_pusher.py (the after_plow per-PendingPlow-commit event). Reward body differs: add +1 stone, NOT push a PendingPlow.
plan: register_minor('rocky_terrain', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(food=1)))  # play cost 1 food; no prereq/vps/passing.

register('after_plow', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply); register_food_payment_resume(CARD_ID, _buy_stone)  # OPTIONAL declinable FireTrigger (each plow's after-phase enumerator surfaces it + Stop as decline).

_eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved): CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved AND _liquidatable_to(s,i,p,Resources(food=1))  # once-per-plow; liquidation-aware (food is at-any-time convertible, per FOOD_PAYMENT_DESIGN / PAY_FOOD_PLOW_CARDS doc: use _liquidatable_to, NOT food>=1). _buy_stone(s,i): p.resources + Resources(stone=1) - Resources(food=1) via fast_replace (reached directly OR as the food-payment resume which leaves raised food in supply to debit).

Add import to agricola/cards/__init__.py. after_plow fires once per PendingPlow commit for the plowing player (verified via _enter_after_phase player_idx + barrow_pusher), so a multi-field plow (Cultivation/Mole Plow) correctly offers the buy once per field.
ordering_note: Event is after_plow (NOT before_action_space): the plow is the TRIGGER, the buy is the reward, and it must fire per-FIELD-PLOWED (once per PendingPlow commit, like barrow_pusher) not per space-use — a Cultivation/Mole-Plow multi-field plow offers the buy once per field. Reward is GOODS (+1 stone -1 food), so unlike every existing food-payment-resume card (plow_hero/ox_goad/etc. push a PendingPlow) the resume body just adds stone — and there is therefore NO _can_plow eligibility gate (the reward never dead-states). The clarification 'playing field cards counts as plowing' is INERT in the current engine (no field-tile-as-card type implemented; the play_minor path creates no FIELD cell), so no play_minor hook is needed.

open_question: Liquidation depth of the reward's food cost: should 'buy 1 stone for 1 food' let the player liquidate OTHER goods (grain/veg/animals) into food to afford the 1 food (the rules-correct, doc-mandated _liquidatable_to reading, spec'd here), or be a plain food>=1 swap? Spec'd as liquidation-aware to match PAY_FOOD_PLOW_CARDS/FOOD_PAYMENT_DESIGN, but note: no existing food-payment-resume card has a goods reward (all push PendingPlow), so the resume->add-stone shape is new (low-risk: the resume mechanism is reward-agnostic). Confirm with user.

template: corf.py (any_player before_action_space amount-gated accumulation hook) + interim_storage.py (Resources-valued CardStore goods-on-card)
ordering_note: Two interacting subtleties. (1) SCOPE (the blocker): the printed text omits Corf's qualifier 'from an accumulation space' — it just says 'takes at least 5 wood, 4 clay, 3 reed, or 3 stone'. The amount-about-to-be-taken read is cleanly available ONLY at accumulation spaces (Corf's before-phase read of get_space(...).accumulated.<res>; _resolve_building_accumulation sweeps the FULL accumulated and resets, so the before-phase value == the take). If 'takes' is broader (card grants, conversions, future spaces) there is NO single event spanning all resource gains and no before/after resource-delta-across-any-action infra — that is a DEFER trigger (new shared infra). (2) DEPLETION ordering: the grant comes 'from this card' (2 of each placed on-play into a Resources-valued CardStore), so eligibility must gate on the card STILL holding >=1 of that resource (else no-op fire); decrement the CardStore and credit the owner in the same apply. Each accumulation space yields exactly ONE resource type, so at most one threshold applies per use — no multi-resource-in-one-take case.
errata: none (no errata or clarifications in revised_minor_improvements.json)
open_question: Does 'takes at least 5 wood / 4 clay / 3 reed / 3 stone' count ONLY accumulation-space takes (forest>=5, clay_pit>=4, reed_bank>=3, western/eastern_quarry>=3 — implementable NOW as Corf+Interim-Storage, Tier 2), or ANY acquisition of that quantity (card grants/conversions/etc. — needs a new generic resource-gain-delta event across all actions, true DEFER)? template: agricola/cards/ox_goad.py (optional paid after_action_space trigger w/ food-payment liquidation) + agricola/cards/basket.py / loam_pit.py (day_laborer atomic space-host hook, flat-goods grant)
plan: register_minor('hardware_store', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1, clay=1)), vps=1).  register('after_action_space','hardware_store',_eligible,_apply) where _eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved): 'hardware_store' not in triggers_resolved AND s.pending_stack[-1].space_id=='day_laborer' AND _liquidatable_to(s,i,p,Resources(food=2)) (never a dead-end; goods grant is always possible so only the 2-food payability gates).  _apply: if p.resources.food>=2 call _buy directly else push PendingFoodPayment(player_idx=i, food_needed=2, resume_kind='hardware_store', reserved=Cost()).  _buy(s,i): debit Resources(food=2), credit Resources(wood=1,clay=1,reed=1,stone=1); register_food_payment_resume('hardware_store',_buy).  register_action_space_hook('hardware_store', frozenset({'day_laborer'})) so atomic Day Laborer is hosted.
ordering_note: Event MUST be after_action_space ('each time AFTER you use Day Laborer'), NOT before_action_space (contrast loam_pit/seasonal_worker which fire before). Once-per-use via 'hardware_store' in triggers_resolved. The 2-food cost must route through the liquidation path (PendingFoodPayment + register_food_payment_resume, the raise-only frame leaves food in supply for _buy to debit) like ox_goad — do NOT just subtract food in _apply, or a player short on banked food but rich in crops/animals is wrongly denied. Goods grant is flat (no PendingPlow), so the grant itself never gates eligibility — only food payability does.

template: young_animal_market.py (closest on-play animal-grant shape) / acorns_basket.py (safe scheduled-animal alternative)
ordering_note: The blocker is animal ACCOMMODATION on an immediate gain. Early Cattle grants 2 cattle FREE on play (cost empty; prereq '1 Pasture'). Doing p.animals + Animals(cattle=2) in on_play bypasses the engine's accommodation machinery (which only runs at the 4 points: animal-market spaces, breeding, harvest feeding, and round-start collection of SCHEDULED animals via _collect_future_rewards). An immediate grant simply inflates the count past capacity = silently wrong. CARD_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md L547-556 makes immediate animal grants a defer-and-ask UNLESS the grant is guaranteed to fit. Unlike young_animal_market (net-zero: pay 1 sheep -> gain 1 cattle, no new overflow) and shepherds_crook (onto a guaranteed-fresh pasture), Early Cattle's '1 Pasture' prereq does NOT guarantee the 2 cattle fit (the pasture may be small or already occupied), so it is NOT guaranteed to fit. Defer until either a general on-play accommodation path exists, the maintainer confirms an acceptable trim/overflow policy, or it is reshaped (e.g. cattle scheduled onto the next round spaces, which would route through _collect_future_rewards). Also note the -3 VPs (negative printed score) handled automatically by register_minor(vps=-3) once the grant blocker is resolved.
errata: None found in card_text output (no errata/clarifications listed). Note: vps = -3 (negative), prereq = 1 Pasture, no resource/animal cost. Deck C #83, Corbarius Expansion, category Livestock Provider.
open_question: Confirm the desired accommodation behavior for a free +2-cattle on-play grant that may exceed capacity: (a) defer until a general on-play accommodation path lands, (b) reshape to schedule the 2 cattle onto the next round space(s) so _collect_future_rewards auto-accommodates (changes game timing slightly), or (c) accept a deterministic trim to the best accommodatable pareto_frontier point at play time. Implementation is otherwise trivial (register_minor with cost=Cost(), prereq=1-pasture check, vps=-3, on_play granting 2 cattle) once the accommodation policy is settled.

template: groom.py (start-of-round granted optional sub-action via FireTrigger) is the closest SHAPE, but the granted action itself has no existing machinery
ordering_note: Two compounding blockers, both lacking machinery. (1) SINGLE-TYPE BREED: the card breeds 'exactly 1 type' and explicitly states '(This is not considered a breeding phase.)'. The only breeding machinery is the full-harvest PendingHarvestBreed + breeding_frontier (agricola/helpers.py:247), which breeds ALL eligible types (every type with >=2 animals) in one shot and is capacity-Pareto'd over all three types together. There is NO single-type-breed pending, enumerator, or frontier — implementing this needs a new PendingPerennialBreed frame + CommitBreed-style enumerator restricted to one type + capacity gate, i.e. new shared infra. (2) TIMING: the printed minor text (revised_minor_improvements.json:859) is 'In each returning home phase after which there is no harvest, you can pay exactly 1 grain — even from a field — to breed exactly one type of animal.' This is RETURN_HOME-phase timing on NON-harvest rounds (rounds NOT in HARVEST_ROUNDS={4,7,9,11,13,14}), and there is no return_home hook event in the registries (events are before_/after_action_space, before_/after_<sub>, start_of_round, harvest_field). (3) 'even from a field' lets the 1 grain be paid directly off a planted field cell — a non-standard payment source with no existing helper.
errata: card_text.py surfaces no errata/clarifications. Note the canonical printed text differs from the scripts/card_text.py summary: the JSON catalog (revised_minor_improvements.json:859) reads 'In each returning home phase after which there is no harvest, you can pay exactly 1 grain — even from a field — to breed exactly one type of animal' (RETURN_HOME timing + pay-from-field clause), whereas card_text.py paraphrases it as 'Each round that does not end with a harvest...'. The RETURN_HOME timing and the 'even from a field' grain source are load-bearing and must be honored from the JSON, not the paraphrase. A sibling occupation (revised_occupations.json:3794) shares the identical single-type-breed mechanism.
open_question: Confirm the correct timing seam: printed text says RETURN_HOME phase of non-harvest rounds (no return_home event exists), not start_of_round. Decide whether single-type non-harvest breeding warrants a dedicated PendingPerennialBreed frame + enumerator (shared with the sibling occupation 'breed exactly one type' card) and how to surface the 'pay 1 grain even directly from a planted field' payment source.

template: none (no template fits; closest seam register_conditional is wrong shape — it is a mandatory auto-firing one-shot, not an optional pay-anytime activation)
ordering_note: Two independent blockers. (1) NEW MECHANISM 'a played card provides room': family-growth legality at legality.py:762 gates on `p.people_total < _num_rooms(p)` where `_num_rooms` counts only ROOM cells in farmyard.grid; end-game 'people'/room scoring (scoring.py:210-218) is likewise grid-derived. Making an occupation card count as +1 housing room would require threading a card-granted-room delta into BOTH the family-growth gate AND scoring — shared infra that does not exist. (2) OPTIONAL AT-ANY-TIME ACTIVATION with a non-trivial cost (1 grain + 2 food): the player CHOOSES whether/when to pay once a clay/stone house exists, and would often decline (it competes with feeding). There is no at-any-time / standalone activation seam; the only one-shot mechanism is `register_conditional`, which is a MANDATORY auto-firing latch (fires the first time the condition is true, no player choice), so it cannot model the optional pay-or-decline decision.
errata: No errata/clarifications printed in card_text output. Verbatim text: 'When you live in a clay or stone house, you can pay 1 grain and 2 food. If you do, for the rest of the game, this card provides room for exactly one person.' (Corbarius Expansion, deck C #85, Farm Planner, players 1+, no cost/prereq/vps/passing).
open_question: How should a card-granted housing room (one CardStore latch on den_builder) feed into (a) the family-growth capacity gate in legality.py and (b) end-game people/room scoring? And what is the activation seam for an OPTIONAL at-any-time pay-1-grain-2-food once a clay/stone house exists — a new optional-conditional event, or surface it as a FireTrigger at some host? Both need a design decision before any C-deck 'card-provides-room' cards are implementable.

template: agricola/cards/animal_tamer.py (closest by shape: house-pet flexible-slot capacity bump via register_house_capacity) — but it does NOT fit, see blocker.
ordering_note: Capacity is keyed on a CONSUMABLE, fluctuating resource (grain in supply), not a structural farm feature. This is the load-bearing distinction from every existing capacity card (Animal Tamer = rooms, Drinking Trough = pasture cells — all stable). Because extract_slots recomputes house_pet_capacity from live state every call, spending grain (sow / bake bread / eat at harvest feeding) would dynamically SHRINK animal capacity mid-game, retroactively un-accommodating already-placed animals — the engine has no eviction/over-capacity model for this. Two further mismatches: (a) the HOUSE_CAPACITY_MODS fold is `max`, not additive, so it cannot correctly express 'base house pet (1) PLUS one slot per grain' — encoding it as `1+grain` abuses max-semantics and breaks when combined with Animal Tamer; (b) grain-as-animal-holder is the per-container animal-tracking shape, not a flat flexible-slot count.
errata: No errata. Clarification in card text: animals held by grain 'count as accommodated on your farm.' On-play: immediately get 1 grain.
open_question: Should Livestock Feeder be modeled as (a) per-grain animal containers (the per-location/container animal tracking infra CARD_SYSTEM_DESIGN.md L546 defers it into, alongside Feedyard/Stockyard), or (b) a flexible-slot bump keyed on current grain count? Option (b) is structurally simpler but requires the engine to handle capacity SHRINKING when grain is spent (e.g. forbid spending grain below the count needed to hold current animals, or force animal discard) — an entirely new mechanic. Defer and confirm the intended ruling with the user: does the card lock the grain (effectively reserving it, so it can't be spent while holding animals) or is the held-animal count simply re-checked against live grain at each accommodation decision?

template: clay_hut_builder.py (conditional latch is the closest existing shape, but does NOT fit) / cottager.py (build-room granting)
ordering_note: The core blocker is the 'at any time, you can add that room' clause: it is a player-INITIATED, OPTIONAL, UNANCHORED action with no firing seam. Unlike every implemented card, it is not triggered by an action-space hook, a sub-action, play-card, renovate, start-of-round, or harvest event. The player may choose to add the on-card stone room on their own initiative at any point once the gate (stone house + >=4 rooms) is met. The engine has no machinery to surface a standalone optional legal action a player can take at will; the legality extension registries (BAKE_BREAD/OCCUPANCY/RENOVATE_TARGET/BAKING_SPEC) only hook specific seams, and register_conditional is MANDATORY one-shot (fires automatically the first instant the condition holds, not player-choosable 'at any time'). A naive conditional latch would force the room placement the moment the gate is met, violating the 'you can ... at any time' optionality and skipping the player's cell choice.
errata: No errata. Clarification (general Consul Dirigens rooms ruling, cf. Freemason's note): 'cards that provide room for a person do not count as rooms unless they self-identify as a room' — relevant to counting the >=4-rooms gate.
open_question: Three pieces of new shared infrastructure are needed before Mason is buildable: (1) a way to surface an UNANCHORED player-initiated optional action in legal_actions (an 'at any time' action registry distinct from the seam-based hooks); (2) GEOMETRY for the room placement — choosing a farmyard cell (a build-room into a chosen cell) — explicitly on the DEFER-for-geometry list; (3) a FREE/zero-resource-cost room build (build-room with no payment) wired so it still updates the farmyard grid, capacity, and scoring, plus a CardStore boolean tracking whether the on-card stone room is still unplaced (consumed once added). Should we batch this with other 'at any time' cards (the comment in cards/__init__.py notes another card is blocked on the same 'at any time' anchor) and design the standalone-optional-action machinery once?

template: bricklayer.py (build_room/build_stable register_reduction) + carpenter.py (house-material gating); NO existing template for the 13th-15th-fence ordinal-free clause
ordering_note: build_index for stables/fences is PER-ACTION (resets each Build action, starts at 0), NOT a lifetime ordinal. The card's '3rd and 4th stable' / '13th to 15th fence' are LIFETIME-total ordinals, so any reduction must add the already-built total to build_index: stable ordinal = (4 - stables_in_supply(farmyard)) + ctx.build_index + 1, free iff in {3,4}; fence ordinal would be fences_built(farmyard) + (edges paid so far this action) + 1. The stable build_index in _build_stable_ctx is the running num_built, so the +existing-total adjustment is the single most error-prone point — getting it wrong discounts the wrong stables.
errata: No errata/clarifications surfaced by scripts/card_text.py. Verbatim text: 'Wood rooms cost you 2 woods less. Your 3rd and 4th stable each cost you 1 wood less. Your 13th to 15th fence each cost you nothing.' Corbarius Expansion, deck C #88, category Farm Planner, players 1+, no cost/prereq/vps/passing.
open_question: The third clause ('your 13th to 15th fence each cost you nothing') has NO supporting mechanism. The three free-fence registries are: FREE_FENCE_SEEDS (scalar per-ACTION budget, reset each action), FREE_FENCE_EDGES (positional board-geometry edges), FREE_FENCE_POOLS (fence pieces physically moved onto the card). None expresses a LIFETIME-cumulative ordinal ('the Nth fence ever built, counting across all Build Fences actions, is free'). A register_reduction('build_fence', ...) operates on the whole-action RUNNING WOOD TOTAL (Resources(wood=accrued+this_pasture)) and cannot isolate which specific edges are the player's 13th/14th/15th fence overall, because the running total is a scalar wood bill, not per-edge. Implementing this faithfully needs a NEW free-fence source: an ordinal-cumulative-by-total-fence-count discount that, at each fence-cost settle, waives wood for however many of the new edges fall in lifetime positions 13-15 (i.e. free_edges = clamp(min(15, fences_built+new_edges) - max(12, fences_built), 0) wood waived). Clauses 1 (wood rooms -2 wood via register_reduction('build_room',...) gated on house_material==WOOD) and 2 (3rd&4th stable -1 wood via register_reduction('build_stable',...) using the lifetime-ordinal adjustment above) ARE both cleanly implementable with existing machinery (build_index is documented in CostCtx as 'Nth room/stable/fence (Carpenter's Apprentice)' — this card was explicitly anticipated). DEFERRING the whole card rather than shipping clauses 1-2 and silently dropping clause 3, since a partial card violates its printed text. Recommend asking the user whether to (a) add the new cumulative-fence-ordinal free-fence source, or (b) ship clauses 1-2 now and stub clause 3 with a TODO.

template: stable_planner.py / mining_hammer.py (on-play PendingBuildStables push) for the buildable half; capacity_mods.py for the capacity half — but no registry fits
ordering_note: The capacity sentence is the blocker. The accommodation model (helpers.extract_slots / can_accommodate) has exactly TWO slot kinds: pasture (capacity-N, single-TYPE) and flexible (capacity-1, ANY-type). An unfenced stable today is a capacity-1 flexible slot. Stable Master requires a THIRD slot kind for exactly ONE unfenced stable: capacity-3, single-TYPE, non-pasture. Neither registry expresses it: house_pet_capacity (capacity_mods.py) only raises the COUNT of capacity-1 flexible slots; pasture_capacity_bonus only adds to PASTURE capacities (an unfenced stable is not a pasture). A correct fix is new shared accommodation infrastructure: a fixed-capacity single-type slot threaded through extract_slots, can_accommodate, the PARETO_OPT_LEVEL>=1 _pareto_frontier_opt / _breeding_frontier_opt paths, AND the C++ accommodation port. Out of scope for a single-card pass.
errata: No errata/clarification block in the card data. Verbatim: 'When you play this card, you can immediately build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood. Exactly one of your unfenced stables can hold up to 3 animals of one type.' Cost: none (occupation, free via Lessons). No prereq/VPs/passing. Deck C #89, Corbarius Expansion, category Farm Planner.
open_question: Add a new 'fixed-capacity single-type non-pasture slot' kind to the accommodation subsystem? This same shape is needed by siblings (minor #i1669 'this card can hold up to 3 animals of the same type, not a pasture'; minor #i4049 size-1-pasture upgrades) and by minor #i4951 (per-unfenced-stable +1 capacity), so it is general infra worth designing once. Separately: is the on-play 'build 1 stable for 1 wood' grant OPTIONAL ('you can ... immediately' => yes, optional, gate on _can_build_stable affordable now) — confirm before any future build.

ordering_note: The 'If you can accommodate the animal' clause is a play-time AFFORDABILITY+CAPACITY GATE evaluated against the live (non-preparation) farm, and the buy is a player CHOICE among sheep/boar/cattle at 0/1/2 food. Unlike Acorns Basket (animals scheduled to future round-start, auto-accommodated decision-free during preparation), this needs an in-turn animal-acquisition decision frame at on_play with overflow/keep+food-conversion handling — i.e. the same machinery the animal-market action spaces use, but invoked from a card's on_play. That frame/path does not exist for cards.

Both are new shared infra (DEFER per cardinal rule).

template: agricola/cards/assistant_tiller.py (near-exact copy; same shape as cooperative_plower.py minus the extra occupancy condition)
plan: register_occupation('field_watchman', lambda s,i: s)  # no on-play effect, no cost/prereq/vps. SPACES=frozenset({'grain_seeds'}). _eligible(s,i,triggers_resolved) = CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved and s.pending_stack[-1].space_id in SPACES and _can_plow(s.players[i]). _apply(s,i) = push(s, PendingPlow(player_idx=i, initiated_by_id='card:field_watchman')). register('before_action_space','field_watchman',_eligible,_apply). register_action_space_hook('field_watchman', SPACES)  # grain_seeds is ATOMIC (in ATOMIC_HANDLERS), so the hook is REQUIRED to host it. Add import in cards/__init__.py.

The grant is OPTIONAL ('you can also plow') -> use register (declinable FireTrigger), NEVER register_auto. 'Each time you use [space]' = BEFORE phase (no 'immediately after'), and once-per-use is enforced by the host's triggers_resolved set (CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved). Gate on _can_plow so a dead-end plow is never offered.
errata: None. Verbatim text: 'Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you can also plow 1 field.' No cost/prereq/vps/passing.

template: childless.py / cob.py (start_of_round host pattern) is the nearest shape, but does NOT cover this card's two infra gaps
ordering_note: Timing is 'immediately before EACH harvest' = the WORK_RETURN_HOME -> HARVEST_FIELD phase boundary (engine.py:869 `state.round_number in HARVEST_ROUNDS`), which fires BEFORE the FIELD sub-phase. This is NOT the existing `harvest_field` event (that fires DURING the field phase, at crop-take in _resolve_harvest_field) and NOT `start_of_round` (post-harvest preparation). No 'before_harvest' event/hook exists in the trigger registry today.
errata: No errata/clarifications returned by card_text.py. Status 'todo'. Corbarius Expansion, deck C #92, category Actions Booster, players 1+.
open_question: TWO independent blockers, both needing shared infra: (1) No 'before harvest' event exists. harvest_field fires during the FIELD sub-phase (crop take in _resolve_harvest_field), not at the WORK->HARVEST_FIELD boundary; 'immediately before each harvest' would need a new event + host-frame push at engine.py:869 (the HARVEST_ROUNDS branch of _resolve_return_home / harvest entry). (2) Card-granted Family Growth has NO action space to place the newborn on: PendingFamilyGrowth (pending.py:730) places the newborn 'on the space named by initiated_by_id'; an off-space grant needs a `place_on_space=False` addition (cheat-sheet explicit DEFER). The 3-food cost on an optional 'you can' grant + the 'if you have room in your house' eligibility (people_total < rooms capacity) are individually expressible, but cannot be wired until both the new before-harvest event and the off-space family-growth path exist. Recommend grouping with other 'before/around harvest timing' + 'off-space family growth' cards and resolving the shared infra with the user before implementing.

template: agricola/cards/throwing_axe.py (closest action-space-hook shape: register_action_space_hook on an atomic accumulation space + before/after fire) — but the payoff effect has NO engine support
ordering_note: Effect text: 'Each time you use the Forest or Clay Pit accumulation space, you can place 1 stone from the general supply on the OTHER space. If you do, you can immediately place another person.' Per cheat-sheet, 'each time you use [space]' = before_action_space (fires BEFORE the accumulated goods are taken). The conditional latch is two-stage: (1) an OPTIONAL declinable choice to add 1 stone to the opposite accumulation space's goods (mutate get_space(board, other).goods/accumulated), then (2) ONLY IF the player took stone, grant an immediate extra worker placement. Clarification: usable TWICE on the same turn (once via Forest, once via Clay Pit), so the grant is not one-shot-latched per turn. The other space being already-occupied (workers!=(0,0)) does not block adding stone (stone goes onto its accumulation pile regardless of occupancy).
errata: Clarification (from card_text.py): 'You may use this ability twice on the same turn.' No errata otherwise.
open_question: The blocker is the second half: 'you can immediately place another person' grants a TEMPORARY ADDITIONAL worker placement within the same player's turn. No engine mechanism exists for this — turn alternation is fixed at one placement per turn via _advance_current_player, and the cheat-sheet explicitly lists 'temp extra worker' as a DEFER trigger. This is shared infrastructure needed by a whole family (Lazy Sowman A94, Stock Protector B94, Outskirts Director C130, Sheep Inspector D93, Canal Boatman D103, Spin Doctor D151). DEFER and ASK the user to design the extra-placement mechanism (likely: a flag/counter that grants the active player an additional PlaceWorker before _advance_current_player rotates, with its own accommodation/legality, fired from an after_action_space hook). The first half alone (add 1 stone to the other space) is trivial, but it is meaningless without the second half.

template: closest is consultant.py/priest.py (on-play occupation, state->state) but NONE model building a major; PendingBuildMajor (pending.py:205 / legality.py:1626) is the build path but cannot express a forced-single-major-at-custom-cost
ordering_note: The build is bundled into a forced occupation-play (Lessons), so unlike a normal Major Improvement worker action it has no natural affordability gate or decline path. Three undecided rules with no template: (1) is the build MANDATORY ('immediately build' reads like a command, so likely yes) or may the player decline it (cardinal-rule memory: granted sub-actions are optional unless 'you must')? (2) what happens if the player lacks 1 stone + 1 reed, or Basketmaker's Workshop (major idx 9) is ALREADY owned by either player (single shared slot) — silently no-op the build, or block the card from being played at all? The card text gives no fallback. (3) the cost is a per-build DISCOUNT (1 stone + 1 reed) vs the printed cost (2 reed + 2 stone, MAJOR_IMPROVEMENT_COSTS[9]) restricted to ONLY major 9 — PendingBuildMajor surfaces ALL unowned majors via effective_payments at printed cost and has no forced-major / custom-cost field, so it can't be reused; doing it raw in on_play would set owners[9]=idx + debit but BYPASS the after_build_major / after_build_improvement trigger events that _execute_build_major fires (no-ops today, but the correct machinery for future cards).
errata: none (status todo; deck C #95, Corbarius Expansion; players 1+; no errata/clarifications surfaced)
open_question: Need a user ruling + likely new shared infra: a way to build a SPECIFIC major at a CUSTOM (discounted) cost from an occupation on-play, with the mandatory-vs-optional, unaffordable, and already-owned semantics settled. Either a forced-major + custom-cost variant of PendingBuildMajor (preserving after_build_major/after_build_improvement firing) or a documented decision that a raw owners[9]=idx + Resources(stone=1,reed=1) debit in on_play is acceptable (skipping those events). template: stable_architect.py (closest scoring occupation) — but its pure register_scoring(fn(s,i)->int) shape CANNOT model this card
ordering_note: The food-per-field bonus is an all-or-nothing STEP threshold, not linear: 1 food in a field = 1pt, 4 food = 2pt, 7 food = 3pt (so 2-3 food still scores only 1, 5-6 still scores 2). Any future implementation must pick the strategically-optimal allocation of the player's food across empty fields, not a naive greedy fill — and food spent here is forgone elsewhere, so it is a genuine choice, not a deterministic max.
errata: ERRATA: This food is considered planted. (So the food sits in the field as a planted crop for scoring purposes.)

This is a multi-mechanism, novel-state-representation card — defer and ask the user whether to build a scoring-phase choice frame, or whether to treat the allocation as a deterministic optimum (it is not — food is forgone elsewhere).

template: agricola/cards/stable_architect.py (pure scoring occupation; register_occupation no-op + register_scoring) — but the _score body must recompute per-category point values, NOT call scoring.score() (re-entrancy), so it is closer in spirit to scoring.py's own category logic.
plan: register_occupation('lord_of_the_manor', lambda s,i: s)  # on-play no-op. register_scoring('lord_of_the_manor', _score). _score(state,idx): recompute the 8 capped-at-4 category point values from state WITHOUT calling scoring.score() (that would recurse through SCORING_TERMS): field_tiles count -> _score_field_tiles; len(pastures) -> _score_pastures; grain total (resources.grain + grain on FIELD cells) -> _score_grain; veg total -> _score_veg; sheep/boar/cattle -> _score_sheep/_score_boar/_score_cattle; fenced_stables (stables inside any pasture, capped 4) -> min(cnt,4). Return sum(1 for each of those 8 == 4). Mirror agricola/scoring.py exactly (reuse its _score_* private helpers via import; the raw counts are inline in score() so duplicate that counting). No cost/prereq/vps on the spec (occupation, played via Lessons).
ordering_note: RE-ENTRANCY: _score must NOT call scoring.score(state,idx) — score() iterates SCORING_TERMS and would re-invoke this very term, infinite recursion. Recompute the eight category point-values inline (or via scoring._score_field_tiles etc.) from raw state instead. The eight eligible categories are precisely those whose scoring table maxes at exactly 4 points: field_tiles, pastures, grain, vegetables, sheep, boar, cattle, fenced_stables. Rooms (clay x1 / stone x2), people (x3), majors, unused, begging are NOT counted — their values are not capped at 4 and the rule is 'maximum 4 points'. The card text confirms fenced stables counts ('also awarded for 4 fenced stables'). Watch grain/veg: the count is supply + crops sitting on FIELD cells, not just resources.
errata: No errata. Clarification (in-text): the bonus point is also awarded for 4 fenced stables — confirming fenced_stables is one of the eight max-4 categories.
open_question: Tier could arguably be 1 (single register_scoring + register_occupation no-op like Stable Architect), but bumped to 2 because the _score body must faithfully mirror scoring.py's eight per-category count+lookup computations (a sync-with-scoring obligation) rather than a one-line derived count. Confirm the eight-category set: standard Agricola Lord of the Manor counts the 8 farm categories that cap at 4 (fields, pastures, grain, veg, sheep, boar, cattle, fenced stables) — high confidence given the parenthetical about fenced stables.

template: agricola/cards/drinking_trough.py (closest existing capacity-mod minor) + agricola/cards/capacity_mods.py (the registry that would have to be extended)
ordering_note: The bonus is NOT a flat per-pasture additive constant. The size-1 capacity formula is 2*1*2^stables (=2 no-stable, =4 with one stable). Lawn Fertilizer raises the per-cell RATE from 2 to 3 ONLY for size-1 pastures -> 3 (no stable) and 6 (one stable). So the additive delta is +1 without a stable but +2 with a stable, and 0 for any pasture of size>=2. The existing pasture_capacity_bonus(player_state)->int fold (applied AFTER the stable doubling in extract_slots, Drinking Trough's +2-to-every-pasture) can express neither the size==1 gate nor the stable-scaled amount. Correct mechanism: a per-pasture bonus function f(pasture)->int = (3*2^p.num_stables - p.capacity) if len(p.cells)==1 else 0 (equivalently +1 if size==1 and no stable, +2 if size==1 and one stable). Must be applied per-pasture inside extract_slots, not as a flat scalar.
errata: None. Raw JSON (revised_minor_improvements.json, Dulcinaria D#11): cost null (free), vps null, prereq null, passing_left null, category Farm Planner. No errata/clarifications field. (A size-1 pasture can hold at most one stable, so the >1-stable case never arises.)
open_question: Needs new shared infra not in the registries: a per-PASTURE, size/stable-aware capacity registry (e.g. register_pasture_capacity_per(card_id, fn(pasture)->int)) + a new fold in capacity_mods.py + an edit to helpers.extract_slots to apply it pasture-by-pasture (the current API is a flat (player_state)->int added uniformly to every pasture). This also requires a matching C++ port in cpp/src/helpers.cpp (extract_slots) to keep the differential gates green. Well-scoped but it's cost-modifier-style infra surgery, so per the cardinal rule defer-and-ask. Confirm with the user whether to add the per-pasture capacity registry now (would also future-proof other size/stable-conditional capacity cards) or batch it. Note Lawn Fertilizer can co-stack with Drinking Trough: order is (base 2*cells*2^stables) -> Lawn Fertilizer rate bump for size-1 -> Drinking Trough flat +2; nail the application order when implemented.

plan: register_minor('wooden_whey_bucket', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1, food=1))).  SPACES={'sheep_market','cattle_market'}; _stable_cost(sid)=Resources(wood=1) if sid=='sheep_market' else Resources() (slash-correspondence: Sheep->1 wood, Cattle->free).  _eligible(state,idx,triggers_resolved): CARD_ID not in triggers_resolved AND state.pending_stack[-1].space_id in SPACES AND _can_build_stable(state, state.players[idx], _stable_cost(sid)).  _apply: sid=pending_stack[-1].space_id; push(state, PendingBuildStables(player_idx=idx, initiated_by_id='card:wooden_whey_bucket', cost=_stable_cost(sid), max_builds=1)).  register('before_action_space', CARD_ID, _eligible, _apply)  (OPTIONAL/declinable, not register_auto -- 'you can build').
