template: agricola/cards/cob.py (closest start_of_round shape) / agricola/cards/childless.py (register_start_of_round_hook + auto)
ordering_note: TIMING SEAM MISSING — the cardinal blocker. Card fires 'at the END of rounds 8, 10, 12', but the engine exposes only `start_of_round` (entering WORK of a new round) and `harvest_field`; there is NO end_of_round / after-work-phase event (grep across engine.py + triggers.py confirms). The natural fix is to map 'end of round R' to `start_of_round` of R+1 (rounds 9, 11, 13) — and that mapping happens to be value-equivalent here because no harvest occurs at end-of-8/10/12 (harvest rounds are {4,7,9,11,13,14}, and start-of-9/11/13 precedes those rounds' own harvests, so the veg-supply/veg-field snapshot is unchanged across the boundary). BUT this is a non-obvious timing remapping that must be confirmed by the user, not locked in unilaterally (DEFER list: 'end-of-turn/after-work-phase/after-harvest timing'). The grant amount is also DYNAMIC at fire time (1 stone per vegetable field = Cells with cell_type==FIELD and veg>0, via enclosed_cells/grid scan; plus wood = current p.resources.veg), so it CANNOT be pre-scheduled as a fixed amount via schedule_resources — it must be computed when the hook fires.
errata: none (no errata/clarifications printed)
open_question: Is mapping 'at the end of rounds 8/10/12' onto `start_of_round` of rounds 9/11/13 acceptable, or do you want a real end-of-round (post-work-phase / pre-next-round) event added to the engine? Confirm before implementing. (If start_of_round-of-R+1 mapping is approved, this becomes a Tier-2 register_auto('start_of_round') + register_start_of_round_hook card gated on round_number in {9,11,13}, applying +1 stone per veg-field and +p.resources.veg wood; prereq=lambda s,i: s.round_number<=8 for 'Play in Round 8 or Before'; cost Cost(Resources(wood=1, clay=2)); vps=2. The auto must be choiceless/mandatory since the grant is pure-positive goods with no decision.)

template: agricola/cards/shifting_cultivation.py (on-play minor that pushes a granted sub-action pending) + ash_trees.py / asparagus_gift.py for the planted/empty-field prereq
ordering_note: The granted sow is CONSTRAINED to exactly the 1 granted grain ('1 grain, which you must sow immediately'), but the existing PendingSow primitive cannot express an amount cap: _enumerate_pending_sow (legality.py:1477) enumerates `g in range(p.resources.grain+1)` and `v in range(p.resources.veg+1)`, so a player holding extra grain/veg could sow MORE than one field and could sow vegetables. Resources are a single fungible pool, so 'the granted grain' is indistinguishable from held grain without a new cap. PendingSow has only (player_idx, initiated_by_id, phase, triggers_resolved) — no max_grain/max_veg field. Constraining it correctly needs a NEW restriction field on PendingSow plus a matching change in _enumerate_pending_sow (mirroring how PendingBuildFences gained `restrictions` for granted fences) = new shared infrastructure, so DEFER per the cardinal rule. Secondary subtlety if it were built: sequencing — _execute_play_minor flips the PendingPlayMinor host to its after-phase BEFORE running on_play, so the pushed PendingSow lands on the already-flipped host (exactly like shifting_cultivation's PendingPlow); the +2 food and +1 grain must be granted in on_play BEFORE the push so the grain is on hand for the forced sow; and the '1 Empty field' prereq is what guarantees the mandatory sow has a target (PendingSow's before-phase offers no Stop, so an empty legal set would dead-end).

open_question: Confirm the intended rule: 'must sow immediately' should sow EXACTLY the 1 granted grain into 1 empty field (not allow the player to also sow held grain/veg). If so, this needs a capped/restricted PendingSow (new `restrictions`-style field + _enumerate_pending_sow change). Group with any other card granting a constrained sow (e.g. cards that grant grain/veg 'which you must sow immediately') so the PendingSow-cap infrastructure is designed once. Would the user accept modeling the granted grain as a +1 grain that just gets force-sown via a single-cell auto-place (no agent choice of which field) to avoid the cap, or is field-placement choice required?

template: junk_room.py (after_build_improvement auto) / wood_workshop.py (before_build_major+before_play_minor auto) — both are the structural shape, but neither can gate on the built improvement's clay cost

The 1 stone is a real grant (banks), order-independent. BUT the load-bearing subtlety is the cost-gate, not the timing: 'costing at least 2 clay' must be measured on the improvement JUST built, and must be the PRINTED clay cost (a major/minor whose printed cost includes >=2 clay) — independent of what the player actually paid after any cost-modifier reduction.

open_question: The auto-effect eligibility/apply fns receive only (state, idx) and cannot determine which improvement was just built or its clay cost: (1) majors — the after-phase PendingBuildMajor frame deliberately does NOT carry major_idx (ENGINE doc: 'Cost is NOT on this pending — keyed off commit.major_idx'), and the commit action is gone by fire-time, so MAJOR_IMPROVEMENT_COSTS[idx].clay is unreachable; (2) minors — no record of the just-played minor's id/cost is on the stack at the after_build_improvement fire. Resolving this needs NEW SHARED INFRA: either thread the built improvement's identity/cost into apply_auto_effects (an event-signature change) or stamp a 'last-built improvement' breadcrumb onto state for the eligibility fn to read. Per the cardinal DEFER rule (needs new shared infra: event/signature/field change), DEFER and ASK the user how to thread the just-built improvement's clay cost to the trigger. Secondary question to confirm with the user: does 'improvement costing at least 2 clay' include MINOR improvements with a >=2-clay printed cost, or only major improvements? (Affects whether the gate must cover the play_minor fire path too.)

template: bricklayer.py (register_reduction('renovate',...)) for the reed reduction + roughcaster.py (register_auto('after_renovate',...)) for the deferred +1 stone
plan: register_minor('roof_ladder', cost=Cost(resources=Resources(wood=1)), on_play=_noop_on_play)  # 0 VP, no on-play effect. def _less_1_reed(state, idx, ctx, cost): return cost - Resources(reed=1); register_reduction('renovate', CARD_ID, _less_1_reed)  # apply_reductions floors at 0. def _grant_stone(state, idx): p = fast_replace(state.players[idx], resources=state.players[idx].resources + Resources(stone=1)); return fast_replace(state, players=tuple(p if i==idx else state.players[i] for i in range(2))); register_auto('after_renovate', CARD_ID, lambda s, i: True, _grant_stone)  # choiceless pure-goods grant, fires once per renovate at the after-phase flip.
ordering_note: The +1 stone is a deferred ('at the end of the action') choiceless pure-goods grant, so it MUST be register_auto (mandatory, no FireTrigger), NOT the declinable register() that Mining Hammer uses — Mining Hammer is declinable only because it pushes a granted sub-action (a real choice); here there is no downside. Use after_renovate (post-application), per the renovate-hook convention, and eligibility is unconditional (lambda s,i: True) — the +1 stone applies to EVERY renovate (clay->clay, clay->stone, etc.), unlike Roughcaster which gates on house_material. Confirm the reed reduction does not need to be gated: clay-renovate costs reed (reduction bites), stone-renovate costs no reed (apply_reductions floors the signed -1 reed at 0, so it is a safe no-op there).

template: ox_goad.py (after_action_space on a single space, once-per-use via triggers_resolved) — firing shape only; the grant body is the blocker
ordering_note: The firing wrapper is fully supported and would mirror Ox Goad exactly: register('after_action_space', 'pigswill', eligible, apply) filtered by state.pending_stack[-1].space_id == 'fencing', once-per-use via `if CARD_ID in triggers_resolved: return False`. Fencing is an ATOMIC space, so it must ALSO be hosted via register_action_space_hook('pigswill', {'fencing'}) to make after_action_space fire there (ox_goad's Cattle Market is non-atomic so it needed no hook — do NOT omit the hook here). The 'each time you use' / 'you also get' wording = immediate, at the Fencing action (an after_action_space auto, not a next-round schedule). The single blocker is the BODY: `p.animals + Animals(boar=1)` is an immediate, NET-POSITIVE animal grant with NO accommodation path — at a Fencing-space hook there is no markets/breeding/harvest/scheduled-round-start route, so a player already at boar capacity silently inflates past capacity (CARD_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md §9 lines 547-556). Reinterpreting as a round-start schedule (the only safe animal-grant shape) would falsify the immediate timing, so it is not a clean substitute.
errata: None. Verbatim text: 'Each time you use the "Fencing" action space, you also get 1 wild boar.' Cost 2 Food OR 1 Grain (alternative 'A OR B' printed cost — a SECOND, independent blocker: MinorSpec.cost is a single Cost and cannot express the disjunction). No prereq, no printed VPs, not passing. Dulcinaria Expansion, deck D #83, category Livestock Provider.
open_question: Two blockers, both DEFER-and-ask: (1) immediate net-positive animal grant (1 boar) with no accommodation at a space hook — needs an immediate-animal-accommodation path (or a user ruling that boar capacity overflow is acceptable / how to handle it); (2) the alternative '2 Food OR 1 Grain' printed cost, unsupported by MinorSpec.cost (single Cost). Both must be resolved before implementing.

ordering_note: If ever built: the harvest half fires in the FEED sub-phase (rounds 4,7,9,11,13,14) and must be offered alongside the food crafts on PendingHarvestFeed BEFORE the final CommitConvert feed-payment, but its output is an ANIMAL not food, so it cannot ride the existing _execute_convert food-owed path; the per-firing 'animal of a type you already have' is a player CHOICE constrained to currently-owned animal types (re-evaluated each harvest), not a fixed output. Once-per-harvest gating would mirror harvest_conversions_used.

template: none (no housing-capacity modifier exists; closest registry-shape would be a new room-capacity hook analogous to register_scoring/cost_mods, which does not exist)
ordering_note: The effect is a PASSIVE, CONTINUOUS capability ('this card provides room for one person' once you own >=6 occupations), NOT a fire-once trigger. It must increase the player's effective house capacity, which gates family growth. The gate lives in legality.py:762 (_legal_basic_wish_for_children: p.people_total < 5 AND p.people_total < _num_rooms(p)), and _num_rooms(p) (legality.py:230) also feeds Farm Expansion's build-rooms room model and the Urgent/House-Redevelopment family-growth paths. There is NO registry to bump room capacity (cost_mods.py covers BUILD COSTS only; register_scoring is end-game only). Correctly modeling Reader means adding a new shared 'effective room capacity' modifier (gated on len(p.occupations) >= 6) and threading it through every family-growth/room-cap site consistently — exactly a new-shared-infra change. Subtlety: the +1 capacity is conditional on the running occupation count INCLUDING Reader itself (>=6 occupations; the 2p non-draft variant uses 6, draft 7 — we use 6), it is dynamic (turns on/off as occupations are played), and it must NOT raise the hard people cap of 5 (the 'room' is virtual but still bounded by the <5 rule).
errata: Card text: 'As soon as you have 6 occupations in front of you (including this one), this card provides room for one person. In the draft variant, you need 7 occupations to play this.' Dulcinaria Expansion, deck D #85, category Farm Planner, players 1+. No cost/prereq/vps/passing listed. The '7 occupations in draft variant' clause is a play-prerequisite for the draft variant only; the engine's card game is non-draft, so the threshold for the room-bonus is 6 occupations including Reader.
open_question: Confirm: implement a generic 'effective house capacity' modifier (a register_room_capacity-style registry summed into _num_rooms-consuming family-growth gates) so Reader and any future capacity cards share it, OR special-case Reader inline at the family-growth gates? Also confirm the threshold is 6 occupations (non-draft 2p) and that the bonus must respect the hard people-cap of 5.

template: agricola/cards/animal_tamer.py + agricola/cards/capacity_mods.py (closest in spirit: a standing capacity card via register_house_capacity / register_pasture_capacity), but NEITHER registry can express this card.
ordering_note: The standing capacity must be TYPE-RESTRICTED (sheep-only) and PER-OCCUPATION-CARD, and excludes occupation cards that already grant their own animal capacity ('unless it is already able to hold animals' — e.g. Animal Tamer, or a card that itself holds animals). The engine's accommodation model (helpers.extract_slots -> can_accommodate / pareto_frontier) has only TWO slot kinds: per-pasture TYPED slots and a flat num_flexible pool of capacity-1 ANY-type slots. A sheep-only slot would let SHEEP overflow there but not boar/cattle, which the flat num_flexible pool fundamentally cannot represent; both PASTURE_CAPACITY_MODS (typed but pasture-bound) and HOUSE_CAPACITY_MODS (any-type flat pool) are the wrong shape. The count of slots = number of owned occupations (including this one) that don't already hold animals — i.e. it scales with future occupation plays, so it's a live derived quantity, not a one-time grant.
errata: No errata/clarifications printed. Verbatim text: 'You can keep 1 sheep on each occupation card in front of you (including this one), unless it is already able to hold animals.' Dulcinaria Expansion, deck D #86, category Farm Planner, players 1+, no cost/prereq/vps/passing listed.
open_question: Implementing this needs a new accommodation slot-kind: a per-occupation-card, SHEEP-ONLY capacity-1 slot, counted as (# owned occupations that don't already hold animals). Add a sheep-only (typed) flexible-slot channel to extract_slots/can_accommodate/pareto_frontier and a registry mirroring capacity_mods. Which existing/future cards count as 'already able to hold animals' for the exclusion (Animal Tamer? other animal-holder occupations?) must be enumerated, and the C++ accommodation path must mirror the new slot-kind. DEFER and ASK the user before building shared accommodation infra.

template: none (no anytime-action machinery exists; closest in spirit is register_conditional in clay_hut_builder.py / manservant.py, but it is the WRONG mechanism here)
ordering_note: The card's defining property is PLAYER-CHOSEN TIMING ('at any time, but only once this game, you CAN add another room at no cost'). The one-shot conditional latch (register_conditional) is EDGE-triggered and AUTO-fires the first instant the standing condition becomes true (>=5 rooms) at a play-card/renovate seam. Using it would (a) strip optionality (it would build a room automatically, not 'you can') and (b) fix the timing to the moment the 5th room appears rather than whenever the player later wants the free room. Both are wrong. There is also no standalone top-level 'anytime action' event/frame in the engine to surface a deferrable optional free build, and the granted-sub-action machinery (mini_pasture / field_fences via PendingGranted*) only offers a grant DURING a specific host action space's resolution, not 'at any time'. A correct implementation needs new shared infrastructure: an anytime-action surfacing point plus a one-shot free-room-build primitive (no build-room card grants a free standalone room today; the room-touching cards Bricklayer/Carpenter/Frame Builder are cost-modifiers, not free grants).
errata: none reported by card_text.py (no errata/clarifications section printed)
open_question: How should an optional, player-chosen-timing, once-per-game free standalone room build be surfaced? Specifically: (1) is there/should there be an 'at any time' top-level action event the player can invoke whenever they choose (gated on >=5 rooms and the once-per-game latch), and (2) what primitive grants a single room build at zero cost (reuse PendingBuildRooms with a free/1-room-capped budget, analogous to mini_pasture's free-fence grant but for a room)? Both are absent today; the at-any-time window is explicitly deferred per CARD_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md and CARD_SYSTEM_DESIGN.md.

Use cost=Resources() (free of wood) and gate eligibility on a free stable being buildable so the trigger never offers a dead-end.
errata: None. card_text.py reports no errata/clarifications for Stablehand. (Sibling note: unlike Stable Planner's scheduled off-turn stables, this is an on-turn grant during the player's own Build Fences action, so no off-turn Stable Tree / Farmyard Manure caveat applies.)
open_question: Confirm 'each time you build at least 1 fence' means once per Build Fences ACTION (the modeled per-action after-phase fire), not once per individual fence piece -- standard ruling and matches Mining Hammer/Loppers per-action semantics, so implemented as once-per-action. (No-op on_play question RESOLVED: register_occupation requires a Callable, stable_architect uses 'lambda state, idx: state'.)

template: agricola/cards/young_animal_market.py (near-identical: on-play animal exchange, passing). Also market_stall.py for the passing-goods exchange shape.
plan: register_minor('game_trade', cost=Cost(animals=Animals(sheep=2)), passing_left=True, on_play=_on_play). _on_play: p = state.players[idx]; p = fast_replace(p, animals=p.animals + Animals(boar=1, cattle=1)); return fast_replace(state, players=tuple(p if i==idx else state.players[i] for i in range(2))). No prereq, no vps. Cost (2 sheep) debited by _execute_play_minor via Cost.animals; gain is the immediate boar+cattle.
ordering_note: This is the accepted on-play-animal-gain idiom (see young_animal_market), NOT a 'defer immediate animal grant' case: the engine does NOT force accommodation on a gain, so no can_accommodate/pareto_frontier handling is needed and none should be added. Two easy misses: (1) it is a TRAVELING card -- the JSON has passing_left='X' -- so passing_left=True is REQUIRED (the card_text.py CLI's 'players -' line is player-count, not passing; confirm via the JSON's passing_left field). (2) the cost is paid as Cost(animals=...), debited by _execute_play_minor, not inside _on_play -- only the +1 boar +1 cattle gain belongs in _on_play.
errata: None. JSON: cost '2 Sheep', vps null, prerequisites null, passing_left 'X' (traveling), category Livestock Provider, deck D #9. Parenthetical in text ('effectively exchanging 2 sheep for 1 wild boar and 1 cattle') is flavor restating the cost+gain, no extra mechanic.

template: agricola/cards/handplow.py (schedule + start-of-round optional plow grant) FUSED WITH agricola/cards/plow_driver.py (pay-1-food-to-plow via the shared food-payment path)
plan: On play: schedule_effect(state, idx, (R+4, R+7, R+10), 'plowman') where R=state.round_number (schedule_effect clamps rounds>14 for free). register_occupation('plowman', _on_play); register('start_of_round', 'plowman', _eligible, _apply); register_food_payment_resume('plowman', _pay_and_plow). _eligible(state,idx,tr): the card id sits in THIS round's future_rewards slot (Handplow's _scheduled_slot helper) AND _liquidatable_to(state,idx,p,Resources(food=1)) AND _can_plow(p). _apply(state,idx): FIRST remove 'plowman' from this round's future_rewards slot (Handplow consume), THEN Plow-Driver body: if p.resources.food>=1 -> _pay_and_plow (debit 1 food, push PendingPlow init 'card:plowman'); else push PendingFoodPayment(player_idx=idx, food_needed=1, resume_kind='plowman', reserved=Cost()). _pay_and_plow debits the 1 food then pushes the plow (it is also the registered resume). NOT register_start_of_round_hook (schedule drives hosting only on the 3 due rounds, like Handplow). No cost/prereq/vps/passing.
ordering_note: The once-per-scheduled-round guarantee must come from CONSUMING the schedule slot (Handplow's pattern), NOT from used_this_round (Plow Driver's latch, which it needs only because it fires every round). Consume the slot in _apply (the GUARD), BEFORE the food-payment branch -- so when food is short and a PendingFoodPayment is pushed, the start-of-round FireTrigger no longer re-qualifies while that frame resolves. Putting the slot-consume in _pay_and_plow (the body/resume) would re-offer the trigger during the food-raise. (Plowman therefore consumes its slot in _apply, unlike Plow Driver which latches in its body, because Plowman's gate is the schedule slot, not used_this_round.)
errata: none (no errata/clarifications surfaced by card_text.py)
open_question: The physical 'place a field tile on each corresponding round space' is modeled as cosmetic only (a round-space reminder), exactly as Handplow ignores its identical 'place 1 field tile on the corresponding round space' clause -- no field-tile supply is tracked in state. Worth a one-line confirm with the user that this is the intended modeling, but Handplow sets the precedent so this is not a blocker.

template: (closest existing shape: assistant_tiller.py for an OPTIONAL after-space granted-action trigger over a hosted space set; childless.py for the start_of_round/round-gated optional pattern. But no template matches the no-space Family-Growth grant.)
ordering_note: Crux: 'take a Family Growth action WITH THAT PERSON' grows a new family member off the worker that was just used — the newborn is NOT placed on any action space. The engine's only family-growth primitive (_resolve_wish_for_children, reached via PendingFamilyGrowth.initiated_by_id) UNCONDITIONALLY places the newborn worker onto a board space_id and increments that space's worker tuple. There is no place_on_space=False / no-space-newborn path — grepped, it does not exist. Reusing the primitive as-is would corrupt the board (placing a phantom worker on whatever space just resolved). Secondary subtleties if ever built: (a) 'at the end of each person action' = after_action_space over EVERY space incl. all atomic ones, requiring register_action_space_hook across the full atomic-space set + correct 'after the action fully resolves' timing; (b) round≥5 gate AND 'room in your house' (room capacity vs people_total) eligibility; (c) the -2 VP per use must be banked (CardStore counter) and read at register_scoring — score is -2 * times_used. The optionality is fine (register, not register_auto) since worded 'you can'.
errata: none returned by card_text.py; status todo, category Actions Booster, Dulcinaria Expansion, players 1+, vps 0, no cost/prereq/passing.
open_question: Confirm the intended placement: in the physical game the newborn from this 'Family Growth with that person' is placed on the action space the parent worker occupies (so it acts like an extra worker that round) — NOT a free between-rounds growth. That requires a new engine affordance to grow a family member on an arbitrary already-occupied space (or with no placement). Needs the user's ruling + a shared place_on_space/place-on-current-space addition before implementation.

template: (none — no mid-phase worker-return precedent exists; closest event shape is mushroom_collector.py / ox_goad.py register("after_action_space", ...) optional FireTrigger, but the EFFECT is unsupported)
ordering_note: If ever built: the trigger fires 'after you complete a person action' = after_action_space on EVERY hosted space (would need register_action_space_hook over the full atomic+non-atomic space set, plus per-space optional FireTrigger). The returned worker is 'another person you placed' (NOT the one whose action just completed), and returning it must (a) increment people_home so the work-phase loop in engine.py grants an extra placement, (b) clear that worker from its board ActionSpaceState (workers tuple) so the space frees up. 'Once per work phase' is a per-phase latch (CardStore scalar reset at start_of_round / phase entry — NOT once-per-action). Cost 1 sheep + 2 food is a multi-good charge with a sheep component (animals), but the blocker is the effect, not the cost.
errata: none (no errata/clarifications returned by card_text.py)
open_question: Returning a placed worker mid-WORK-phase to re-place it is a 'temporary extra worker' mechanic — explicitly on the DEFER list. Engine has no machinery to return ONE worker mid-phase: returns happen only in _resolve_return_home (RETURN_HOME phase, all workers at once, engine.py:828). No card mutates people_home (catcher/plow_hero only READ it as a placement counter). Building this needs: a new pending/action to SELECT which placed worker to return, logic to increment people_home + clear the chosen board space's workers tuple, an after_action_space hook over ALL spaces, and a per-work-phase latch. This is shared infra spanning legality + engine + a new selection frame. Defer and ask the user.

template: (none — no existing card returns a placed worker home mid-round; closest firing-shape precedent is roughcaster.py / wall_builder.py on after_build_rooms, but the EFFECT is unsupported)
ordering_note: The effect needs to identify WHICH action space holds 'the first person you placed' this round and physically return that worker home mid-round (decrement that space's workers count + increment people_home + re-enable re-placement this round), AND gate the trigger on the Build Rooms action being placed with the player's 2nd worker. ActionSpaceState.workers is only a (int,int) COUNT with no placement-order / which-space provenance, and no engine or card path returns a worker home mid-round (bulk return only at _resolve_return_home end-of-round, workers=(0,0)). The Meeting-Place exception ('unless it is on the Meeting Place') further requires knowing the first worker's exact space.
errata: none (no errata/clarifications in card_text output)
open_question: Two new shared mechanisms are required before this is implementable: (1) per-round placement-order state recording which space each of a player's workers was placed on (to find 'the first person you placed' and check the Meeting-Place exception, and to know the build-rooms action is the '2nd person'); (2) a return-a-placed-worker-home-mid-round primitive (remove worker from an arbitrary space, +1 people_home, allow re-placement this round). Both are new shared infra in the DEFER family ('temp extra worker / re-place', 'needs new shared field/state'). Confirm with user whether to build placement-order provenance + a mid-round worker-return helper for this and the broader 'worker-return' Dulcinaria/D-deck cluster.

template: wood_expert.py (the cost-conversion half) + shifting_cultivation.py (the on_play-pushes-a-primitive precedent for the grant half)
ordering_note: The grant is MANDATORY-if-affordable ('immediately build a major improvement', not 'you may'), and PendingBuildMajor's before-phase enumerator (_enumerate_pending_build_major) emits NO Stop in its before-phase — only one CommitBuildMajor per (major, payment), Stop appears only after the commit flips phase to 'after'. So pushing PendingBuildMajor from on_play when NO major is affordable produces an empty legal-action set (a dead state) — exactly the failure Shifting Cultivation avoids with its _can_plow prereq. Site Manager therefore needs a playability prereq = _can_afford_any_major_improvement (line 686, which routes through can_pay/effective_payments). Note the prereq must be evaluated with Site Manager ALREADY COUNTED AS OWNED, because its food-substitution conversion changes which majors are affordable; but _execute_play_occupation moves the card into p.occupations BEFORE running on_play, while the PLAYABILITY check (_legal_occupations) runs at enumeration time when the card is still in hand — so the affordability seen at play-time vs at grant-time can disagree if not handled carefully.
errata: No errata or clarifications printed. Verbatim text: 'When you play this card, immediately build a major improvement. When paying its cost, you can replace up to 1 building resource of each type with 1 food each.' Occupation, Dulcinaria Expansion, deck D #95, players 1+, category Actions Booster. No cost/prereq/vps/passing printed (occupation play cost is the route-supplied Lessons/Scholar food cost).
open_question: Two genuinely-novel pieces of shared infra are needed, both DEFER triggers per the cardinal rule: (1) NO occupation has ever GRANTED a build-major action — only Shifting Cultivation's on_play-pushes-PendingPlow precedent exists, for a leaf primitive with no internal payment machinery; pushing a bare PendingBuildMajor (heavier: effective_payments + oven wrapper + after-phase) onto the after-flipped PendingPlayOccupation host is plausible but unproven. (2) OccupationSpec has NO prereq field ('Occupations have no prerequisites' — legality.py:1227) and _legal_occupations returns the whole hand unfiltered (line 1233-1234); the mandatory-build affordability gate requires adding a prereq parameter to register_occupation/OccupationSpec AND a filter in _legal_occupations — a shared-infra change. Ask the user: (a) add an occupation-prereq mechanism + gate the grant on _can_afford_any_major_improvement, or (b) model the grant as an OPTIONAL/declinable build-major wrapper (no such wrapper exists for build-major; only PendingGrantedBuildFences does the optional-grant pattern today) so a no-affordable-major case degrades gracefully instead of dead-ending — which also raises whether the rules intend the build to be skippable when you can't/won't afford one. The cost-conversion half ('replace up to 1 building resource of each type with 1 food each') is fully supported: register_conversion('build_major','site_manager',expand) modeled on wood_expert (one substitution per building-resource type: each of wood/clay/reed/stone independently swappable for 1 food, floor at the cost's amount), with the food-payment shortfall path already wired into build_major. Implementation is blocked only on the grant half.

template: field_fences.py (closest: PendingGrantedBuildFences choose-or-decline grant) + cost_mods register_reduction; but neither covers a granted build/play-improvement action
ordering_note: Furnisher has TWO parts: (1) on-play +2 wood is trivial (one-line register_occupation, copy consultant.py). (2) The hard part: 'After EACH new room you build, you can build or play 1 improvement for 1 wood less' grants a whole improvement-build/play action as an OPTIONAL follow-up, with a 1-wood reduction scoped ONLY to that grant (clarification: the improvement may cost no wood => floor at 0). Three blockers, each needing new shared infra: (a) NO granted-improvement pending exists — only PendingGrantedBuildFences; there is no PendingGrantedBuildMajor/PendingGrantedPlayMinor 'build or play 1 improvement' choose-or-decline frame, and 'build OR play' spans both the major-improvement and minor-improvement entry points. (b) Firing granularity mismatch: the text is PER-ROOM ('after each new room'), but after_build_rooms fires ONCE per build-rooms session at the Proceed work-complete flip (see rustic.py/wall_builder.py comments) — there is no per-room hook, so a 2-room build should grant TWO separate improvement opportunities. (c) The 1-wood reduction must be a GRANT-SCOPED reduction routed into effective_payments that applies only to the granted improvement (analogous to field_fences' grant-scoped free-fence edges keyed on initiated_by_id), not a global register_reduction. Per the cardinal rule, DEFER and ASK rather than build all three pieces speculatively.
errata: clarification: 'The improvement does not need to cost any wood.' (i.e. the 1-wood reduction floors at 0 wood; reduction does NOT require the improvement to have a wood component). Consul Dirigens Expansion, players 1+.
open_question: Should we build a generic 'grant build/play 1 improvement' follow-up frame (PendingGrantedImprovement spanning build_major + play_minor) with a per-room firing hook and a grant-scoped cost reduction into effective_payments? This is shared infra reusable by other 'build/play an improvement' grant cards. Confirm the per-room (not per-session) firing semantics and the OR-decline-at-parent optionality before implementing.

template: consultant.py (on-play goods) for the begging-marker part only; no template exists for the recurring start-of-harvest free-occupation-play part
ordering_note: The recurring effect fires 'at the start of EACH harvest' (rounds 4,7,9,11,13,14), NOT at start_of_round and NOT at harvest_field. The harvest entry point is _resolve_harvest_field (engine.py ~797) which does mechanical FIELD work then pushes FEED frames; there is no host frame or firing seam BEFORE the field work for cards to hook. A new 'start_of_harvest' event + a host/firing point at the WORK->HARVEST_FIELD boundary (engine.py ~869, before _resolve_harvest_field) would have to be added, and it must fire once per harvest for both players in a defined order.
errata: Errata/clarifications: none returned by card_text.py beyond the printed text. Printed: 'When you play this card, you must immediately take 1 begging marker. At the start of each harvest, you can play 1 occupation without paying an occupation cost.' Consul Dirigens Expansion, deck D #97, category Actions Booster, players 1+.
open_question: Two pieces of NEW shared infrastructure are needed and should be designed with the user before implementing: (1) a 'start_of_harvest' firing event/host at the WORK->HARVEST_FIELD boundary (no such seam exists; start_of_round and harvest_field are both wrong timings), and (2) a way to play 1 occupation mid-harvest WITHOUT paying the occupation cost. Today PendingPlayOccupation is pushed only from Lessons (resolution.py:727) during WORK, and the play cost is route-dependent (Lessons charges occupation_cost; Scholar charges 1 food) — a zero-cost free-occupation-play route does not exist, nor does pushing PendingPlayOccupation as an optional/declinable choice during the harvest phase. The begging-marker on-play half is trivial (p.begging_markers - 1, mirroring scoring.py: begging_markers is <=0, penalized -3 each), but it is inseparable from the unimplementable recurring effect, so the whole card defers. Recommend deferring until the start-of-harvest hook + free-occupation-play machinery is designed.
